<article class="level4 chunking-large type-Chapter chunknode" id="12.14" data-cite="Chapter 12.14 BMC" data-short-cite="Ch. 12.14"><header class=" mini-header" data-type="Chapter" data-search="/BMC/12.14/search" data-share="/BMC/12.14/share" data-levelnodes-target="header"><h4 class=" inner-header"><span class="num">Chapter 12.14</span> <span class="name">AREA, DIMENSIONS AND DESIGN</span></h4></header><div class="track-indicator selected d-none">
<span class="glyphicons glyphicons-check" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="text">This chapter is included in your selections.</span>
</div>




<p class="tocHeading heading">Sections:</p><ul class="toc from-level4 to-level6">

<li class="tocItem level6"><a href="12.14.010"><span class="num">12.14.010</span> <span class="name">Purpose.</span></a></li>
<li class="tocItem level6"><a href="12.14.020"><span class="num">12.14.020</span> <span class="name">Interpretation of tables.</span></a></li>
<li class="tocItem level6"><a href="12.14.030"><span class="num">12.14.030</span> <span class="name">Residential area and dimensions.</span></a></li>
<li class="tocItem level6"><a href="12.14.040"><span class="num">12.14.040</span> <span class="name">Commercial and industrial dimensions.</span></a></li>
<li class="tocItem level6"><a href="12.14.050"><span class="num">12.14.050</span> <span class="name">Setbacks&#xA0;&#x2013; General measurement method.</span></a></li>
<li class="tocItem level6"><a href="12.14.060"><span class="num">12.14.060</span> <span class="name">Setbacks&#xA0;&#x2013; Adjoining alleys.</span></a></li>
<li class="tocItem level6"><a href="12.14.070"><span class="num">12.14.070</span> <span class="name">Setbacks&#xA0;&#x2013; Applying to specific building or use.</span></a></li>
<li class="tocItem level6"><a href="12.14.080"><span class="num">12.14.080</span> <span class="name">Setbacks&#xA0;&#x2013; Modifications.</span></a></li>
<li class="tocItem level6"><a href="12.14.085"><span class="num">12.14.085</span> <span class="name">Setbacks&#xA0;&#x2013; Offspring and unit lot setbacks applicable within attached residential zones.</span></a></li>
<li class="tocItem level6"><a href="12.14.090"><span class="num">12.14.090</span> <span class="name">Setbacks&#xA0;&#x2013; Projections and facilities allowed.</span></a></li>
<li class="tocItem level6"><a href="12.14.100"><span class="num">12.14.100</span> <span class="name">Setbacks&#xA0;&#x2013; Panhandle lots.</span></a></li>
<li class="tocItem level6"><a href="12.14.110"><span class="num">12.14.110</span> <span class="name">Building height&#xA0;&#x2013; General measurement method.</span></a></li>
<li class="tocItem level6"><a href="12.14.120"><span class="num">12.14.120</span> <span class="name">Building height&#xA0;&#x2013; Exceptions to limit.</span></a></li>
<li class="tocItem level6"><a href="12.14.130"><span class="num">12.14.130</span> <span class="name">Building limitations&#xA0;&#x2013; Accessory buildings and structures.</span></a></li>
<li class="tocItem level6"><a href="12.14.133"><span class="num">12.14.133</span> <span class="name">Floor area ratios.</span></a></li>
<li class="tocItem level6"><a href="12.14.134"><span class="num">12.14.134</span> <span class="name">Middle housing.</span></a></li>
<li class="tocItem level6"><a href="12.14.135"><span class="num">12.14.135</span> <span class="name">Accessory dwelling units.</span></a></li>
<li class="tocItem level6"><a href="12.14.140"><span class="num">12.14.140</span> <span class="name">Building and hard surface coverage.</span></a></li>
<li class="tocItem level6"><a href="12.14.150"><span class="num">12.14.150</span> <span class="name">Fences and freestanding walls.</span></a></li>
<li class="tocItem level6"><a href="12.14.152"><span class="num">12.14.152</span> <span class="name">Retaining walls, rockeries, and other retaining structures.</span></a></li>
<li class="tocItem level6"><a href="12.14.155"><span class="num">12.14.155</span> <span class="name">Storage space and collection points for recyclables and garbage.</span></a></li>
<li class="tocItem level6"><a href="12.14.160"><span class="num">12.14.160</span> <span class="name">Lot divided by zone boundary.</span></a></li>
<li class="tocItem level6"><a href="12.14.170"><span class="num">12.14.170</span> <span class="name">Site and exterior building design, generally.</span></a></li>
<li class="tocItem level6"><a href="12.14.180"><span class="num">12.14.180</span> <span class="name">Site design.</span></a></li>
<li class="tocItem level6"><a href="12.14.190"><span class="num">12.14.190</span> <span class="name">Exterior building design.</span></a></li>
<li class="tocItem level6"><a href="12.14.200"><span class="num">12.14.200</span> <span class="name">Exterior building design&#xA0;&#x2013; Multiple-family residential.</span></a></li>
<li class="tocItem level6"><a href="12.14.210"><span class="num">12.14.210</span> <span class="name">Exterior building design&#xA0;&#x2013; Commercial and industrial.</span></a></li>
<li class="tocItem level6"><a href="12.14.220"><span class="num">12.14.220</span> <span class="name">Exterior building design&#xA0;&#x2013; Multifamily residential, commercial or industrial development abutting single-family residential zoning.</span></a></li>
<li class="tocItem level6"><a href="12.14.230"><span class="num">12.14.230</span> <span class="name">Design of developments on or adjacent to historic register or historic inventory properties.</span></a></li>
<li class="tocItem level6"><a href="12.14.240"><span class="num">12.14.240</span> <span class="name">Exterior lighting.</span></a></li>
<li class="tocItem level6"><a href="12.14.250"><span class="num">12.14.250</span> <span class="name">Alternative energy infrastructure.</span></a></li>
</ul>
<article class="level6 chunking-small type-Section has-history" id="12.14.010" data-cite="BMC 12.14.010" data-short-cite="12.14.010" data-history="/BMC/12.14.010/history"><header class=" full-header" data-type="Section" data-search="/BMC/12.14.010/search" data-share="/BMC/12.14.010/share" data-levelnodes-target="header"><h6 class=" inner-header"><span class="num">12.14.010</span> <span class="name">Purpose.</span></h6></header><div class="track-indicator selected d-none">
<span class="glyphicons glyphicons-check" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="text">This section is included in your selections.</span>
</div>




<p>The purpose of this chapter is to establish area, dimension and design regulations which comply with and implement the goals and policies of the Imagine Bothell Comprehensive Plan with respect to the desired intensity and appearance of development within the city&#x2019;s residential, commercial and industrial areas. <span class="note history history--linked" data-action="click-&gt;levelnodes#openHistory">(Ord. 2171 &#xA7;&#xA0;3 (Exh. C), 2015; Ord. 2053 &#xA7;&#xA0;3 (Exh. C), 2010; Ord. 1876 &#xA7;&#xA0;2, 2002; Ord. 1815 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 2000; Ord. 1629 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 1996).</span></p>







</article>
<article class="level6 chunking-small type-Section has-history" id="12.14.020" data-cite="BMC 12.14.020" data-short-cite="12.14.020" data-history="/BMC/12.14.020/history"><header class=" full-header" data-type="Section" data-search="/BMC/12.14.020/search" data-share="/BMC/12.14.020/share" data-levelnodes-target="header"><h6 class=" inner-header"><span class="num">12.14.020</span> <span class="name">Interpretation of tables.</span></h6></header><div class="track-indicator selected d-none">
<span class="glyphicons glyphicons-check" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="text">This section is included in your selections.</span>
</div>




<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.020(A)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.020(A)" data-short-cite="12.14.020(A)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">A.&#x2002;</span></span>BMC <a class="cite" href="12.14.030" title="12.14.030&#x2002;Residential area and dimensions">12.14.030</a> and <a class="cite" href="12.14.040" title="12.14.040&#x2002;Commercial and industrial dimensions">12.14.040</a> contain general density and dimension standards for the various zones. Subarea regulations may establish specific and different density and dimensional standards and take precedent over city-wide regulations. Methods for measuring these city-wide standards are set forth in BMC <a class="cite" href="12.14.050" title="12.14.050&#x2002;Setbacks&#xA0;&#x2013; General measurement method">12.14.050</a> through <a class="cite" href="12.14.140" title="12.14.140&#x2002;Building and hard surface coverage">12.14.140</a>.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.020(B)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.020(B)" data-short-cite="12.14.020(B)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">B.&#x2002;</span></span>The area and dimension standards are arranged in a table for each of two general land use categories:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.020(B)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.020(B)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.020(B)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span></span>Residential;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.020(B)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.020(B)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.020(B)(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span></span>Commercial/industrial.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.020(C)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.020(C)" data-short-cite="12.14.020(C)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">C.&#x2002;</span></span>Development standards are listed down the left side of both tables, and the zones are listed at the top. The matrix cells contain the area and dimensional requirements of each zone. All dimensions are measured in lineal feet, and all areas are measured in square feet. The parenthetical numbers in the matrix identify specific requirements or other information which is set forth following the matrix. <span class="note history history--linked" data-action="click-&gt;levelnodes#openHistory">(Ord. 2353 &#xA7;&#xA0;8, 2021; Ord. 2171 &#xA7;&#xA0;3 (Exh. C), 2015; Ord. 2053 &#xA7;&#xA0;3 (Exh. C), 2010; Ord. 1876 &#xA7;&#xA0;2, 2002; Ord. 1815 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 2000; Ord. 1629 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 1996).</span></p>



</section>








</article>
<article class="level6 chunking-small type-Section has-history" id="12.14.030" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030" data-short-cite="12.14.030" data-history="/BMC/12.14.030/history"><header class=" full-header" data-type="Section" data-search="/BMC/12.14.030/search" data-share="/BMC/12.14.030/share" data-levelnodes-target="header"><h6 class=" inner-header"><span class="num">12.14.030</span> <span class="name">Residential area and dimensions.</span></h6></header><div class="track-indicator selected d-none">
<span class="glyphicons glyphicons-check" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="text">This section is included in your selections.</span>
</div>




<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.030(A)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(A)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(A)"><p class="header"><span class="num">A.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Development Standards Table.</span> </p>



<table class="role-medium table-ruled-outside-both table-ruled-inside-both"><colgroup><col style="width: 30pt;"/><col style="width: 30pt;"/><col style="width: 30pt;"/><col style="width: 30pt;"/><col style="width: 30pt;"/><col style="width: 30pt;"/><col style="width: 30pt;"/><col style="width: 30pt;"/><col style="width: 30pt;"/><col style="width: 30pt;"/>
</colgroup><thead>
<tr>
<th rowspan="2" colspan="2">

<p>Zoning Classification</p>

</td>

<p>Minimum side yard setback <a class="cite" href="12.14.030(B)(4)" title="12.14.030(B)(4)&#x2002;Setbacks">(4)</a><a class="cite" href="12.14.030(B)(8)" title="12.14.030(B)(8)">(8)</a><a class="cite" href="12.14.030(B)(9)" title="12.14.030(B)(9)">(9)</a></p>
</td>
<td></td>
<td>
<p>5</p>
</td>

</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>
<p>Maximum building height <a class="cite" href="12.14.030(B)(5)" title="12.14.030(B)(5)">(5)</a></p>
</td>
<td></td>
<td>
<p>35</p>
</td>
<td>
<p>35</p>
</td>
<td>
<p>35</p>
</td>

<p>35</p>
</td>

</td>
<td>
<p>45</p>
</td>
<td>
<p>55</p>
</td>
<td></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td rowspan="2">
<p>Maximum hard surface coverage <a class="cite" href="12.14.030(B)(7)" title="12.14.030(B)(7)">(7)</a></p>
</td>
<td></td>
<td>
<p>35%</p>
</td>
<td>
<p>55%</p>
</td>
<td>
<p>60%</p>
</td>
<td>
<p>70%</p>
</td>
<td>
<p>75%</p>
</td>
<td>
<p>80%</p>
</td>
<td>
<p>90%</p>
</td>
<td>
<p>80%</p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="5">
<p>The area within the front yard setback shall not contain any hard surface except for driveways, walkways, and structures allowed to project into the setback in accordance with BMC <a class="cite" href="12.14.090" title="12.14.090&#x2002;Setbacks&#xA0;&#x2013; Projections and facilities allowed">12.14.090</a>. If critical areas are present anywhere on the property, impervious surface coverage is further regulated by BMC Title <a class="cite" href="14" title="Title 14&#x2002;Environment">14</a>, Environment.</p>
</td>
<td colspan="4">
<p>If critical areas are present anywhere on the property, impervious surface coverage is further regulated by BMC Title <a class="cite" href="14" title="Title 14&#x2002;Environment">14</a>, Environment.</p>
</td>
</tr>
</tbody></table>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.030(B)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)"><p class="header"><span class="num">B.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Notes.</span> </p>



<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.030(B)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(1)"><p class="header"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">General Notes.</span> </p>



<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.030(B)(1)(a)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(1)(a)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(1)(a)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span></span>Where a district combines multiple zoning classifications or overlays (e.g., R-AC, OP, CB, MU-N), the most permissive standards of the individual zoning classifications shall apply.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.030(B)(1)(b)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(1)(b)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(1)(b)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span></span>Development standards may be modified through the planned unit development process. See Chapter <a class="cite" href="12.30" title="Ch. 12.30&#x2002;Planned Unit Developments">12.30</a> BMC.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.030(B)(1)(c)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(1)(c)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(1)(c)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">c.&#x2002;</span></span>Development standards for manufactured home parks are contained in Chapter <a class="cite" href="12.08" title="Ch. 12.08&#x2002;Manufactured Home Parks">12.08</a> BMC.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.030(B)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(2)"><p class="header"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Minimum, Average and Maximum Lot Area.</span> </p>



<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.030(B)(2)(a)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(2)(a)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(2)(a)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span></span>In the R-C zone, no lot shall be less than the minimum lot area except as may otherwise be permitted under an approved planned unit development, in accordance with Chapter <a class="cite" href="12.30" title="Ch. 12.30&#x2002;Planned Unit Developments">12.30</a> BMC or under Fitzgerald/35th Avenue SE Subarea regulations in accordance with Chapter <a class="cite" href="12.52" title="Ch. 12.52&#x2002;Fitzgerald/35th Avenue SE Subarea Regulations">12.52</a> BMC.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.030(B)(2)(b)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(2)(b)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(2)(b)"><p class="heading"><span class="header"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Lot Size Averaging in Land Divisions.</span> </span>Lot size averaging applies in the R-L1 and R-L2 zones. When creating new residential lots through land division, individual lots shall be considered in compliance with minimum lot size requirements if the average of the areas of all the lots in the land division meets the minimum requirement for the base zone in which the land division is located, provided:</p>




<section class="subsec subsec4 chunking-none type-Subsec4" id="12.14.030(B)(2)(b)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(2)(b)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(2)(b)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(1)&#x2002;</span></span>That no individual lot therein shall be reduced below the average lot size for the next densest zone. For example, lots in the R-L1 zone may not be reduced below R-L2 square feet and lots in the R-L2 zone may not be reduced below R-M1 square feet equivalent of units per acre.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.030(B)(2)(c)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(2)(c)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(2)(c)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">c.&#x2002;</span></span>In order to promote efficient use of land, no subdivision shall contain any lot having more than one and one-half times the minimum lot area in the R-C, R-L1, R-L2, and R-M1 zones, except as follows:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec4 chunking-none type-Subsec4" id="12.14.030(B)(2)(c)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(2)(c)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(2)(c)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(1)&#x2002;</span></span>Any subdivision of nine lots or fewer may contain larger lots, but the property lines of such a subdivision shall be laid out so as to allow future subdivisions which comply with this subsection;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec4 chunking-none type-Subsec4" id="12.14.030(B)(2)(c)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(2)(c)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(2)(c)(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(2)&#x2002;</span></span>A subdivision of 10 or more lots may contain larger lots to accommodate phasing of the subdivision; provided, that at completion of all phases, the subdivision complies with this subsection;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec4 chunking-none type-Subsec4" id="12.14.030(B)(2)(c)(3)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(2)(c)(3)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(2)(c)(3)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(3)&#x2002;</span></span>A subdivision of 10 or more lots may contain a larger lot to permit the preexisting house and any related outbuildings and grounds to be retained intact on one property;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec4 chunking-none type-Subsec4" id="12.14.030(B)(2)(c)(4)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(2)(c)(4)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(2)(c)(4)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(4)&#x2002;</span></span>These maximum lot size regulations do not apply to any common tracts for critical area protection, open space retention, storm water retention/detention or other purposes as may be required by the city as a condition of subdivision approval.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.030(B)(2)(d)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(2)(d)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(2)(d)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">d.&#x2002;</span></span>Land area in dedicated public rights-of-way, surface storm water retention/detention/water quality facilities located within required tracts, critical areas, critical area buffers, or any other land dedicated to the city, shall not be included in any proposed single-family lot, unless so stated in the conditions of an approved planned unit development, in accordance with Chapter <a class="cite" href="12.30" title="Ch. 12.30&#x2002;Planned Unit Developments">12.30</a> BMC.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.030(B)(2)(e)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(2)(e)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(2)(e)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">e.&#x2002;</span></span>Land in an access easement, utility easement, or other form of easement which is not set aside as a separate tract as required under subsection <a class="cite" href="12.14.030(B)(2)(c)(4)" title="12.14.030(B)(2)(c)(4)">(B)(2)(c)(4)</a> of this section shall be counted as part of the area of a parcel for the purpose of calculating minimum lot area.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.030(B)(3)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(3)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(3)"><p class="header"><span class="num">3.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Multifamily Minimum and Maximum Density.</span> </p>



<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.030(B)(3)(a)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(3)(a)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(3)(a)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span></span>In the R-M1 through R-M4 zones, one multifamily dwelling unit shall be allowed for each whole number multiple of the stated allowed density, measured as units per acre. Only whole numbers may be credited toward unit count. Rounding up is not permitted. The following exceptions apply:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec4 chunking-none type-Subsec4" id="12.14.030(B)(3)(a)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(3)(a)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(3)(a)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(1)&#x2002;</span></span>Specialized senior housing subject to an approved conditional use permit in accordance with Chapter <a class="cite" href="12.10" title="Ch. 12.10&#x2002;Specialized Senior Housing and Nursing Homes">12.10</a> BMC; or</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec4 chunking-none type-Subsec4" id="12.14.030(B)(3)(a)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(3)(a)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(3)(a)(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(2)&#x2002;</span></span>Middle housing developments created consistent with BMC <a class="cite" href="12.06.140" title="12.06.140&#x2002;Residential uses">12.06.140</a> and <a class="cite" href="12.14.134" title="12.14.134&#x2002;Middle housing">12.14.134</a>.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.030(B)(3)(b)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(3)(b)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(3)(b)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span></span>In order to promote efficient use of land, no multifamily development in the R-M2 through R-M4 zones shall contain fewer than 50 percent of the maximum base units for each zone, except as follows:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec4 chunking-none type-Subsec4" id="12.14.030(B)(3)(b)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(3)(b)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(3)(b)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(1)&#x2002;</span></span>The total number of units may be fewer than as required above if the development is proposed to be phased; provided, that at completion of all phases, the development complies with the above requirements;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec4 chunking-none type-Subsec4" id="12.14.030(B)(3)(b)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(3)(b)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(3)(b)(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(2)&#x2002;</span></span>These minimum density regulations do not apply to any common tracts for critical area protection, open space retention, storm water retention/detention or other purposes as may be required by the city as a condition of development approval.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.030(B)(3)(c)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(3)(c)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(3)(c)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">c.&#x2002;</span></span>In the R-M2 through R-M4 zones, density, calculated as units per acre, is determined using the net buildable area, unless so stated in the conditions of an approved planned unit development, in accordance with Chapter <a class="cite" href="12.30" title="Ch. 12.30&#x2002;Planned Unit Developments">12.30</a> BMC.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.030(B)(3)(d)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(3)(d)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(3)(d)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">d.&#x2002;</span></span>Land in an access easement, utility easement, or other form of easement which is not set aside as a separate tract as required under subsection <a class="cite" href="12.14.030(B)(3)(b)(2)" title="12.14.030(B)(3)(b)(2)">(B)(3)(b)(2)</a> of this section shall be counted as part of the net buildable area of a parcel for the purpose of calculating number of units allowed.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.030(B)(3)(e)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(3)(e)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(3)(e)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">e.&#x2002;</span></span>In the R-AC zone, minimum residential density is based on the net buildable area and shall be consistent with the applicable subarea regulations.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.030(B)(4)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(4)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(4)"><p class="header"><span class="num">4.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Setbacks.</span> </p>



<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.030(B)(4)(a)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(4)(a)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(4)(a)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span></span>&#x201C;Setback&#x201D; shall mean the distance from the lot line to the point where a structure may be constructed, not including those structures permitted under BMC <a class="cite" href="12.14.090" title="12.14.090&#x2002;Setbacks&#xA0;&#x2013; Projections and facilities allowed">12.14.090</a> to project into the setback. The area between a lot line and a setback is a yard. Setbacks shall be in accordance with BMC <a class="cite" href="12.14.050" title="12.14.050&#x2002;Setbacks&#xA0;&#x2013; General measurement method">12.14.050</a> through <a class="cite" href="12.14.100" title="12.14.100&#x2002;Setbacks&#xA0;&#x2013; Panhandle lots">12.14.100</a>.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.030(B)(4)(b)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(4)(b)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(4)(b)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span></span>Garages, carports, and other structures whose intent is to house or provide parking for vehicles shall be set back from streets and access tracts a minimum of 20 feet.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.030(B)(4)(c)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(4)(c)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(4)(c)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">c.&#x2002;</span></span>Special setbacks apply to specific building types, uses and accessory structures. See BMC <a class="cite" href="12.14.070" title="12.14.070&#x2002;Setbacks&#xA0;&#x2013; Applying to specific building or use">12.14.070</a>.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.030(B)(4)(d)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(4)(d)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(4)(d)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">d.&#x2002;</span></span>When middle housing is created by retaining and renovating a legally established nonconforming residential structure, building setbacks and height of the nonconforming residential structure can be matched in any building addition or expansion necessary to convert the existing dwelling unit to middle housing.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.030(B)(4)(e)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(4)(e)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(4)(e)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">e.&#x2002;</span></span>For duplex, triplex, or fourplex development, front and rear setbacks may be reduced to a minimum of five feet in the R-C, R-L1, and R-L2 zones, when at least one of the following conditions exists:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec4 chunking-none type-Subsec4" id="12.14.030(B)(4)(e)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(4)(e)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(4)(e)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(1)&#x2002;</span></span>Such reduction is allowed with an equal increase in setback distance on the opposite side of the structure. For example, a 10-foot reduction in front yard setback would require a 10-foot increase in rear yard setback.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec4 chunking-none type-Subsec4" id="12.14.030(B)(4)(e)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(4)(e)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(4)(e)(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(2)&#x2002;</span></span>Such reduction is allowed when necessary to preserve or create natural amenities on the lot. Natural amenities may include, but are not limited to, trees, low impact development features such as rain gardens, or other similar amenities as approved by the community development director. Such amenities shall be placed in a conservation, utility, drainage or similar easement.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec4 chunking-none type-Subsec4" id="12.14.030(B)(4)(e)(3)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(4)(e)(3)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(4)(e)(3)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(3)&#x2002;</span></span>Such reduction is allowed when the reduced front and rear setbacks or the associated increased setback pursued under subsection <a class="cite" href="12.14.030(B)(4)(e)(1)" title="12.14.030(B)(4)(e)(1)">(B)(4)(e)(1)</a> of this section is planted with a 10-foot-wide Type V landscape strip. Low impact development-compliant features such as rain gardens designed to treat storm water runoff from the entire site are allowed in lieu of the required planting strip.</p>



</section>
</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.030(B)(5)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(5)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(5)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">5.&#x2002;</span></span>Specific building height, minimum residential densities, and minimum floor area ratios may apply to certain activity centers as follows:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.030(B)(5)(a)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(5)(a)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(5)(a)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span></span>Within the following activity centers identified in the Imagine Bothell Comprehensive Plan:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec4 chunking-none type-Subsec4" id="12.14.030(B)(5)(a)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(5)(a)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(5)(a)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(1)&#x2002;</span></span>Filbert/Winesap neighborhood activity center, in accordance with Chapter <a class="cite" href="12.72" title="Ch. 12.72&#x2002;Filbert/Winesap Subarea Regulations">12.72</a> BMC.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec4 chunking-none type-Subsec4" id="12.14.030(B)(5)(a)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(5)(a)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(5)(a)(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(2)&#x2002;</span></span>Juanita-Woodinville Way/I-405 neighborhood activity center, in accordance with Chapter <a class="cite" href="12.60" title="Ch. 12.60&#x2002;Brickyard Road/Queensgate Subarea Regulations">12.60</a> BMC.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec4 chunking-none type-Subsec4" id="12.14.030(B)(5)(a)(3)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(5)(a)(3)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(5)(a)(3)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(3)&#x2002;</span></span>Maltby/York/Jewel neighborhood activity center, in accordance with Chapter <a class="cite" href="12.74" title="Ch. 12.74&#x2002;Thrasher&#x2019;s Corner/Red Hawk Subarea Regulations">12.74</a> BMC.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec4 chunking-none type-Subsec4" id="12.14.030(B)(5)(a)(4)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(5)(a)(4)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(5)(a)(4)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(4)&#x2002;</span></span>Canyon Park community activity center, in accordance with Chapter <a class="cite" href="12.48" title="Ch. 12.48&#x2002;Canyon Park Subarea Regulations">12.48</a> BMC. In zones where multiple designations exist, the commercial development regulations in BMC <a class="cite" href="12.14.040(A)" title="12.14.040(A)&#x2002;Development Standards Table">12.14.040(A)</a> shall apply.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec4 chunking-none type-Subsec4" id="12.14.030(B)(5)(a)(5)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(5)(a)(5)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(5)(a)(5)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(5)&#x2002;</span></span>Downtown community activity center, in accordance with Chapter <a class="cite" href="12.64" title="Ch. 12.64&#x2002;Downtown Subarea Regulations">12.64</a> BMC (Downtown Subarea Regulations).</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec4 chunking-none type-Subsec4" id="12.14.030(B)(5)(a)(6)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(5)(a)(6)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(5)(a)(6)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(6)&#x2002;</span></span>Thrasher&#x2019;s Corner community activity center, in accordance with Chapters <a class="cite" href="12.48" title="Ch. 12.48&#x2002;Canyon Park Subarea Regulations">12.48</a> and <a class="cite" href="12.74" title="Ch. 12.74&#x2002;Thrasher&#x2019;s Corner/Red Hawk Subarea Regulations">12.74</a> BMC.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec4 chunking-none type-Subsec4" id="12.14.030(B)(5)(a)(7)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(5)(a)(7)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(5)(a)(7)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(7)&#x2002;</span></span>Canyon Park regional activity center, in accordance with Chapter <a class="cite" href="12.48" title="Ch. 12.48&#x2002;Canyon Park Subarea Regulations">12.48</a> BMC.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec4 chunking-none type-Subsec4" id="12.14.030(B)(5)(a)(8)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(5)(a)(8)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(5)(a)(8)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(8)&#x2002;</span></span>North Creek regional activity center, in accordance with Chapter <a class="cite" href="12.56" title="Ch. 12.56&#x2002;North Creek/NE 195th Street Subarea Regulations">12.56</a> BMC.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec4 chunking-none type-Subsec4" id="12.14.030(B)(5)(a)(9)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(5)(a)(9)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(5)(a)(9)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(9)&#x2002;</span></span>Midtown neighborhood activity center in accordance with Chapter <a class="cite" href="12.46" title="Ch. 12.46&#x2002;Midtown Subarea Regulations">12.46</a> BMC.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.030(B)(5)(b)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(5)(b)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(5)(b)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span></span><i>Repealed by Ord. 2472.</i></p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.030(B)(6)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(6)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(6)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">6.&#x2002;</span></span>Hard surface coverage shall be measured as set forth in BMC <a class="cite" href="12.14.140" title="12.14.140&#x2002;Building and hard surface coverage">12.14.140</a>. For the purposes of this section, driveways within the front yard setback shall be no wider than necessary to accommodate three vehicles across, and walkways within the front yard setback shall not exceed five feet in width.</p>



<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.030(B)(6)(a)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(6)(a)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(6)(a)"><p><span class="header"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Notes.</span> </span>Hard surfaces legally established on a site prior to December 31, 2016, and which exceed the limits set forth in this section and BMC <a class="cite" href="12.14.040" title="12.14.040&#x2002;Commercial and industrial dimensions">12.14.040</a> shall be subject to nonconforming provisions of Chapter <a class="cite" href="12.26" title="Ch. 12.26&#x2002;Nonconforming Uses, Buildings and Other Site Improvements">12.26</a> BMC.</p>




</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.030(B)(6)(b)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(6)(b)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(6)(b)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span></span>In determining the hard surface coverage for a panhandle lot, the handle or access portion of the lot shall not be used to determine hard surface coverage. Hard surface coverage shall be measured as though no handle was on the lot.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.030(B)(6)(c)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(6)(c)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(6)(c)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">c.&#x2002;</span></span>For duplex, triplex and fourplex development, maximum hard surface coverage may be increased by 10 percent, subject to the conditions in subsection <a class="cite" href="12.14.030(B)" title="12.14.030(B)&#x2002;Notes">(B)</a> of this section.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.030(B)(7)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(7)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(7)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">7.&#x2002;</span></span>Within the R-AC zoning classification, minimum residential density is prescribed in the applicable subarea regulations. The maximum number of dwelling units attainable shall be controlled by the applicable site and building envelope design regulations.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.030(B)(8)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(8)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(8)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">8.&#x2002;</span></span>Lands with a zoning classification of R-M3, R-M4, DC, DT, DN, GDC, or R-AC where offspring or unit lots are proposed to be created within a parent site through the subdivision provisions of BMC Title <a class="cite" href="15" title="Title 15&#x2002;Subdivisions">15</a> may apply setback dimensions to the offspring lots as set forth in BMC <a class="cite" href="12.14.085" title="12.14.085&#x2002;Setbacks&#xA0;&#x2013; Offspring and unit lot setbacks applicable within attached residential zones">12.14.085</a>.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.030(B)(9)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.030(B)(9)" data-short-cite="12.14.030(B)(9)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">9.&#x2002;</span></span>Within specific subareas duplexes shall be allowed on individual lots to meet affordable housing requirements (see Chapter <a class="cite" href="12.07" title="Ch. 12.07&#x2002;Affordable Housing">12.07</a> BMC). Such duplexes do not need to meet the side yard setbacks for the common walls between dwelling units, but all duplex lots shall provide all setbacks from adjacent property lines pursuant to subsection <a class="cite" href="12.14.030(A)" title="12.14.030(A)&#x2002;Development Standards Table">A</a> of this section. <span class="note history history--linked" data-action="click-&gt;levelnodes#openHistory">(Ord. 2472 &#xA7;&#xA0;1 (Exh. A), 2025; Ord. 2459 &#xA7;&#xA0;1 (Exh. A), 2025; Ord. 2457 &#xA7;&#xA0;3 (Exh. C), 2025; Ord. 2445 &#xA7;&#xA0;6 (Exh. F), 2024; Ord. 2415 &#xA7;&#xA0;5 (Exh. D), 2024; Ord. 2373 &#xA7;&#xA7;&#xA0;6, 7, 2022; Ord. 2353 &#xA7;&#xA0;8, 2021; Ord. 2348 &#xA7;&#xA7;&#xA0;4, 5, 2021; Ord. 2282 &#xA7;&#xA0;5, 2019; Ord. 2258 &#xA7;&#xA0;2, 2018; Ord. 2255 &#xA7;&#xA0;3, 2018; Ord. 2252 &#xA7;&#xA7;&#xA0;6, 7, 2018; Ord. 2200 &#xA7;&#xA0;2 (Exh. B), 2016; Ord. 2171 &#xA7;&#xA0;3 (Exh. C), 2015; Ord. 2140 &#xA7;&#xA0;2 (Exh. B), 2014; Ord. 2123 &#xA7;&#xA0;2 (Exh. B), 2013; Ord. 2053 &#xA7;&#xA0;3 (Exh. C), 2010; Ord. 2043 &#xA7;&#xA0;2 (Exh. B), 2010; Ord. 1995 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 2008; Ord. 1988 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 2008; Ord. 1980 &#xA7;&#xA0;2, 2007; Ord. 1946 &#xA7;&#xA0;2, 2005; Ord. 1876 &#xA7;&#xA0;2, 2002; Ord. 1871 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 2002; Ord. 1815 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 2000; Ord. 1798 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 2000; Ord. 1629 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 1996).</span></p>



</section>
</section>





























</article>
<article class="level6 chunking-small type-Section has-history" id="12.14.040" data-cite="BMC 12.14.040" data-short-cite="12.14.040" data-history="/BMC/12.14.040/history"><header class=" full-header" data-type="Section" data-search="/BMC/12.14.040/search" data-share="/BMC/12.14.040/share" data-levelnodes-target="header"><h6 class=" inner-header"><span class="num">12.14.040</span> <span class="name">Commercial and industrial dimensions.</span></h6></header><div class="track-indicator selected d-none">
<span class="glyphicons glyphicons-check" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="text">This section is included in your selections.</span>
</div>




<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.040(A)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.040(A)" data-short-cite="12.14.040(A)"><p class="header"><span class="num">A.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Development Standards Table.</span> </p>



<table class="role-medium table-ruled-outside-both table-ruled-inside-both"><colgroup><col style="width: 80pt;"/><col style="width: 80pt;"/><col style="width: 80pt;"/><col style="width: 80pt;"/><col style="width: 80pt;"/><col style="width: 80pt;"/>
</colgroup><thead>
<tr>
<th rowspan="2">
<p>Commercial and Industrial Development Standards <a class="cite" href="12.14.040(B)(1)" title="12.14.040(B)(1)&#x2002;General Notes">(1)</a></p>
</th>
<th colspan="5">
<p>Zoning Classification</p>
</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th>
<p>OP</p>
</th>
<th>
<p>NB</p>
</th>
<th>
<p>CB</p>
</th>
<th>
<p>GC</p>
</th>
<th>
<p>LI</p>
</th>
</tr>
</thead><tbody>
<tr>
<td>
<p>Minimum front yard setback <a class="cite" href="12.14.040(B)(2)" title="12.14.040(B)(2)">(2)</a></p>
</td>
<td rowspan="3" colspan="5">
<p>Setbacks shall be the distance required for landscaping, in accordance with Chapter <a class="cite" href="12.18" title="Ch. 12.18&#x2002;Tree Retention and Landscaping">12.18</a> BMC, except as otherwise provided under BMC <a class="cite" href="12.14.080" title="12.14.080&#x2002;Setbacks&#xA0;&#x2013; Modifications">12.14.080</a></p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>
<p>Minimum rear yard setback <a class="cite" href="12.14.040(B)(2)" title="12.14.040(B)(2)">(2)</a></p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>
<p>Minimum side yard setback <a class="cite" href="12.14.040(B)(2)" title="12.14.040(B)(2)">(2)</a></p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>
<p>Maximum building height <a class="cite" href="12.14.040(B)(3)" title="12.14.040(B)(3)">(3)</a></p>
</td>
<td>
<p>35, or as established for designated centers <a class="cite" href="12.14.040(B)(3)" title="12.14.040(B)(3)">(3)</a></p>
</td>
<td>
<p>35, or as established for designated centers</p>
</td>
<td>
<p>35, or as established for designated centers <a class="cite" href="12.14.040(B)(3)" title="12.14.040(B)(3)">(3)</a></p>
</td>
<td>
<p>35</p>
</td>
<td>
<p>35, or as established for designated centers <a class="cite" href="12.14.040(B)(3)" title="12.14.040(B)(3)">(3)</a></p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>
<p>Floor area ratios <a class="cite" href="12.14.040(B)(3)" title="12.14.040(B)(3)">(3)</a></p>
</td>
<td>
<p>As established for designated centers</p>
</td>
<td>
<p>As established for designated centers</p>
</td>
<td>
<p>As established for designated centers</p>
</td>
<td>
<p>As established for designated centers</p>
</td>
<td>
<p>As established for designated centers</p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>
<p>Maximum hard surface coverage <a class="cite" href="12.14.040(B)(4)" title="12.14.040(B)(4)">(4)</a></p>
</td>
<td rowspan="1" colspan="5">
<p>100%, except for any required landscaping, in accordance with Chapter <a class="cite" href="12.18" title="Ch. 12.18&#x2002;Tree Retention and Landscaping">12.18</a> BMC, and required critical areas and their buffers, in accordance with BMC Title <a class="cite" href="14" title="Title 14&#x2002;Environment">14</a>, Environment</p>
</td>
</tr>
</tbody></table>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.040(B)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.040(B)" data-short-cite="12.14.040(B)"><p class="header"><span class="num">B.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Notes.</span> </p>



<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.040(B)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.040(B)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.040(B)(1)"><p class="header"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">General Notes.</span> </p>



<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.040(B)(1)(a)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.040(B)(1)(a)" data-short-cite="12.14.040(B)(1)(a)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span></span>Where a district combines multiple zoning classifications (e.g., R-AC, OP, CB), the most permissive standards of the individual zoning classifications shall apply; provided, however, that within activity centers, minimum density and/or floor area ratio requirements as established within the applicable subarea regulations shall be met.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.040(B)(1)(b)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.040(B)(1)(b)" data-short-cite="12.14.040(B)(1)(b)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span></span>Development standards may be modified through the planned unit development process consistent with Chapter <a class="cite" href="12.30" title="Ch. 12.30&#x2002;Planned Unit Developments">12.30</a> BMC.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.040(B)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.040(B)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.040(B)(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span></span>Setbacks shall be in accordance with BMC <a class="cite" href="12.14.050" title="12.14.050&#x2002;Setbacks&#xA0;&#x2013; General measurement method">12.14.050</a> through <a class="cite" href="12.14.100" title="12.14.100&#x2002;Setbacks&#xA0;&#x2013; Panhandle lots">12.14.100</a>.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.040(B)(3)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.040(B)(3)" data-short-cite="12.14.040(B)(3)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">3.&#x2002;</span></span>Specific building height, minimum residential densities, and minimum floor area ratios may apply in the following activity centers identified in the Imagine Bothell Comprehensive Plan:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.040(B)(3)(a)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.040(B)(3)(a)" data-short-cite="12.14.040(B)(3)(a)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span></span>Canyon Park community activity center, in accordance with Chapters <a class="cite" href="12.44" title="Ch. 12.44&#x2002;Shelton View/Meridian/3rd Avenue SE Subarea Regulations">12.44</a> and <a class="cite" href="12.48" title="Ch. 12.48&#x2002;Canyon Park Subarea Regulations">12.48</a> BMC.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.040(B)(3)(b)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.040(B)(3)(b)" data-short-cite="12.14.040(B)(3)(b)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span></span>Thrasher&#x2019;s Corner community activity center, in accordance with Chapters <a class="cite" href="12.48" title="Ch. 12.48&#x2002;Canyon Park Subarea Regulations">12.48</a> and <a class="cite" href="12.74" title="Ch. 12.74&#x2002;Thrasher&#x2019;s Corner/Red Hawk Subarea Regulations">12.74</a> BMC.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.040(B)(3)(c)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.040(B)(3)(c)" data-short-cite="12.14.040(B)(3)(c)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">c.&#x2002;</span></span>Canyon Park regional activity center, in accordance with Chapter <a class="cite" href="12.48" title="Ch. 12.48&#x2002;Canyon Park Subarea Regulations">12.48</a> BMC.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.040(B)(3)(d)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.040(B)(3)(d)" data-short-cite="12.14.040(B)(3)(d)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">d.&#x2002;</span></span>North Creek regional activity center, in accordance with Chapter <a class="cite" href="12.56" title="Ch. 12.56&#x2002;North Creek/NE 195th Street Subarea Regulations">12.56</a> BMC.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.040(B)(3)(e)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.040(B)(3)(e)" data-short-cite="12.14.040(B)(3)(e)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">e.&#x2002;</span></span>Downtown community activity center, in accordance with Chapter <a class="cite" href="12.64" title="Ch. 12.64&#x2002;Downtown Subarea Regulations">12.64</a> BMC (Downtown Subarea Regulations).</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.040(B)(3)(f)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.040(B)(3)(f)" data-short-cite="12.14.040(B)(3)(f)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">f.&#x2002;</span></span>Juanita-Woodinville Way/I-405 neighborhood activity center, in accordance with Chapters <a class="cite" href="12.60" title="Ch. 12.60&#x2002;Brickyard Road/Queensgate Subarea Regulations">12.60</a> and <a class="cite" href="12.66" title="Ch. 12.66&#x2002;Waynita/Simonds/Norway Hill Subarea Regulations">12.66</a> BMC.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.040(B)(3)(g)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.040(B)(3)(g)" data-short-cite="12.14.040(B)(3)(g)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">g.&#x2002;</span></span>Filbert/Winesap neighborhood activity center, in accordance with Chapter <a class="cite" href="12.72" title="Ch. 12.72&#x2002;Filbert/Winesap Subarea Regulations">12.72</a> BMC.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.040(B)(3)(h)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.040(B)(3)(h)" data-short-cite="12.14.040(B)(3)(h)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">h.&#x2002;</span></span>Maltby/York/Jewel neighborhood activity center, in accordance with Chapter <a class="cite" href="12.74" title="Ch. 12.74&#x2002;Thrasher&#x2019;s Corner/Red Hawk Subarea Regulations">12.74</a> BMC.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.040(B)(3)(i)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.040(B)(3)(i)" data-short-cite="12.14.040(B)(3)(i)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">i.&#x2002;</span></span>Country Village/Bothell-Everett Highway/Lake Pleasant/Bothell-Everett Highway neighborhood activity center in accordance with Chapter <a class="cite" href="12.46" title="Ch. 12.46&#x2002;Midtown Subarea Regulations">12.46</a> BMC.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.040(B)(4)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.040(B)(4)" data-short-cite="12.14.040(B)(4)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">4.&#x2002;</span></span>Building and hard surface coverage shall be measured as set forth in BMC <a class="cite" href="12.14.140" title="12.14.140&#x2002;Building and hard surface coverage">12.14.140</a>.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.040(C)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.040(C)" data-short-cite="12.14.040(C)"><p class="header"><span class="num">C.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Development Standards Table&#xA0;&#x2013; Overlays.</span> </p>



<table class="role-medium table-ruled-outside-both table-ruled-inside-both"><colgroup><col style="width: 80pt;"/><col style="width: 80pt;"/><col style="width: 80pt;"/><col style="width: 80pt;"/><col style="width: 80pt;"/><col style="width: 80pt;"/>
</colgroup><thead>
<tr>
<th>
<p><b></b>Commercial and Industrial Development Standards (1)</p>
</th>
<th colspan="5">
<p>Zoning Classification</p>
</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th>
<p></p>
</th>
<th>
<p>MU-N</p>
</th>
<th>
<p>MU-C</p>
</th>
<th>
<p>MU-E</p>
</th>
<th>
<p>C-G</p>
</th>
<th>
<p>E-MC</p>
</th>
</tr>
</thead><tbody>
<tr>
<td>
<p>Minimum front yard setback <a class="cite" href="12.14.040(B)(2)" title="12.14.040(B)(2)">(2)</a></p>
</td>
<td colspan="5" rowspan="3">
<p>Setbacks shall be the distance required for landscaping, in accordance with Chapter <a class="cite" href="12.18" title="Ch. 12.18&#x2002;Tree Retention and Landscaping">12.18</a> BMC, except as otherwise provided under BMC <a class="cite" href="12.14.080" title="12.14.080&#x2002;Setbacks&#xA0;&#x2013; Modifications">12.14.080</a></p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>
<p>Minimum rear yard setback <a class="cite" href="12.14.040(B)(2)" title="12.14.040(B)(2)">(2)</a></p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>
<p>Minimum side yard setback <a class="cite" href="12.14.040(B)(2)" title="12.14.040(B)(2)">(2)</a></p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>
<p>Maximum building height <a class="cite" href="12.14.040(B)(3)" title="12.14.040(B)(3)">(3)</a></p>
</td>
<td>
<p>60, or as established for designated centers, whichever is greater <a class="cite" href="12.14.040(B)(3)" title="12.14.040(B)(3)">(3)</a></p>
</td>
<td>
<p>60, or as established for designated centers, whichever is greater <a class="cite" href="12.14.040(B)(3)" title="12.14.040(B)(3)">(3)</a></p>
</td>
<td>
<p>85, or as established for designated centers, whichever is greater <a class="cite" href="12.14.040(B)(3)" title="12.14.040(B)(3)">(3)</a></p>
</td>
<td>
<p>35</p>
</td>
<td>
<p>60, or as established for designated centers, whichever is greater <a class="cite" href="12.14.040(B)(3)" title="12.14.040(B)(3)">(3)</a></p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>
<p>Floor area ratios<a class="cite" href="12.14.040(B)(3)" title="12.14.040(B)(3)">(3)</a></p>
</td>
<td>
<p>3.0 or as established for designated centers, whichever is greater</p>
</td>
<td>
<p>3.0 or as established for designated centers, whichever is greater</p>
</td>
<td>
<p>3.5 or as established for designated centers, whichever is greater</p>
</td>
<td>
<p>2.0 or as established for designated centers, whichever is greater</p>
</td>
<td>
<p>3.0 or as established for designated centers, whichever is greater</p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>
<p>Maximum hard surface coverage<a class="cite" href="12.14.040(B)(4)" title="12.14.040(B)(4)">(4)</a></p>
</td>
<td colspan="5" rowspan="1">
<p>100%, except for any required landscaping, in accordance with Chapter <a class="cite" href="12.18" title="Ch. 12.18&#x2002;Tree Retention and Landscaping">12.18</a> BMC, and required critical areas and their buffers, in accordance with BMC Title <a class="cite" href="14" title="Title 14&#x2002;Environment">14</a>, Environment</p>
</td>
</tr>
</tbody></table>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.040(D)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.040(D)" data-short-cite="12.14.040(D)"><p class="header"><span class="num">D.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Notes.</span> </p>



<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.040(D)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.040(D)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.040(D)(1)"><p class="header"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">General Notes.</span> </p>



<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.040(D)(1)(a)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.040(D)(1)(a)" data-short-cite="12.14.040(D)(1)(a)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span></span>Where a district combines multiple zoning classifications (e.g., R-AC, OP, CB), the most permissive standards of the individual zoning classifications shall apply; provided, however, that within activity centers, minimum density and/or floor area ratio requirements as established within the applicable subarea regulations shall be met.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.040(D)(1)(b)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.040(D)(1)(b)" data-short-cite="12.14.040(D)(1)(b)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span></span>Development standards may be modified through the planned unit development process consistent with Chapter <a class="cite" href="12.30" title="Ch. 12.30&#x2002;Planned Unit Developments">12.30</a> BMC.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.040(D)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.040(D)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.040(D)(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span></span>Setbacks shall be in accordance with BMC <a class="cite" href="12.14.050" title="12.14.050&#x2002;Setbacks&#xA0;&#x2013; General measurement method">12.14.050</a> through <a class="cite" href="12.14.100" title="12.14.100&#x2002;Setbacks&#xA0;&#x2013; Panhandle lots">12.14.100</a>.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.040(D)(3)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.040(D)(3)" data-short-cite="12.14.040(D)(3)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">3.&#x2002;</span></span>Specific building height, minimum residential densities, and minimum floor area ratios may apply in the following activity centers identified in the Imagine Bothell Comprehensive Plan:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.040(D)(3)(a)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.040(D)(3)(a)" data-short-cite="12.14.040(D)(3)(a)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span></span>Canyon Park community activity center, in accordance with Chapters <a class="cite" href="12.44" title="Ch. 12.44&#x2002;Shelton View/Meridian/3rd Avenue SE Subarea Regulations">12.44</a> and <a class="cite" href="12.48" title="Ch. 12.48&#x2002;Canyon Park Subarea Regulations">12.48</a> BMC.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.040(D)(3)(b)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.040(D)(3)(b)" data-short-cite="12.14.040(D)(3)(b)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span></span>Thrasher&#x2019;s Corner community activity center, in accordance with Chapters <a class="cite" href="12.48" title="Ch. 12.48&#x2002;Canyon Park Subarea Regulations">12.48</a> and <a class="cite" href="12.74" title="Ch. 12.74&#x2002;Thrasher&#x2019;s Corner/Red Hawk Subarea Regulations">12.74</a> BMC.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.040(D)(3)(c)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.040(D)(3)(c)" data-short-cite="12.14.040(D)(3)(c)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">c.&#x2002;</span></span>Canyon Park regional activity center, in accordance with Chapter <a class="cite" href="12.48" title="Ch. 12.48&#x2002;Canyon Park Subarea Regulations">12.48</a> BMC.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.040(D)(3)(d)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.040(D)(3)(d)" data-short-cite="12.14.040(D)(3)(d)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">d.&#x2002;</span></span>North Creek regional activity center, in accordance with Chapter <a class="cite" href="12.56" title="Ch. 12.56&#x2002;North Creek/NE 195th Street Subarea Regulations">12.56</a> BMC.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.040(D)(3)(e)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.040(D)(3)(e)" data-short-cite="12.14.040(D)(3)(e)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">e.&#x2002;</span></span>Downtown community activity center, in accordance with Chapter <a class="cite" href="12.64" title="Ch. 12.64&#x2002;Downtown Subarea Regulations">12.64</a> BMC (Downtown Subarea Regulations).</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.040(D)(3)(f)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.040(D)(3)(f)" data-short-cite="12.14.040(D)(3)(f)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">f.&#x2002;</span></span>Juanita-Woodinville Way/I-405 neighborhood activity center, in accordance with Chapters <a class="cite" href="12.60" title="Ch. 12.60&#x2002;Brickyard Road/Queensgate Subarea Regulations">12.60</a> and <a class="cite" href="12.66" title="Ch. 12.66&#x2002;Waynita/Simonds/Norway Hill Subarea Regulations">12.66</a> BMC.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.040(D)(3)(g)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.040(D)(3)(g)" data-short-cite="12.14.040(D)(3)(g)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">g.&#x2002;</span></span>Filbert/Winesap neighborhood activity center, in accordance with Chapter <a class="cite" href="12.72" title="Ch. 12.72&#x2002;Filbert/Winesap Subarea Regulations">12.72</a> BMC.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.040(D)(3)(h)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.040(D)(3)(h)" data-short-cite="12.14.040(D)(3)(h)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">h.&#x2002;</span></span>Maltby/York/Jewel neighborhood activity center, in accordance with Chapter <a class="cite" href="12.74" title="Ch. 12.74&#x2002;Thrasher&#x2019;s Corner/Red Hawk Subarea Regulations">12.74</a> BMC.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.040(D)(3)(i)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.040(D)(3)(i)" data-short-cite="12.14.040(D)(3)(i)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">i.&#x2002;</span></span>Country Village/Bothell-Everett Highway/Lake Pleasant/Bothell-Everett Highway neighborhood activity center in accordance with Chapter <a class="cite" href="12.46" title="Ch. 12.46&#x2002;Midtown Subarea Regulations">12.46</a> BMC.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.040(D)(4)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.040(D)(4)" data-short-cite="12.14.040(D)(4)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">4.&#x2002;</span></span>Building and hard surface coverage shall be measured as set forth in BMC <a class="cite" href="12.14.140" title="12.14.140&#x2002;Building and hard surface coverage">12.14.140</a>. <span class="note history history--linked" data-action="click-&gt;levelnodes#openHistory">(Ord. 2459 &#xA7;&#xA0;1 (Exh. A), 2025; Ord. 2445 &#xA7;&#xA0;6 (Exh. F), 2024; Ord. 2353 &#xA7;&#xA0;8, 2021; Ord. 2200 &#xA7;&#xA0;2 (Exh. B), 2016; Ord. 2171 &#xA7;&#xA0;3 (Exh. C), 2015; Ord. 2053 &#xA7;&#xA0;3 (Exh. C), 2010; Ord. 2043 &#xA7;&#xA0;2 (Exh. B), 2010; Ord. 1876 &#xA7;&#xA0;2, 2002; Ord. 1871 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 2002; Ord. 1815 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 2000; Ord. 1629 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 1996).</span></p>



</section>
</section>













</article>
<article class="level6 chunking-small type-Section has-history" id="12.14.050" data-cite="BMC 12.14.050" data-short-cite="12.14.050" data-history="/BMC/12.14.050/history"><header class=" full-header" data-type="Section" data-search="/BMC/12.14.050/search" data-share="/BMC/12.14.050/share" data-levelnodes-target="header"><h6 class=" inner-header"><span class="num">12.14.050</span> <span class="name">Setbacks&#xA0;&#x2013; General measurement method.</span></h6></header><div class="track-indicator selected d-none">
<span class="glyphicons glyphicons-check" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="text">This section is included in your selections.</span>
</div>




<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.050(A)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.050(A)" data-short-cite="12.14.050(A)"><p class="header"><span class="num">A.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Front Yard Setbacks.</span> </p>



<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.050(A)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.050(A)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.050(A)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span></span>Front yard setbacks shall be measured from the street right-of-way boundary to a line parallel to and measured perpendicularly from the street right-of-way boundary. In the case of corner lots, there shall be a front yard setback provided for each lot side abutting an existing or proposed public right-of-way. The yards opposite said front yards need only meet the side yard setback requirements.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.050(A)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.050(A)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.050(A)(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span></span>Where a property abuts a street identified as a collector, minor arterial, principal arterial or freeway/limited access highway in the Imagine Bothell Comprehensive Plan, the front yard setback shall be measured from the projected right-of-way boundary for the street as if it were fully developed in accordance with the plan.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.050(A)(3)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.050(A)(3)" data-short-cite="12.14.050(A)(3)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">3.&#x2002;</span></span>Should the edge of a street surface improvement, including roadway, curb and gutter, and sidewalk, extend beyond the street right-of-way boundary, the front yard setback shall be measured from the edge of the surface improvement. For the purposes of this chapter, the edge of the surface improvements shall be the edge of the pavement, curb or sidewalk.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.050(B)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.050(B)" data-short-cite="12.14.050(B)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">B.&#x2002;</span></span>Side and rear yard setbacks shall be measured from the interior lot line to a line parallel to and measured perpendicularly from the interior lot lines at the depth prescribed for each zone.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.050(C)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.050(C)" data-short-cite="12.14.050(C)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">C.&#x2002;</span></span>The director is authorized to designate front, side and rear yard setbacks in accordance with subsections <a class="cite" href="12.14.050(A)" title="12.14.050(A)&#x2002;Front Yard Setbacks">A</a> and <a class="cite" href="12.14.050(B)" title="12.14.050(B)">B</a> of this section. If these subsections do not establish a front, side and rear yard setback, the director shall establish the setbacks based upon orientation of the lot to surrounding lots and to any existing development pattern. All other setbacks shall be established in relation to the established front, side and rear yard setbacks. <span class="note history history--linked" data-action="click-&gt;levelnodes#openHistory">(Ord. 2123 &#xA7;&#xA0;2 (Exh. B), 2013; Ord. 1815 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 2000; Ord. 1798 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 2000; Ord. 1629 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 1996).</span></p>



</section>






</article>
<article class="level6 chunking-small type-Section has-history" id="12.14.060" data-cite="BMC 12.14.060" data-short-cite="12.14.060" data-history="/BMC/12.14.060/history"><header class=" full-header" data-type="Section" data-search="/BMC/12.14.060/search" data-share="/BMC/12.14.060/share" data-levelnodes-target="header"><h6 class=" inner-header"><span class="num">12.14.060</span> <span class="name">Setbacks&#xA0;&#x2013; Adjoining alleys.</span></h6></header><div class="track-indicator selected d-none">
<span class="glyphicons glyphicons-check" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="text">This section is included in your selections.</span>
</div>




<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.060(A)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.060(A)" data-short-cite="12.14.060(A)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">A.&#x2002;</span></span>Structures shall be set back from a property line abutting an alley a minimum of five feet, except as provided in BMC <a class="cite" href="12.14.030" title="12.14.030&#x2002;Residential area and dimensions">12.14.030</a> and subsection <a class="cite" href="12.14.060(B)" title="12.14.060(B)">B</a> of this section.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.060(B)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.060(B)" data-short-cite="12.14.060(B)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">B.&#x2002;</span></span>Vehicle access points from garages, carports or fenced parking areas shall be set back from the alley property line to provide a straight line length of at least 25 feet from the access point to the opposite edge of the alley. No portion of the garage or the garage door in motion may cross the property line. <span class="note history history--linked" data-action="click-&gt;levelnodes#openHistory">(Ord. 2445 &#xA7;&#xA0;6 (Exh. F), 2024; Ord. 2123 &#xA7;&#xA0;2 (Exh. B), 2013; Ord. 1815 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 2000; Ord. 1629 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 1996).</span></p>



</section>






</article>
<article class="level6 chunking-small type-Section has-history" id="12.14.070" data-cite="BMC 12.14.070" data-short-cite="12.14.070" data-history="/BMC/12.14.070/history"><header class=" full-header" data-type="Section" data-search="/BMC/12.14.070/search" data-share="/BMC/12.14.070/share" data-levelnodes-target="header"><h6 class=" inner-header"><span class="num">12.14.070</span> <span class="name">Setbacks&#xA0;&#x2013; Applying to specific building or use.</span></h6></header><div class="track-indicator selected d-none">
<span class="glyphicons glyphicons-check" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="text">This section is included in your selections.</span>
</div>




<p>When a building or use is required to maintain a specific setback from a property line or other building, such setback shall apply only to the specified building or use.</p>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.070(A)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.070(A)" data-short-cite="12.14.070(A)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">A.&#x2002;</span></span>Any structures containing two or more attached primary dwelling units shall be set back a minimum of 25 feet from any property line in or abutting the R-40,000 zone, unless the adjacent property already is occupied by structures containing two or more primary dwelling units. This special setback requirement shall not apply to middle housing structures within detached residential zones that provide affordable dwelling units consistent with the affordable housing provisions of Chapter <a class="cite" href="12.07" title="Ch. 12.07&#x2002;Affordable Housing">12.07</a> BMC.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.070(B)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.070(B)" data-short-cite="12.14.070(B)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">B.&#x2002;</span></span>Within all residential zone classifications, rear yard setbacks for accessory buildings and structures, other than detached accessory dwelling units, shall be five feet. Detached accessory dwelling units shall maintain the same rear yard setback as primary structures, except for those accessory dwelling units located on an alley, in which case the setbacks for structures adjoining alleys in BMC <a class="cite" href="12.14.060" title="12.14.060&#x2002;Setbacks&#xA0;&#x2013; Adjoining alleys">12.14.060</a> shall apply.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.070(C)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.070(C)" data-short-cite="12.14.070(C)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">C.&#x2002;</span></span>Gas station pump islands shall be set back a minimum of 25 feet from any property line.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.070(D)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.070(D)" data-short-cite="12.14.070(D)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">D.&#x2002;</span></span>Abutting an Residential-zoned property, not including combined zones, the setback shall be 50 feet for any building containing manufacturing, distribution, storage or warehousing uses, and 30 feet for any other nonresidential use.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.070(E)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.070(E)" data-short-cite="12.14.070(E)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">E.&#x2002;</span></span>Pad-mounted heating, ventilation, and air conditioning (HVAC) equipment such as heat pumps, air conditioning units, furnaces, and other similar pad-mounted equipment installed after October 16, 2010, shall comply with the following (note: these requirements shall not apply to portable generator use during times of emergency):</p>



<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.070(E)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.070(E)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.070(E)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span></span>Not be located in any front yard setback area;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.070(E)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.070(E)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.070(E)(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span></span>Be located at least three feet from any adjacent property line or structure, including fences;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.070(E)(3)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.070(E)(3)" data-short-cite="12.14.070(E)(3)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">3.&#x2002;</span></span>Be screened from view of any public right-of-way by means of either landscaping or fencing. Any such screening should be installed such that it can either be readily removed to provide access to said equipment, or should be placed at least three feet from said equipment to allow for access. <span class="note history history--linked" data-action="click-&gt;levelnodes#openHistory">(Ord. 2415 &#xA7;&#xA0;5 (Exh. D), 2024; Ord. 2373 &#xA7;&#xA0;8, 2022; Ord. 2258 &#xA7;&#xA0;3, 2018; Ord. 2255 &#xA7;&#xA0;4, 2018; Ord. 2171 &#xA7;&#xA0;3 (Exh. C), 2015; Ord. 2123 &#xA7;&#xA0;2 (Exh. B), 2013; Ord. 2047 &#xA7;&#xA0;2 (Exh. B), 2010; Ord. 1946 &#xA7;&#xA0;2, 2005; Ord. 1815 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 2000; Ord. 1629 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 1996. Formerly 12.14.080).</span></p>



</section>
</section>













</article>
<article class="level6 chunking-small type-Section has-history" id="12.14.080" data-cite="BMC 12.14.080" data-short-cite="12.14.080" data-history="/BMC/12.14.080/history"><header class=" full-header" data-type="Section" data-search="/BMC/12.14.080/search" data-share="/BMC/12.14.080/share" data-levelnodes-target="header"><h6 class=" inner-header"><span class="num">12.14.080</span> <span class="name">Setbacks&#xA0;&#x2013; Modifications.</span></h6></header><div class="track-indicator selected d-none">
<span class="glyphicons glyphicons-check" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="text">This section is included in your selections.</span>
</div>




<p class="heading">The following setback modifications are permitted:</p>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.080(A)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.080(A)" data-short-cite="12.14.080(A)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">A.&#x2002;</span></span>When the common property line of two lots is covered by a building(s), the setbacks required by this chapter shall not apply along the common property line;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.080(B)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.080(B)" data-short-cite="12.14.080(B)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">B.&#x2002;</span></span>When a lot is located between lots containing structures which are located at nonconforming front yard setbacks, the required front yard setback for such lot may be the average of the two nonconforming setbacks or 60 percent of the required front yard setback, whichever results in the greater front yard setback; and</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.080(C)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.080(C)" data-short-cite="12.14.080(C)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">C.&#x2002;</span></span>Any modification of setbacks shall be reviewed by the fire department for potential effects on fire flows. <span class="note history history--linked" data-action="click-&gt;levelnodes#openHistory">(Ord. 2123 &#xA7;&#xA0;2 (Exh. B), 2013; Ord. 1815 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 2000; Ord. 1629 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 1996. Formerly 12.14.090).</span></p>



</section>






</article>
<article class="level6 chunking-small type-Section has-history" id="12.14.085" data-cite="BMC 12.14.085" data-short-cite="12.14.085" data-history="/BMC/12.14.085/history"><header class=" full-header" data-type="Section" data-search="/BMC/12.14.085/search" data-share="/BMC/12.14.085/share" data-levelnodes-target="header"><h6 class=" inner-header"><span class="num">12.14.085</span> <span class="name">Setbacks&#xA0;&#x2013; Offspring and unit lot setbacks applicable within attached residential zones.</span></h6></header><div class="track-indicator selected d-none">
<span class="glyphicons glyphicons-check" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="text">This section is included in your selections.</span>
</div>




<p class="heading">Lands with a zoning classification of R-M3, R-M4, DC, DT, DN, GDC, or R-AC where offspring lots are created through the subdivision provisions of BMC Title <a class="cite" href="15" title="Title 15&#x2002;Subdivisions">15</a> may apply the following special setbacks to offspring lots within a parent site; provided, however, that setbacks from the exterior lot lines of the parent site shall be consistent with BMC <a class="cite" href="12.14.030(A)" title="12.14.030(A)&#x2002;Development Standards Table">12.14.030(A)</a>, <a class="cite" href="12.14.050" title="12.14.050&#x2002;Setbacks&#xA0;&#x2013; General measurement method">12.14.050</a>, <a class="cite" href="12.14.060" title="12.14.060&#x2002;Setbacks&#xA0;&#x2013; Adjoining alleys">12.14.060</a>, <a class="cite" href="12.14.070" title="12.14.070&#x2002;Setbacks&#xA0;&#x2013; Applying to specific building or use">12.14.070</a>, and <a class="cite" href="12.14.080(B)" title="12.14.080(B)">12.14.080(B)</a>:</p>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.085(A)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.085(A)" data-short-cite="12.14.085(A)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">A.&#x2002;</span></span>Side and rear yard setbacks from offspring lot lines that are not a parent site lot line may be based upon the building separation requirements of the applicable building and fire codes; provided, that zero, common, or shared lot lines may be allowed as set forth within subsection <a class="cite" href="12.14.085(C)" title="12.14.085(C)">C</a> of this section;</p>



</section>
