<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.085(B)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.085(B)" data-short-cite="12.14.085(B)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">B.&#x2002;</span></span>Front yard setbacks from internal private access streets and/or access drives shall be at least five feet or shall be set back from the internal private access street and/or access drive tract or easement line sufficient to provide a straight line length of at least 25 feet from the access point of the garage, carport or parking area to the opposite edge of the private access street and/or access drive tract or easement. No portion of a garage or any garage door which may be in motion may cross any lot line;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.085(C)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.085(C)" data-short-cite="12.14.085(C)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">C.&#x2002;</span></span>Zero, common, or shared lot lines may be permitted subject to the standards of the applicable building and fire codes;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.085(D)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.085(D)" data-short-cite="12.14.085(D)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">D.&#x2002;</span></span>Existing developments comprising detached condominiums or common wall townhouses may utilize the setback provisions of this section if converting to fee simple lots as provided by the subdivision process of BMC Title <a class="cite" href="15" title="Title 15&#x2002;Subdivisions">15</a>; and</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.085(E)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.085(E)" data-short-cite="12.14.085(E)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">E.&#x2002;</span></span>Figure 12.14.085-1 provides a visual example of the parent site and offspring lot line setback requirements as set forth within subsections <a class="cite" href="12.14.085(A)" title="12.14.085(A)">A</a>, <a class="cite" href="12.14.085(B)" title="12.14.085(B)">B</a>, and <a class="cite" href="12.14.085(C)" title="12.14.085(C)">C</a> of this section and Figure 12.14.085-2 provides a visual example of zero, common, or shared offspring lot line requirements as set forth within subsection <a class="cite" href="12.14.085(C)" title="12.14.085(C)">C</a> of this section.</p>



<figure class="type-Figure">
<picture class="role-medium">
<img class="role-medium web-winner" src="media/figure1214085_1.png" data-rank="62" style="width: 480pt;"/>
</picture>
</figure>
<figure class="type-Figure">
<picture class="role-medium">
<img class="role-medium web-winner" src="media/figure1214085_2.png" data-rank="62" style="width: 480pt;"/>
</picture>
</figure>
</section>

<div class="note history history--linked" data-action="click-&gt;levelnodes#openHistory"><p>(Ord. 2445 &#xA7;&#xA0;6 (Exh. F), 2024; Ord. 2123 &#xA7;&#xA0;2 (Exh. B), 2013).</p></div>



</article>
<article class="level6 chunking-small type-Section has-history" id="12.14.090" data-cite="BMC 12.14.090" data-short-cite="12.14.090" data-history="/BMC/12.14.090/history"><header class=" full-header" data-type="Section" data-search="/BMC/12.14.090/search" data-share="/BMC/12.14.090/share" data-levelnodes-target="header"><h6 class=" inner-header"><span class="num">12.14.090</span> <span class="name">Setbacks&#xA0;&#x2013; Projections and facilities allowed.</span></h6></header><div class="track-indicator selected d-none">
<span class="glyphicons glyphicons-check" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="text">This section is included in your selections.</span>
</div>




<p class="heading">Projections and facilities may extend into required setbacks as follows:</p>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.090(A)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.090(A)" data-short-cite="12.14.090(A)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">A.&#x2002;</span></span>Fireplace structures, bay or garden windows, enclosed stair landing, or similar structures may project into any setback, provided such projections are:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.090(A)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.090(A)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.090(A)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span></span>Limited to one per facade;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.090(A)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.090(A)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.090(A)(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span></span>Not wider than 10 feet; and</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.090(A)(3)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.090(A)(3)" data-short-cite="12.14.090(A)(3)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">3.&#x2002;</span></span>Not more than 18 inches into a setback.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.090(B)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.090(B)" data-short-cite="12.14.090(B)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">B.&#x2002;</span></span>Uncovered porches and decks which exceed 18 inches above the finished grade may project:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.090(B)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.090(B)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.090(B)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span></span>Eighteen inches into side and rear yard setbacks; and</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.090(B)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.090(B)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.090(B)(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span></span>Thirty-six inches into the front yard setback.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.090(C)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.090(C)" data-short-cite="12.14.090(C)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">C.&#x2002;</span></span>Uncovered porches and decks not exceeding 18 inches above the finished grade may project to the property line.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.090(D)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.090(D)" data-short-cite="12.14.090(D)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">D.&#x2002;</span></span>Eaves may not project more than:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.090(D)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.090(D)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.090(D)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span></span>Eighteen inches into a side or rear yard setback;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.090(D)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.090(D)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.090(D)(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span></span>Twenty-four inches into a front yard setback; or</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.090(D)(3)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.090(D)(3)" data-short-cite="12.14.090(D)(3)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">3.&#x2002;</span></span>Eighteen inches across a lot line in a zero lot line development.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.090(E)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.090(E)" data-short-cite="12.14.090(E)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">E.&#x2002;</span></span>Fences with a height of six feet or less may project into any setback; and</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.090(F)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.090(F)" data-short-cite="12.14.090(F)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">F.&#x2002;</span></span>Storm water facilities, such as, but not limited to, retention/detention ponds, bioswales, vaults, bioretention or other facilities as required by the Bothell Standards, may be located within any front, rear and side yard setback, provided such facilities are set back from any property line a minimum of five feet. Any portion of a surface water retaining structure that is exposed to the air for a dimension of four feet or more above city-approved finish grade shall be consistent with BMC <a class="cite" href="12.14.152(B)(3)" title="12.14.152(B)(3)">12.14.152(B)(3)</a>. <span class="note history history--linked" data-action="click-&gt;levelnodes#openHistory">(Ord. 2269 &#xA7;&#xA0;5, 2018; Ord. 1815 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 2000; Ord. 1629 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 1996. Formerly 12.14.100).</span></p>



</section>






</article>
<article class="level6 chunking-small type-Section has-history" id="12.14.100" data-cite="BMC 12.14.100" data-short-cite="12.14.100" data-history="/BMC/12.14.100/history"><header class=" full-header" data-type="Section" data-search="/BMC/12.14.100/search" data-share="/BMC/12.14.100/share" data-levelnodes-target="header"><h6 class=" inner-header"><span class="num">12.14.100</span> <span class="name">Setbacks&#xA0;&#x2013; Panhandle lots.</span></h6></header><div class="track-indicator selected d-none">
<span class="glyphicons glyphicons-check" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="text">This section is included in your selections.</span>
</div>




<p>In determining setbacks for a panhandle lot, the handle or access portion of the lot shall not be used to determine building setbacks. Setbacks shall be determined as though no handle was on the lot. <span class="note history history--linked" data-action="click-&gt;levelnodes#openHistory">(Ord. 1815 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 2000; Ord. 1629 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 1996. Formerly 12.14.110).</span></p>





</article>
<article class="level6 chunking-small type-Section has-history" id="12.14.110" data-cite="BMC 12.14.110" data-short-cite="12.14.110" data-history="/BMC/12.14.110/history"><header class=" full-header" data-type="Section" data-search="/BMC/12.14.110/search" data-share="/BMC/12.14.110/share" data-levelnodes-target="header"><h6 class=" inner-header"><span class="num">12.14.110</span> <span class="name">Building height&#xA0;&#x2013; General measurement method.</span></h6></header><div class="track-indicator selected d-none">
<span class="glyphicons glyphicons-check" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="text">This section is included in your selections.</span>
</div>




<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.110(A)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.110(A)" data-short-cite="12.14.110(A)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">A.&#x2002;</span></span>Building height shall be measured vertically from the average city-approved finish grade to the highest point of the structure.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.110(B)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.110(B)" data-short-cite="12.14.110(B)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">B.&#x2002;</span></span>The average city-approved finish grade shall be measured by first delineating the smallest rectangle which can enclose the building and then averaging the existing ground elevations taken at the midpoint of each side of the rectangle. In the event the midpoint of the rectangle drawn is not located on the subject property, the measurement point shall be determined by establishing the midpoint of the property line where it intersects the rectangle.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.110(C)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.110(C)" data-short-cite="12.14.110(C)"><p class="header"><span class="num">C.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Submittal Requirements.</span> </p>



<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.110(C)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.110(C)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.110(C)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span></span>The site plan shall indicate the existing ground elevations and proposed finish grades, as determined by a licensed land surveyor.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.110(C)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.110(C)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.110(C)(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span></span>Building elevations or sections shall be provided for all sides of the building.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.110(D)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.110(D)" data-short-cite="12.14.110(D)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">D.&#x2002;</span></span>Compliance with the height requirement shall be determined by:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.110(D)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.110(D)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.110(D)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span></span>Review of building permit plans, including a site plan indicating existing and proposed finished grades and building footprint, and elevations for each side of the structure; and</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.110(D)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.110(D)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.110(D)(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span></span>Field verification at the completion of building framing. On sloping lots or when the structure is within two feet of the height limit, certification by a licensed surveyor is required. On all other structures, verification shall be provided to the building official as required at the time of the framing inspection. <span class="note history history--linked" data-action="click-&gt;levelnodes#openHistory">(Ord. 1980 &#xA7;&#xA0;2, 2007; Ord. 1815 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 2000; Ord. 1798 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 2000; Ord. 1629 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 1996. Formerly 12.14.120).</span></p>



</section>
</section>







</article>
<article class="level6 chunking-small type-Section has-history" id="12.14.120" data-cite="BMC 12.14.120" data-short-cite="12.14.120" data-history="/BMC/12.14.120/history"><header class=" full-header" data-type="Section" data-search="/BMC/12.14.120/search" data-share="/BMC/12.14.120/share" data-levelnodes-target="header"><h6 class=" inner-header"><span class="num">12.14.120</span> <span class="name">Building height&#xA0;&#x2013; Exceptions to limit.</span></h6></header><div class="track-indicator selected d-none">
<span class="glyphicons glyphicons-check" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="text">This section is included in your selections.</span>
</div>




<p class="heading">The following structures may be erected above the height limits as set forth in BMC <a class="cite" href="12.14.110" title="12.14.110&#x2002;Building height&#xA0;&#x2013; General measurement method">12.14.110</a>:</p>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.120(A)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.120(A)" data-short-cite="12.14.120(A)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">A.&#x2002;</span></span>Roof structures housing or screening ventilating fans, HVAC (heating and cooling equipment), or similar equipment required for building operation and maintenance;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.120(B)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.120(B)" data-short-cite="12.14.120(B)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">B.&#x2002;</span></span>Fire or parapet walls, flagpoles, chimneys, smokestacks, communication transmission and receiving structures, utility line towers and poles, water towers/storage tanks, and similar structures;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.120(C)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.120(C)" data-short-cite="12.14.120(C)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">C.&#x2002;</span></span>On properties with any of the following zoning designations: R-M4, R-AC, OP, NB, CB, CG, or LI, architectural elements including, but not limited to, peaked roofs and steeples; provided, that the increase in height shall not increase usable floor area;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.120(D)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.120(D)" data-short-cite="12.14.120(D)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">D.&#x2002;</span></span>Structures containing certain manufacturing processes within the Canyon Park regional activity center, in accordance with BMC <a class="cite" href="12.48.020" title="12.48.020&#x2002;How the provisions of this chapter are applied">12.48.020</a>, or the North Creek regional activity center, in accordance with BMC <a class="cite" href="12.56.080" title="12.56.080&#x2002;Architectural standards, minimum densities, and floor area ratios">12.56.080</a>;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1 node-repealed" id="12.14.120(E)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.120(E)" data-short-cite="12.14.120(E)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">E.&#x2002;</span></span><span class="placeholder">Repealed.</span></p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.120(F)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.120(F)" data-short-cite="12.14.120(F)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">F.&#x2002;</span></span>Primary and secondary schools in the R-C, R-L1, R-L2, and R-M1 through R-M4 zones, through the conditional use permit process, in accordance with the following:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.120(F)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.120(F)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.120(F)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span></span>The maximum height for school buildings shall be 35 feet except as provided in subsection <a class="cite" href="12.14.120(F)(2)" title="12.14.120(F)(2)">(F)(2)</a> of this section.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.120(F)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.120(F)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.120(F)(2)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span></span>Architectural elements which do not increase usable floor area, including but not limited to peaked roofs, may extend above 35 feet but in no case shall exceed a height of 55 feet, subject to the following:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.120(F)(2)(a)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.120(F)(2)(a)" data-short-cite="12.14.120(F)(2)(a)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span></span>For each one foot of building height over 35 feet, the required building setback shall be increased one foot from street property lines and two feet from interior property lines. Such increased setbacks shall apply only to that portion of the building exceeding 35 feet in height.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.120(F)(2)(b)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.120(F)(2)(b)" data-short-cite="12.14.120(F)(2)(b)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span></span>For each one foot of building height over 35 feet, the required interior lot line landscaping width shall be increased 0.25 foot (three inches) along those interior lot lines from which the portion of the building over 35 feet is visible.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.120(F)(2)(c)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.120(F)(2)(c)" data-short-cite="12.14.120(F)(2)(c)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">c.&#x2002;</span></span>Additional site and building design requirements may be imposed through the conditional use permit process if deemed necessary to mitigate the visual impacts of buildings over the normal height limit and otherwise ensure that such buildings comply with the criteria for approval of conditional use permits, as set forth in BMC <a class="cite" href="12.28.040" title="12.28.040&#x2002;Criteria for approval">12.28.040</a>. <span class="note history history--linked" data-action="click-&gt;levelnodes#openHistory">(Ord. 2445 &#xA7;&#xA0;6 (Exh. F), 2024; Ord. 2025 &#xA7;&#xA0;2 (Exh. C), 2009; Ord. 1946 &#xA7;&#xA0;2, 2005; Ord. 1922 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 2004; Ord. 1876 &#xA7;&#xA0;2, 2002; Ord. 1815 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 2000; Ord. 1629 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 1996. Formerly 12.14.130).</span></p>



</section>
</section>
</section>










</article>
<article class="level6 chunking-small type-Section has-history" id="12.14.130" data-cite="BMC 12.14.130" data-short-cite="12.14.130" data-history="/BMC/12.14.130/history"><header class=" full-header" data-type="Section" data-search="/BMC/12.14.130/search" data-share="/BMC/12.14.130/share" data-levelnodes-target="header"><h6 class=" inner-header"><span class="num">12.14.130</span> <span class="name">Building limitations&#xA0;&#x2013; Accessory buildings and structures.</span></h6></header><div class="track-indicator selected d-none">
<span class="glyphicons glyphicons-check" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="text">This section is included in your selections.</span>
</div>




<p>Accessory buildings and structures within residential zone classifications, including but not limited to storage buildings and detached private garages, shall maintain the same residential character of the primary structure, shall cover a maximum of five percent of the lot area, and shall have an exterior height no greater than 20 feet or the height of the primary building or structure, whichever is less, except as provided for detached accessory dwelling units in BMC <a class="cite" href="12.14.135" title="12.14.135&#x2002;Accessory dwelling units">12.14.135</a>.</p>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.130(A)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.130(A)" data-short-cite="12.14.130(A)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">A.&#x2002;</span></span>Accessory buildings shall utilize similar siding, roofing and detailing as the primary structure, and be of a style that complements the primary structure. Metal buildings that are clearly of a different style than the primary structure shall not be allowed for accessory buildings over 120 square feet.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.130(B)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.130(B)" data-short-cite="12.14.130(B)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">B.&#x2002;</span></span>Accessory buildings with a footprint greater than 1,200 square feet shall be set back from the required setback line an additional foot for each 100 square feet of area above 1,200 square feet. A minimum 10-foot-wide Type II landscape buffer shall be installed between any accessory building with a footprint greater than 1,200 square feet and any property line within 50 feet of that structure.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.130(C)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.130(C)" data-short-cite="12.14.130(C)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">C.&#x2002;</span></span>Accessory buildings shall have a maximum height of 10 feet at the five-foot accessory building rear setback line, increasing by one foot for each additional foot of setback to the maximum height allowed for accessory buildings. <span class="note history history--linked" data-action="click-&gt;levelnodes#openHistory">(Ord. 2258 &#xA7;&#xA0;4, 2018; Ord. 1995 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 2008; Ord. 1815 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 2000; Ord. 1629 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 1996. Formerly 12.14.140).</span></p>



</section>







</article>
<article class="level6 chunking-small type-Section has-history" id="12.14.133" data-cite="BMC 12.14.133" data-short-cite="12.14.133" data-history="/BMC/12.14.133/history"><header class=" full-header" data-type="Section" data-search="/BMC/12.14.133/search" data-share="/BMC/12.14.133/share" data-levelnodes-target="header"><h6 class=" inner-header"><span class="num">12.14.133</span> <span class="name">Floor area ratios.</span></h6></header><div class="track-indicator selected d-none">
<span class="glyphicons glyphicons-check" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="text">This section is included in your selections.</span>
</div>




<p>Floor area ratio (FAR) is a mechanism for establishing a relationship between the floor area of a building and the size of the parcel upon which the building is located.</p>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.133(A)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.133(A)" data-short-cite="12.14.133(A)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">A.&#x2002;</span></span>FAR is calculated using the following method:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.133(A)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.133(A)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.133(A)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span></span>First, determine the net buildable area of the site.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.133(A)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.133(A)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.133(A)(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span></span>Second, determine the total square footage of all enclosed and temperature-controlled portions of the proposed, existing or any combination thereof of building(s) located on the subject parcel. Square footage within the building(s) dedicated for parking, parking garages, drive aisles, and interior recreation purposes consistent with BMC <a class="cite" href="12.20.020" title="12.20.020&#x2002;Minimum area required">12.20.020</a> are exempt from the FAR ratio.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.133(A)(3)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.133(A)(3)" data-short-cite="12.14.133(A)(3)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">3.&#x2002;</span></span>Finally, divide the floor area by the net buildable site area using similar units of measure to derive the floor area ratio.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.133(A)(4)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.133(A)(4)" data-short-cite="12.14.133(A)(4)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">4.&#x2002;</span></span>For example, a 10,000-square-foot parcel within an activity center that requires a minimum FAR of 0.5 establishes that the minimum square footage of temperature-controlled and enclosed building area must be at least 5,000 square feet.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.133(B)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.133(B)" data-short-cite="12.14.133(B)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">B.&#x2002;</span></span>Visual examples of FAR:</p>



<figure class="type-Figure">
<figcaption><p class="header"><span class="name">Figure 12.14-133-A</span></p>


</figcaption>
<picture class="role-medium">
<img class="role-medium web-winner" src="media/12.14.133_1.png" data-rank="62" style="width: 429pt;"/>
</picture>
</figure>
<figure class="type-Figure">
<figcaption><p class="header"><span class="name">Figure 12.14-133-B</span></p>


</figcaption>
<picture class="role-medium">
<img class="role-medium web-winner" src="media/12.14.133_2.png" data-rank="62" style="width: 470pt;"/>
</picture>
</figure>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.133(C)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.133(C)" data-short-cite="12.14.133(C)"><p><span class="header"><span class="num">C.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">FAR Transference.</span> </span>Square footage of building used to satisfy the floor area ratio may be located anywhere within the subject property and may further be transferred across contiguous or noncontinuous parcels provided the parcels are included as part of an overall development, the FAR transfer runs with the land in perpetuity, and transfer documents shall be recorded with the applicable county. This provision does not relieve an applicant from compliance with all other applicable provisions of the code.</p>




</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.133(D)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.133(D)" data-short-cite="12.14.133(D)"><p><span class="header"><span class="num">D.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Mixed-Use Developments.</span> </span>Where residential and nonresidential uses are combined within one or more buildings on a single parcel or development area, the mix of minimum residential density and nonresidential floor area ratio shall be established within the subarea regulations. <span class="note history history--linked" data-action="click-&gt;levelnodes#openHistory">(Ord. 2457 &#xA7;&#xA0;3 (Exh. C), 2025; Ord. 2353 &#xA7;&#xA0;8, 2021).</span></p>




</section>




</article>
<article class="level6 chunking-small type-Section has-history" id="12.14.134" data-cite="BMC 12.14.134" data-short-cite="12.14.134" data-history="/BMC/12.14.134/history"><header class=" full-header" data-type="Section" data-search="/BMC/12.14.134/search" data-share="/BMC/12.14.134/share" data-levelnodes-target="header"><h6 class=" inner-header"><span class="num">12.14.134</span> <span class="name">Middle housing.</span></h6></header><div class="track-indicator selected d-none">
<span class="glyphicons glyphicons-check" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="text">This section is included in your selections.</span>
</div>




<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.134(A)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.134(A)" data-short-cite="12.14.134(A)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">A.&#x2002;</span></span>Middle housing is allowed in residential zones pursuant to BMC <a class="cite" href="12.06.140" title="12.06.140&#x2002;Residential uses">12.06.140</a>. Middle housing projects shall comply with the design requirements in this section.</p>



<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.134(A)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.134(A)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.134(A)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span></span>All middle housing units directly abutting a public street shall have entries and windows facing the street.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.134(A)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.134(A)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.134(A)(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span></span>Where alleys are available, vehicular access for all middle housing units shall be provided from the alley, unless alley access is demonstrated to be infeasible and alternate access is approved by the public works director as a deviation.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.134(B)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.134(B)" data-short-cite="12.14.134(B)"><p class="header"><span class="num">B.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Cottage Housing.</span> </p>



<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.134(B)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.134(B)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.134(B)(1)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span></span>Cottage housing projects shall meet the following site layout requirements:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.134(B)(1)(a)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.134(B)(1)(a)" data-short-cite="12.14.134(B)(1)(a)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span></span>Maximum lot sizes shall not apply to cottage housing developments.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.134(B)(1)(b)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.134(B)(1)(b)" data-short-cite="12.14.134(B)(1)(b)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span></span>Cottages shall be separated by a minimum distance of six feet.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.134(B)(1)(c)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.134(B)(1)(c)" data-short-cite="12.14.134(B)(1)(c)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">c.&#x2002;</span></span>Cottages shall be oriented to a shared open space and shall be connected to the shared open space by a pedestrian path. Cottages within 20 feet of a street property line shall be oriented toward the street.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.134(B)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.134(B)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.134(B)(2)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span></span>Shared open space owned in common shall be provided for cottage housing as follows:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.134(B)(2)(a)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.134(B)(2)(a)" data-short-cite="12.14.134(B)(2)(a)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span></span>A minimum of 150 square feet per cottage of shared open space is required. The shared open space shall be one contiguous area unless infeasible due to space constraints, as approved by the community development director. Parking areas, driveways, setback areas, and private open space do not qualify as shared open space. Storm water management facilities do not qualify as shared open space unless they are low impact development features that do not adversely impact access to or use of the shared open space for typical use by residents. The shared open space must be a minimum of 15 feet wide in any dimension.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.134(B)(2)(b)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.134(B)(2)(b)" data-short-cite="12.14.134(B)(2)(b)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span></span>Land located between dwelling units and an abutting right-of-way or access easement greater than 21 feet in width shall not serve as required common open space, unless the area is reserved as a separate tract, and does not contain pathways leading to individual units or other elements that detract from its appearance and function as a shared space for all residents.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.134(B)(2)(c)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.134(B)(2)(c)" data-short-cite="12.14.134(B)(2)(c)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">c.&#x2002;</span></span>At least 50 percent of the cottages shall abut the shared open space. All cottages shall be within 60 feet walking distance measured from the nearest entrance of the cottage along the shortest accessible walking route to the nearest point of the shared open space.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.134(B)(2)(d)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.134(B)(2)(d)" data-short-cite="12.14.134(B)(2)(d)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">d.&#x2002;</span></span>Shared open space shall have cottages on at least two sides.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.134(B)(2)(e)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.134(B)(2)(e)" data-short-cite="12.14.134(B)(2)(e)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">e.&#x2002;</span></span>The shared open space shall be developed in accordance with Chapter <a class="cite" href="12.18" title="Ch. 12.18&#x2002;Tree Retention and Landscaping">12.18</a> BMC and shall include a mix of landscaping, lawn area, pedestrian paths, and/or paved courtyard area, and may also include recreational amenities. Impervious surfaces shall not exceed 75 percent of the total shared open space.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.134(B)(3)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.134(B)(3)" data-short-cite="12.14.134(B)(3)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">3.&#x2002;</span></span>Private open space shall be provided for cottage housing as follows:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.134(B)(3)(a)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.134(B)(3)(a)" data-short-cite="12.14.134(B)(3)(a)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span></span>Each cottage shall be provided with a minimum of 200 square feet of usable private open space that is clearly separated from the shared open space.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.134(B)(3)(b)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.134(B)(3)(b)" data-short-cite="12.14.134(B)(3)(b)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span></span>No dimension of the private open space shall be less than six feet.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.134(B)(4)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.134(B)(4)" data-short-cite="12.14.134(B)(4)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">4.&#x2002;</span></span>Parking shall meet the following requirements:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.134(B)(4)(a)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.134(B)(4)(a)" data-short-cite="12.14.134(B)(4)(a)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span></span>Parking shall be located on the same property as the cottage housing. Parking can be consolidated into one or more off-street parking areas. Off-street parking areas shall be located to the side or rear of the cottage housing development. Off-street parking areas shall not be located between the cottage housing development and the primary street frontage.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.134(B)(4)(b)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.134(B)(4)(b)" data-short-cite="12.14.134(B)(4)(b)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span></span>Off-street parking may be located in or under an accessory parking structure (such as a single or multi-auto carport or garage), but such structures shall not be attached to individual cottages. Uncovered parking is also permitted.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.134(B)(4)(c)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.134(B)(4)(c)" data-short-cite="12.14.134(B)(4)(c)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">c.&#x2002;</span></span>Off-street parking shall be screened from direct street view by garage doors, six-foot-high solid sight-obscuring fencing, and/or Type III landscaping that is at least five feet wide.</p>



</section>
</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.134(C)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.134(C)" data-short-cite="12.14.134(C)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">C.&#x2002;</span></span>Courtyard housing projects shall meet the following site layout requirements:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.134(C)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.134(C)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.134(C)(1)"><p><span class="header"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Number of Dwelling Units.</span> </span>A single courtyard housing development shall include a maximum of eight units, except in R-M3 zones and denser.</p>




</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.134(C)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.134(C)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.134(C)(2)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span></span>Courtyard housing projects shall meet the following site layout requirements:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.134(C)(2)(a)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.134(C)(2)(a)" data-short-cite="12.14.134(C)(2)(a)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span></span>Lots developed with courtyard housing shall meet the average lot size requirements of the zone.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.134(C)(2)(b)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.134(C)(2)(b)" data-short-cite="12.14.134(C)(2)(b)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span></span>The main entry to each ground floor dwelling unit shall be directly off a common courtyard or directly from a street. Access to upper-level dwelling units may be through an open or roofed stair.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.134(C)(2)(c)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.134(C)(2)(c)" data-short-cite="12.14.134(C)(2)(c)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">c.&#x2002;</span></span>Clear and obvious pedestrian access and visual access between the sidewalk shall be provided.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.134(C)(3)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.134(C)(3)" data-short-cite="12.14.134(C)(3)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">3.&#x2002;</span></span>Courtyard housing shall be in a U- or L-shaped configuration where the ends facing the street have the dimensions no larger than a single-family detached structure on the combined lots or adjacent lots on the block-face.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.134(C)(4)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.134(C)(4)" data-short-cite="12.14.134(C)(4)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">4.&#x2002;</span></span>Shared open space shall be provided for courtyard housing as follows:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.134(C)(4)(a)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.134(C)(4)(a)" data-short-cite="12.14.134(C)(4)(a)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span></span>A minimum of 200 square feet per dwelling unit of shared open space is required. At least 800 square feet of shared open space shall be provided for each courtyard housing development. Parking areas, driveways, setback areas, and private open space do not qualify as shared open space. Storm water management facilities do not qualify as shared open space unless they are low impact development features that do not adversely impact access to or use of the shared open space for typical use by residents. The shared open space must be a minimum of 15 feet wide in any dimension.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.134(C)(4)(b)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.134(C)(4)(b)" data-short-cite="12.14.134(C)(4)(b)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span></span>Fences are not allowed in any shared open space areas.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.134(C)(4)(c)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.134(C)(4)(c)" data-short-cite="12.14.134(C)(4)(c)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">c.&#x2002;</span></span>The shared open space shall be developed with a mix of landscaping, lawn area, pedestrian paths, and/or paved courtyard area, and may also include recreational amenities. Impervious surfaces shall not exceed 75 percent of the total shared open space.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.134(C)(5)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.134(C)(5)" data-short-cite="12.14.134(C)(5)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">5.&#x2002;</span></span>Private open space shall be provided for courtyard housing as follows:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.134(C)(5)(a)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.134(C)(5)(a)" data-short-cite="12.14.134(C)(5)(a)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span></span>Each dwelling unit shall be provided with a minimum of 60 square feet of usable private open space in the form of a private yard, porch, patio, balcony, or similar configuration.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.134(C)(5)(b)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.134(C)(5)(b)" data-short-cite="12.14.134(C)(5)(b)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span></span>No dimension of the private open space shall be less than six feet.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.134(C)(6)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.134(C)(6)" data-short-cite="12.14.134(C)(6)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">6.&#x2002;</span></span>Parking shall meet the following requirements:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.134(C)(6)(a)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.134(C)(6)(a)" data-short-cite="12.14.134(C)(6)(a)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span></span>Parking shall be located on the same property as the courtyard housing development. Parking can be consolidated into one or more off-street parking areas. Off-street parking areas shall be located to the side or rear of the courtyard housing development. Off-street parking areas shall not be located between the courtyard housing development and the primary street frontage.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.134(C)(6)(b)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.134(C)(6)(b)" data-short-cite="12.14.134(C)(6)(b)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span></span>Off-street parking may be located in or under an accessory parking structure (such as a single or multi-auto carport or garage), but such structures shall not be attached to individual units. Uncovered parking is also permitted.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.134(C)(6)(c)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.134(C)(6)(c)" data-short-cite="12.14.134(C)(6)(c)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">c.&#x2002;</span></span>Off-street parking shall be screened from direct street view by garage doors, solid sight-obscuring fencing, and/or Type III landscaping that is five feet wide. <span class="note history history--linked" data-action="click-&gt;levelnodes#openHistory">(Ord. 2461 &#xA7;&#xA0;1 (Exh. A), 2025; Ord. 2445 &#xA7;&#xA0;6 (Exh. F), 2024; Ord. 2415 &#xA7;&#xA0;5 (Exh. D), 2024).</span></p>



</section>
</section>
</section>





</article>
<article class="level6 chunking-small type-Section has-history" id="12.14.135" data-cite="BMC 12.14.135" data-short-cite="12.14.135" data-history="/BMC/12.14.135/history"><header class=" full-header" data-type="Section" data-search="/BMC/12.14.135/search" data-share="/BMC/12.14.135/share" data-levelnodes-target="header"><h6 class=" inner-header"><span class="num">12.14.135</span> <span class="name">Accessory dwelling units.</span></h6></header><div class="track-indicator selected d-none">
<span class="glyphicons glyphicons-check" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="text">This section is included in your selections.</span>
</div>




<p class="heading">Accessory dwelling units are permitted subject to the following requirements:</p>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.135(A)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.135(A)" data-short-cite="12.14.135(A)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">A.&#x2002;</span></span>Two accessory dwelling units are allowed per lot.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.135(B)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.135(B)" data-short-cite="12.14.135(B)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">B.&#x2002;</span></span>One accessory dwelling unit in any configuration is allowed on a lot developed with middle housing.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.135(C)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.135(C)" data-short-cite="12.14.135(C)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">C.&#x2002;</span></span>One accessory dwelling unit is allowed on lots designated with critical areas or their buffers.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.135(D)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.135(D)" data-short-cite="12.14.135(D)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">D.&#x2002;</span></span>The size of an accessory dwelling unit shall be limited as follows:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.135(D)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.135(D)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.135(D)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span></span>An accessory dwelling unit may be developed up to 1,200 square feet. Conversion of an existing space to an accessory dwelling unit may exceed 1,200 square feet as approved by the community development director, provided this does not exceed 40 percent of the primary dwelling unit&#x2019;s gross floor area.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.135(D)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.135(D)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.135(D)(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span></span>Detached accessory dwelling units are exempt from the provisions of BMC <a class="cite" href="12.14.130" title="12.14.130&#x2002;Building limitations&#xA0;&#x2013; Accessory buildings and structures">12.14.130</a> that limit the size of accessory buildings to five percent of the lot area.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.135(E)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.135(E)" data-short-cite="12.14.135(E)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">E.&#x2002;</span></span>Detached accessory dwelling units shall have a height no greater than 30 feet or no greater than 33 feet for units over an existing accessory structure.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.135(F)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.135(F)" data-short-cite="12.14.135(F)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">F.&#x2002;</span></span>For existing accessory dwelling units that were created without an approved application, the property owner must apply to the city for approval. If an accessory dwelling unit was created without a building permit, the city shall require a building inspection to determine whether the structure is sound, will not pose a hazard to people or property, and meets the requirements of this section and BMC Title <a class="cite" href="20" title="Title 20&#x2002;Buildings and Construction">20</a>. The accessory dwelling unit application fee will cover the building inspection of the accessory dwelling unit. Permits and inspections may be required for existing attached and detached ADUs per the building official.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.135(G)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.135(G)" data-short-cite="12.14.135(G)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">G.&#x2002;</span></span>The city will not assess impact fees on the construction of accessory dwelling units that are greater than 50 percent of the impact fees that would be imposed on the principal unit. <span class="note history history--linked" data-action="click-&gt;levelnodes#openHistory">(Ord. 2461 &#xA7;&#xA0;1 (Exh. A), 2025; Ord. 2445 &#xA7;&#xA0;6 (Exh. F), 2024; Ord. 2415 &#xA7;&#xA0;5 (Exh. D), 2024; Ord. 2277 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 2019; Ord. 2258 &#xA7;&#xA0;5, 2018).</span></p>



</section>







</article>
<article class="level6 chunking-small type-Section has-history" id="12.14.140" data-cite="BMC 12.14.140" data-short-cite="12.14.140" data-history="/BMC/12.14.140/history"><header class=" full-header" data-type="Section" data-search="/BMC/12.14.140/search" data-share="/BMC/12.14.140/share" data-levelnodes-target="header"><h6 class=" inner-header"><span class="num">12.14.140</span> <span class="name">Building and hard surface coverage.</span></h6></header><div class="track-indicator selected d-none">
<span class="glyphicons glyphicons-check" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="text">This section is included in your selections.</span>
</div>




<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.140(A)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.140(A)" data-short-cite="12.14.140(A)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">A.&#x2002;</span></span>Building coverage shall mean that portion of a lot that is occupied by the principal building and its accessory buildings, including all projections except eaves. Decks are exempted from the building coverage provisions. Building coverage shall be expressed as a percentage of the total lot area.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.140(B)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.140(B)" data-short-cite="12.14.140(B)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">B.&#x2002;</span></span>Hard surface coverage shall mean the ground areas of the entire development site covered by any impervious surface, permeable pavement, or vegetated roof, determined by extending a vertical projection to the ground from the widest points of any hard surface, and expressed as a percentage of total lot area. Permeable pavement and vegetated roofs shall count only 50 percent of their site area as hard surface for the purposes calculating coverage in the Development Standards Tables in BMC <a class="cite" href="12.14.030" title="12.14.030&#x2002;Residential area and dimensions">12.14.030</a> and <a class="cite" href="12.14.040" title="12.14.040&#x2002;Commercial and industrial dimensions">12.14.040</a>. <span class="note history history--linked" data-action="click-&gt;levelnodes#openHistory">(Ord. 2200 &#xA7;&#xA0;2 (Exh. B), 2016; Ord. 1815 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 2000; Ord. 1629 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 1996. Formerly 12.14.150).</span></p>



</section>






</article>
<article class="level6 chunking-small type-Section has-history" id="12.14.150" data-cite="BMC 12.14.150" data-short-cite="12.14.150" data-history="/BMC/12.14.150/history"><header class=" full-header" data-type="Section" data-search="/BMC/12.14.150/search" data-share="/BMC/12.14.150/share" data-levelnodes-target="header"><h6 class=" inner-header"><span class="num">12.14.150</span> <span class="name">Fences and freestanding walls.</span></h6></header><div class="track-indicator selected d-none">
<span class="glyphicons glyphicons-check" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="text">This section is included in your selections.</span>
</div>




<p class="heading">Fences and freestanding walls may be erected in any zone district as provided below:</p>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.150(A)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.150(A)" data-short-cite="12.14.150(A)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">A.&#x2002;</span></span>Fences and/or freestanding walls six feet or less in nominal height shall be consistent with the following:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.150(A)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.150(A)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.150(A)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span></span>Shall be placed behind any landscape buffer required by Chapter <a class="cite" href="12.18" title="Ch. 12.18&#x2002;Tree Retention and Landscaping">12.18</a> BMC.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.150(A)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.150(A)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.150(A)(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span></span>Where no landscape buffer is required, fences and freestanding walls may be allowed without a setback within any front, rear or side yard.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.150(A)(3)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.150(A)(3)" data-short-cite="12.14.150(A)(3)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">3.&#x2002;</span></span>Fence and freestanding wall height shall be measured from the horizontal projection of the predominant ground level of either the city-approved finish grade where such grade has been established, or from the horizontal projection of the predominant existing grade in the vicinity of the fence.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.150(A)(4)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.150(A)(4)" data-short-cite="12.14.150(A)(4)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">4.&#x2002;</span></span>Fence and freestanding wall height shall be measured to the upper surface of the fence or freestanding wall panel.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.150(A)(5)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.150(A)(5)" data-short-cite="12.14.150(A)(5)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">5.&#x2002;</span></span>Where the International Residential Code (IRC) or the International Building Code (IBC) requires a guard on top of a retaining wall or retaining structure, any fence or freestanding wall so located shall meet the minimum guard height and construction requirements of the IRC or IBC, as applicable.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.150(A)(6)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.150(A)(6)" data-short-cite="12.14.150(A)(6)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">6.&#x2002;</span></span>No fence or freestanding wall shall be placed closer than three feet to or may obscure the vision of any fire hydrant.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.150(A)(7)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.150(A)(7)" data-short-cite="12.14.150(A)(7)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">7.&#x2002;</span></span>All fences and freestanding walls shall comply with the sight distance requirements of the Bothell Standards.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.150(B)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.150(B)" data-short-cite="12.14.150(B)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">B.&#x2002;</span></span>Fences and freestanding walls that exceed a nominal height of six feet shall comply with any required front, side, and rear yard setbacks.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.150(C)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.150(C)" data-short-cite="12.14.150(C)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">C.&#x2002;</span></span>Electric fences shall:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.150(C)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.150(C)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.150(C)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span></span>Be permitted only within the residential zone classifications and only for the raising and keeping of animals for agricultural purposes or pasturing;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.150(C)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.150(C)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.150(C)(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span></span>Be posted with permanent signs a minimum of two square feet in area at 50-foot intervals stating that the fence is electrified;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.150(C)(3)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.150(C)(3)" data-short-cite="12.14.150(C)(3)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">3.&#x2002;</span></span>Be placed no closer than six feet to any fire hydrant.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.150(D)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.150(D)" data-short-cite="12.14.150(D)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">D.&#x2002;</span></span>Barbed wire fencing is permitted in association with animal husbandry or pasturing and for security purposes to protect sensitive locations or facilities as identified by the chief of police.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.150(E)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.150(E)" data-short-cite="12.14.150(E)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">E.&#x2002;</span></span>Razor wire fencing is prohibited; provided, however, that military or police services may install razor wire to protect sensitive locations or facilities as identified by the chief of police. <span class="note history history--linked" data-action="click-&gt;levelnodes#openHistory">(Ord. 2445 &#xA7;&#xA0;6 (Exh. F), 2024; Ord. 2269 &#xA7;&#xA0;6, 2018; Ord. 1997 &#xA7;&#xA0;2, 2008; Ord. 1946 &#xA7;&#xA0;2, 2005; Ord. 1815 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 2000; Ord. 1629 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 1996. Formerly 12.14.160).</span></p>



</section>









</article>
<article class="level6 chunking-small type-Section has-history" id="12.14.152" data-cite="BMC 12.14.152" data-short-cite="12.14.152" data-history="/BMC/12.14.152/history"><header class=" full-header" data-type="Section" data-search="/BMC/12.14.152/search" data-share="/BMC/12.14.152/share" data-levelnodes-target="header"><h6 class=" inner-header"><span class="num">12.14.152</span> <span class="name">Retaining walls, rockeries, and other retaining structures.</span></h6></header><div class="track-indicator selected d-none">
<span class="glyphicons glyphicons-check" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="text">This section is included in your selections.</span>
</div>




<p class="heading">Retaining walls, rockeries, modular block walls, rock walls, or any other structures which retain soil, retain earth surcharge, protect an exposed soil face, or serve as a gravity retaining wall, shall, for the purposes of this chapter, be known as retaining structures. Retaining structures shall be consistent with the following:</p>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.152(A)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.152(A)" data-short-cite="12.14.152(A)"><p><span class="header"><span class="num">A.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Height Measurement.</span> </span>The height of a retaining structure shall be measured from the point at which the ground elevation of the city-approved finish grade intersects with the retaining structure to the highest point of the retaining structure as illustrated in Figure 12.14.152-1.</p>




<figure class="type-Figure">
<figcaption><p class="header"><span class="name">Figure 12.14.152-1</span></p>


</figcaption>
<picture class="role-medium">
<img class="role-medium web-winner" src="media/12.14.152-1.180119.png" data-rank="62" style="width: 297pt;"/>
</picture>
</figure>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.152(B)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.152(B)" data-short-cite="12.14.152(B)"><p class="heading"><span class="header"><span class="num">B.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Outward Facing Retaining Structures.</span> </span>The following section regulates retaining structures that face outward from a property and are primarily and directly visible to adjacent properties:</p>




<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.152(B)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.152(B)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.152(B)(1)"><p class="header"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Retaining Structure Location, Maximum Heights and Minimum Separation within Setbacks.</span> </p>



<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.152(B)(1)(a)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.152(B)(1)(a)" data-short-cite="12.14.152(B)(1)(a)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span></span>All retaining structures, including footings or foundations, shall be set back at least one foot or a horizontal distance equal to the height of the structure, whichever is greater, from any property or right-of-way line, unless the applicant provides a recorded easement from the adjacent property owner or owners to allow either all or a portion of the retaining structure to be on or closer to an adjacent property.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.152(B)(1)(b)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.152(B)(1)(b)" data-short-cite="12.14.152(B)(1)(b)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span></span>Within rear and front yard setback areas, retaining structures shall not be higher than four feet. Two or more up to four-foot-high retaining structures may be permitted within rear or front yard setbacks, provided the retaining structures are separated by a distance that is equal to two times the height of the structure as illustrated in Figures 12.14.152-2 and 12.14.152-3.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.152(B)(1)(c)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.152(B)(1)(c)" data-short-cite="12.14.152(B)(1)(c)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">c.&#x2002;</span></span>The maximum slope gradient allowed between retaining structures shall be a four-foot horizontal to a one-foot vertical (4H:1V) slope.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.152(B)(1)(d)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.152(B)(1)(d)" data-short-cite="12.14.152(B)(1)(d)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">d.&#x2002;</span></span>Within side yard setback areas, retaining structures shall not be higher than six feet as illustrated in Figure 12.14.152-2.</p>



<figure class="type-Figure">
<figcaption><p class="header"><span class="name">Figure 12.14.152-2</span></p>


</figcaption>
<picture class="role-medium">
<img class="role-medium web-winner" src="media/12.14.152-2.180119.png" data-rank="62" style="width: 315pt;"/>
</picture>
</figure>
<figure class="type-Figure">
<figcaption><p class="header"><span class="name">Figure 12.14.152-3</span></p>


</figcaption>
<picture class="role-medium">
<img class="role-medium web-winner" src="media/12.14.152-3.180119.png" data-rank="62" style="width: 480pt;"/>
</picture>
</figure>
<figure class="type-Figure">
<figcaption><p class="header"><span class="name">Figure 12.14.152-4</span></p>


</figcaption>
<picture class="role-medium">
<img class="role-medium web-winner" src="media/12.14.152-4.180119.jpg" data-rank="61" style="width: 291pt;"/>
</picture>
</figure>
</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.152(B)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.152(B)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.152(B)(2)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span></span>Retaining structures located outside the setback or yard area shall be consistent with the following:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.152(B)(2)(a)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.152(B)(2)(a)" data-short-cite="12.14.152(B)(2)(a)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span></span>Retaining structures shall have a maximum height of 12 feet;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.152(B)(2)(b)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.152(B)(2)(b)" data-short-cite="12.14.152(B)(2)(b)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span></span>All retaining structures shall be separated by a distance that is equal to the height of the downslope retaining structure; and</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.152(B)(2)(c)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.152(B)(2)(c)" data-short-cite="12.14.152(B)(2)(c)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">c.&#x2002;</span></span>A maximum slope gradient of four feet horizontal to one-foot vertical (4H:1V) shall be placed between retaining structures;</p>



<p>These provisions are illustrated in Figure 12.14.152-5 below.</p>
<figure class="type-Figure">
<figcaption><p class="header"><span class="name">Figure 12.14.152-5</span></p>


</figcaption>
<picture class="role-medium">
<img class="role-medium web-winner" src="media/12.14.152-5.180119.png" data-rank="62" style="width: 438pt;"/>
</picture>
</figure>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.152(B)(2)(d)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.152(B)(2)(d)" data-short-cite="12.14.152(B)(2)(d)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">d.&#x2002;</span></span>The community development director, in consultation with the public works director, may waive or reduce the wall separation distance, may increase the maximum allowed slope gradient between retaining structures up to a three-to-one slope (3H:1V), and may increase the allowed maximum height of a retaining structure if the applicant demonstrates the reduced separation distance and/or increased gradient and/or wall height is necessary to:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec4 chunking-none type-Subsec4" id="12.14.152(B)(2)(d)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.152(B)(2)(d)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.152(B)(2)(d)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(1)&#x2002;</span></span>Reduce potential adverse impacts to a critical area(s) or buffer(s) as defined by Chapter <a class="cite" href="14.04" title="Ch. 14.04&#x2002;Critical Area Regulations">14.04</a> BMC; or</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec4 chunking-none type-Subsec4" id="12.14.152(B)(2)(d)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.152(B)(2)(d)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.152(B)(2)(d)(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(2)&#x2002;</span></span>Retain a greater number or diameter inches of significant trees; or</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec4 chunking-none type-Subsec4" id="12.14.152(B)(2)(d)(3)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.152(B)(2)(d)(3)" data-short-cite="12.14.152(B)(2)(d)(3)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(3)&#x2002;</span></span>Permit the installation of transportation improvements; or</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec4 chunking-none type-Subsec4" id="12.14.152(B)(2)(d)(4)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.152(B)(2)(d)(4)" data-short-cite="12.14.152(B)(2)(d)(4)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(4)&#x2002;</span></span>The alternative separation, slope gradient, or height is in the public interest.</p>



</section>
</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.152(B)(3)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.152(B)(3)" data-short-cite="12.14.152(B)(3)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">3.&#x2002;</span></span>All retaining structures, building or structure foundation walls or the walls of surface water vaults or facilities that are visible from adjacent public rights-of-way or residential properties shall apply the following:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.152(B)(3)(a)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.152(B)(3)(a)" data-short-cite="12.14.152(B)(3)(a)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span></span>All retaining structures four or more feet in height shall be constructed of or faced with brick, stone, split-face or fluted concrete block, textured poured-in-place concrete, or other materials with texture to reduce the apparent mass of the wall, as illustrated in Figure 12.14.152-6;</p>



<figure class="type-Figure">
<figcaption><p class="header"><span class="name">Figure 12.14.152-6</span></p>


</figcaption>
<picture class="role-medium">
<img class="role-medium web-winner" src="media/12146.png" data-rank="62" style="width: 480pt;"/>
</picture>
</figure>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.152(B)(3)(b)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.152(B)(3)(b)" data-short-cite="12.14.152(B)(3)(b)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span></span>All retaining structures greater than six feet in height shall be installed with hanging and/or climbing vegetation above or below the wall, and/or shall be installed with a bas-relief sculpture or other art forms as approved by the community development director, which sculpture or art shall be incorporated on or into the face of the wall, as illustrated in Figure 12.14.152-7.</p>



<figure class="type-Figure">
<figcaption><p class="header"><span class="name">Figure 12.14.152-7</span></p>


</figcaption>
<picture class="role-medium">
<img class="role-medium web-winner" src="media/12.14.152-7.180119.png" data-rank="62" style="width: 399pt;"/>
</picture>
</figure>
</section>
</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.152(C)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.152(C)" data-short-cite="12.14.152(C)"><p class="heading"><span class="header"><span class="num">C.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Inward Facing Retaining Structures.</span> </span>The following subsection regulates retaining structures that face into a development site and are primarily visible to the tenants and users of that development:</p>




<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.152(C)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.152(C)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.152(C)(1)"><p class="header"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Retaining Structure Location, Maximum Heights and Minimum Separation.</span> </p>



<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.152(C)(1)(a)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.152(C)(1)(a)" data-short-cite="12.14.152(C)(1)(a)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span></span>Retaining structures, including footings, foundations and tie-backs or other structural anchors, shall be set back at least one foot from any property or right-of-way line or a horizontal distance equal to the height of the structure, whichever is greater, unless the applicant provides a recorded easement from the adjacent property owner or owners to allow either all or a portion of the retaining structure to be on or closer to an adjacent property (see Figure 12.14.152-8).</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.152(C)(1)(b)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.152(C)(1)(b)" data-short-cite="12.14.152(C)(1)(b)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span></span>Engineered retaining structures may be built one foot from the property line regardless of the height of the structure as long as the horizontal distance to any structures on the adjacent property is equal to the height of the structure (see Figure 12.14.152-9).</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.152(C)(1)(c)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.152(C)(1)(c)" data-short-cite="12.14.152(C)(1)(c)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">c.&#x2002;</span></span>Maximum exposed height shall be 12 feet unless mitigated by measures described in subsections <a class="cite" href="12.14.152(B)(2)(d)" title="12.14.152(B)(2)(d)">(B)(2)(d)</a> and/or <a class="cite" href="12.14.152(B)(3)" title="12.14.152(B)(3)">(B)(3)</a> of this section.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.152(C)(1)(d)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.152(C)(1)(d)" data-short-cite="12.14.152(C)(1)(d)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">d.&#x2002;</span></span>Height of exposed walls may also be mitigated through the use of one or more planters designed to accommodate minimum landscape buffers. Width of such planters shall be a minimum of five feet.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.152(C)(1)(e)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.152(C)(1)(e)" data-short-cite="12.14.152(C)(1)(e)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">e.&#x2002;</span></span>Minimum separation from face of building shall be 10 feet, or more if determined to be necessary for fire or other access requirements.</p>



</section>
</section>
<figure class="type-Figure">
<figcaption><p class="header"><span class="name">Figure 12.14.152-8</span></p>


</figcaption>
<picture class="role-medium">
<img class="role-medium web-winner" src="media/12.14.152-8.png" data-rank="62" style="width: 273pt;height: 237.8pt;"/>
</picture>
</figure>
<figure class="type-Figure">
<figcaption><p class="header"><span class="name">Figure 12.14.152-9</span></p>


</figcaption>
<picture class="role-medium">
<img class="role-medium web-winner" src="media/12.14.152-9.png" data-rank="62" style="width: 353.3pt;height: 260.3pt;"/>
</picture>
</figure>
</section>

<div class="note history history--linked" data-action="click-&gt;levelnodes#openHistory"><p>(Ord. 2417 &#xA7;&#xA0;2 (Exh. A), 2024; Ord. 2269 &#xA7;&#xA0;7, 2018).</p></div>



</article>
<article class="level6 chunking-small type-Section has-history" id="12.14.155" data-cite="BMC 12.14.155" data-short-cite="12.14.155" data-history="/BMC/12.14.155/history"><header class=" full-header" data-type="Section" data-search="/BMC/12.14.155/search" data-share="/BMC/12.14.155/share" data-levelnodes-target="header"><h6 class=" inner-header"><span class="num">12.14.155</span> <span class="name">Storage space and collection points for recyclables and garbage.</span></h6></header><div class="track-indicator selected d-none">
<span class="glyphicons glyphicons-check" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="text">This section is included in your selections.</span>
</div>




<p class="heading">All development other than single-family residential development shall provide storage space for the collection of recyclables and garbage as follows:</p>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.155(A)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.155(A)" data-short-cite="12.14.155(A)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">A.&#x2002;</span></span>The storage space shall be provided at the rate of:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.155(A)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.155(A)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.155(A)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span></span>Three square feet per dwelling unit in multifamily developments except where the development is participating in a public agency-sponsored or approved direct collection program in which individual recycling bins are used for curbside collection;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.155(A)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.155(A)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.155(A)(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span></span>Four square feet per every 1,000 square feet of building gross floor area in office, educational and institutional developments;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.155(A)(3)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.155(A)(3)" data-short-cite="12.14.155(A)(3)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">3.&#x2002;</span></span>Six square feet per every 1,000 square feet of building gross floor area in manufacturing developments and all developments other than those specified in this subsection; and</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.155(A)(4)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.155(A)(4)" data-short-cite="12.14.155(A)(4)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">4.&#x2002;</span></span>Ten square feet per every 1,000 square feet of building gross floor area in retail developments.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.155(B)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.155(B)" data-short-cite="12.14.155(B)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">B.&#x2002;</span></span>The storage space for multifamily residential developments shall be apportioned and located in collection points as follows:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.155(B)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.155(B)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.155(B)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span></span>The required storage area shall be dispersed among collection points throughout the site when there is more than one building.</p>



</section>
