<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.155(B)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.155(B)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.155(B)(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span></span>There shall be a minimum of one collection point for every 30 dwelling units.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.155(B)(3)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.155(B)(3)" data-short-cite="12.14.155(B)(3)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">3.&#x2002;</span></span>Collection points may be located within residential buildings, in separate buildings/structures without dwelling units, or outdoors. Outdoor collection points shall not be located in any required setback areas.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.155(B)(4)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.155(B)(4)" data-short-cite="12.14.155(B)(4)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">4.&#x2002;</span></span>Collection points located in separate buildings/structures or outdoors shall be no more than 200 feet from a common entrance of a residential building.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.155(B)(5)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.155(B)(5)" data-short-cite="12.14.155(B)(5)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">5.&#x2002;</span></span>Collection points shall be located in a manner so that hauling trucks do not obstruct pedestrian traffic or project into any public right-of-way.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.155(C)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.155(C)" data-short-cite="12.14.155(C)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">C.&#x2002;</span></span>The storage space for nonresidential development shall be apportioned and located in collection points as follows:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.155(C)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.155(C)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.155(C)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span></span>Storage space may be allocated to a centralized collection point.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.155(C)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.155(C)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.155(C)(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span></span>Outdoor collection points shall not be located in any required setback areas.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.155(C)(3)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.155(C)(3)" data-short-cite="12.14.155(C)(3)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">3.&#x2002;</span></span>Collection points shall be located in a manner so that hauling trucks do not obstruct pedestrian or vehicle traffic , or project into any public right-of-way.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.155(C)(4)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.155(C)(4)" data-short-cite="12.14.155(C)(4)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">4.&#x2002;</span></span>Access to collection points may be limited, except during regular business hours and/or specified collection hours.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.155(D)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.155(D)" data-short-cite="12.14.155(D)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">D.&#x2002;</span></span>The collection points shall be designed as follows:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.155(D)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.155(D)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.155(D)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span></span>Dimensions of the collection points shall be of sufficient width and depth to enclose containers for recyclables and garbage. The area enclosed in such collection points shall be in accordance with the storage space requirements set forth in subsection <a class="cite" href="12.14.155(A)" title="12.14.155(A)">A</a> of this section.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.155(D)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.155(D)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.155(D)(2)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span></span>Architectural design of any structure enclosing an outdoor collection point or any building primarily used to contain a collection point shall be consistent with the design of the primary structure(s) on the site through all of the following:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.155(D)(2)(a)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.155(D)(2)(a)" data-short-cite="12.14.155(D)(2)(a)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span></span>Similar architectural style;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.155(D)(2)(b)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.155(D)(2)(b)" data-short-cite="12.14.155(D)(2)(b)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span></span>Similar exterior finish materials and colors;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.155(D)(2)(c)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.155(D)(2)(c)" data-short-cite="12.14.155(D)(2)(c)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">c.&#x2002;</span></span>Similar architectural details or features.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.155(D)(3)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.155(D)(3)" data-short-cite="12.14.155(D)(3)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">3.&#x2002;</span></span>Collection points shall be identified by signs not exceeding two square feet each.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.155(D)(4)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.155(D)(4)" data-short-cite="12.14.155(D)(4)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">4.&#x2002;</span></span>A seven-foot wall or fence with a gate or gates shall enclose any outdoor collection point, excluding collection points located in industrial developments that are greater than 100 feet from residentially zoned property and not visible from the street right-of-way.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.155(D)(5)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.155(D)(5)" data-short-cite="12.14.155(D)(5)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">5.&#x2002;</span></span>Enclosures for outdoor collection points and buildings used primarily to contain a collection point shall have gate openings at least 12 feet wide for hauling trucks. In addition, the gate opening for any building or other roofed structure used primarily as a collection point shall have a vertical clearance of at least 12 feet. Each enclosure may have more than one gate opening.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.155(D)(6)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.155(D)(6)" data-short-cite="12.14.155(D)(6)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">6.&#x2002;</span></span>Weather protection of recyclables shall be ensured by using weather-proof containers or by providing a roof over the storage area.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.155(D)(7)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.155(D)(7)" data-short-cite="12.14.155(D)(7)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">7.&#x2002;</span></span>No collection point or container shall be visible from the street right-of-way or adjacent single-family development. The collection point or container shall either be located behind the building or shall be adequately screened with Type I landscaping.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.155(E)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.155(E)" data-short-cite="12.14.155(E)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">E.&#x2002;</span></span>Only recyclable materials and garbage generated shall be collected and stored at such collection points. Except for initial sorting of recyclables by users, all other processing of such materials shall be conducted off site.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.155(F)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.155(F)" data-short-cite="12.14.155(F)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">F.&#x2002;</span></span>No container shall be collected and stored at any location on the site except in a designated storage facility.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.155(G)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.155(G)" data-short-cite="12.14.155(G)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">G.&#x2002;</span></span>The lids of all garbage and recyclables containers shall be maintained in a closed position. <span class="note history history--linked" data-action="click-&gt;levelnodes#openHistory">(Ord. 1815 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 2000; Ord. 1798 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 2000. Formerly 12.14.165).</span></p>



</section>





</article>
<article class="level6 chunking-small type-Section has-history" id="12.14.160" data-cite="BMC 12.14.160" data-short-cite="12.14.160" data-history="/BMC/12.14.160/history"><header class=" full-header" data-type="Section" data-search="/BMC/12.14.160/search" data-share="/BMC/12.14.160/share" data-levelnodes-target="header"><h6 class=" inner-header"><span class="num">12.14.160</span> <span class="name">Lot divided by zone boundary.</span></h6></header><div class="track-indicator selected d-none">
<span class="glyphicons glyphicons-check" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="text">This section is included in your selections.</span>
</div>




<p class="heading">When a lot is divided by a zone boundary, the following rules shall apply:</p>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.160(A)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.160(A)" data-short-cite="12.14.160(A)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">A.&#x2002;</span></span>When a lot contains both residential and nonresidential zoning, the zone boundary between the zones shall be considered a lot line for determining permitted building height and required setbacks on the site;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.160(B)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.160(B)" data-short-cite="12.14.160(B)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">B.&#x2002;</span></span>When a lot contains residential zones of varying density, any residential density transfer within the lot shall only be allowed from the portion with the lesser residential density to that of the greater residential density; and</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.160(C)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.160(C)" data-short-cite="12.14.160(C)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">C.&#x2002;</span></span>Uses on each portion of the lot shall only be those permitted in each zone pursuant to Chapter <a class="cite" href="12.06" title="Ch. 12.06&#x2002;Permitted Uses">12.06</a> BMC. <span class="note history history--linked" data-action="click-&gt;levelnodes#openHistory">(Ord. 1815 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 2000; Ord. 1629 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 1996. Formerly 12.14.170).</span></p>



</section>





</article>
<article class="level6 chunking-small type-Section has-history" id="12.14.170" data-cite="BMC 12.14.170" data-short-cite="12.14.170" data-history="/BMC/12.14.170/history"><header class=" full-header" data-type="Section" data-search="/BMC/12.14.170/search" data-share="/BMC/12.14.170/share" data-levelnodes-target="header"><h6 class=" inner-header"><span class="num">12.14.170</span> <span class="name">Site and exterior building design, generally.</span></h6></header><div class="track-indicator selected d-none">
<span class="glyphicons glyphicons-check" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="text">This section is included in your selections.</span>
</div>




<p>Design of sites and buildings shall comply with BMC <span class="range"><a class="cite" href="12.14.180" title="12.14.180&#x2002;Site design">12.14.180</a> through <a class="cite" href="12.14.230" title="12.14.230&#x2002;Design of developments on or adjacent to historic register or historic inventory properties">12.14.230</a></span> and other applicable regulations in this code which implement the goals and policies of the urban design element of the Imagine Bothell Comprehensive Plan. In addition to compliance with these regulations, persons interested in developing property are required to consult the illustrations and written guidelines in the urban design element and in the residential development handbook for Snohomish County Communities, which is incorporated by reference in the comprehensive plan as Appendix H. As an appendix to the plan, the residential development handbook is an adopted SEPA policy of the city of Bothell. <span class="note history history--linked" data-action="click-&gt;levelnodes#openHistory">(Ord. 2445 &#xA7;&#xA0;6 (Exh. F), 2024; Ord. 1946 &#xA7;&#xA0;2, 2005; Ord. 1815 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 2000; Ord. 1798 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 2000; Ord. 1629 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 1996. Formerly 12.14.180).</span></p>








</article>
<article class="level6 chunking-small type-Section has-history" id="12.14.180" data-cite="BMC 12.14.180" data-short-cite="12.14.180" data-history="/BMC/12.14.180/history"><header class=" full-header" data-type="Section" data-search="/BMC/12.14.180/search" data-share="/BMC/12.14.180/share" data-levelnodes-target="header"><h6 class=" inner-header"><span class="num">12.14.180</span> <span class="name">Site design.</span></h6></header><div class="track-indicator selected d-none">
<span class="glyphicons glyphicons-check" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="text">This section is included in your selections.</span>
</div>




<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.180(A)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.180(A)" data-short-cite="12.14.180(A)"><p class="heading"><span class="header"><span class="num">A.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Residential Development Access Design.</span> </span>The access design of residential developments shall comply with the following:</p>




<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.180(A)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.180(A)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.180(A)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span></span>Streets shall be located and configured in such a manner as to not promote residential neighborhood cut-through traffic. Improvements to the existing street network shall be planned to restrict through traffic to arterials and to reduce the amount of through traffic on residential neighborhood streets. Neighborhood cut-through traffic shall mean motor vehicle traffic which neither originates nor terminates within the neighborhood, but travels through the neighborhood for the purposes of avoiding arterial traffic and/or saving time between an origin and a destination outside the neighborhood. Whether a proposed street network promotes or does not promote residential neighborhood cut-through traffic will be determined on the basis of the required traffic impact analysis submitted with the development application.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.180(A)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.180(A)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.180(A)(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span></span>Connections to surrounding streets and developments shall be provided for pedestrians and bicyclists as warranted for safe and efficient nonmotorized travel within and between neighborhoods, and in particular to provide access to activity centers, parks, schools, transit stops and other gathering places. Such connections shall include but are not limited to bicycle lanes, sidewalks and dedicated pedestrian/bicycle paths, and shall be provided in accordance with BMC <a class="cite" href="12.16.120" title="12.16.120&#x2002;Pedestrian circulation and access">12.16.120</a> and this section.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.180(A)(3)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.180(A)(3)" data-short-cite="12.14.180(A)(3)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">3.&#x2002;</span></span>Connections to surrounding streets and developments shall be provided for police, fire and emergency medical services vehicles as warranted to minimize response times to, from and within the neighborhood. Where such connections are determined on the basis of the required transportation impact analysis to promote residential neighborhood cut-through traffic, emergency vehicle-only access ways shall be provided.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.180(B)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.180(B)" data-short-cite="12.14.180(B)"><p><span class="header"><span class="num">B.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Commercial and Mixed-Use Development Access Design.</span> </span>In conjunction with new development or redevelopment in areas zoned for commercial or mixed commercial and residential uses, proposed streets shall be located and configured in such a manner as to create an interconnected network which facilitates motorized and nonmotorized travel; provided, however, that proposed streets or connections which are determined to promote residential neighborhood cut-through traffic, as defined in subsection <a class="cite" href="12.14.180(A)" title="12.14.180(A)&#x2002;Residential Development Access Design">A</a> of this section, are not allowed.</p>




</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.180(C)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.180(C)" data-short-cite="12.14.180(C)"><p class="heading"><span class="header"><span class="num">C.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Development on Hillsides.</span> </span>Development on hillsides shall blend visually and functionally into the natural environment to the maximum extent possible. Such development shall comply with the following:</p>




<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.180(C)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.180(C)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.180(C)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span></span>Changes to the natural topography shall be minimized. Roads, lots, buildings and parking areas shall be located, configured and constructed so as to minimize grading and its consequent impacts on soil stability and surface and groundwater movement.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.180(C)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.180(C)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.180(C)(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span></span>Clearing of existing native vegetation shall be minimized. Existing vegetation shall be retained in accordance with BMC <a class="cite" href="12.18.030" title="12.18.030&#x2002;Existing vegetation retention">12.18.030</a> and this section to preserve wildlife habitat and natural groundwater recharge functions and promote soil stability.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.180(C)(3)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.180(C)(3)" data-short-cite="12.14.180(C)(3)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">3.&#x2002;</span></span>Retaining walls and high foundations on the underside of buildings shall be screened by vegetation.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.180(D)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.180(D)" data-short-cite="12.14.180(D)"><p class="heading"><span class="header"><span class="num">D.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Location of Buildings and Parking.</span> </span>In any development in the OP, NB, CB, and GC zoning classifications, and in any zone which combines one or more of these classifications with an R zoning classification, parking shall not be located between any building to which the general public has access and the front lot line, subject to the following:</p>




<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.180(D)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.180(D)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.180(D)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span></span>Parking shall be prohibited in the area between the front lot line and a line running parallel to the front lot line and tangent to the closest point of the building to the front lot line, and extending along the front lot line that distance by which the building faces the front lot line, as measured between points projected from the most distant points of the building at right angles to the front lot line. Examples of how this regulation applies are provided in Figures 12.14-1, 12.14-2 and 12.14-3.</p>



<figure class="type-Figure">
<figcaption><p class="header"><span class="name">Fig. 12.14-1</span></p>


</figcaption>
<picture class="role-medium">
<img class="role-medium web-winner" src="media/bothel12.13.10.11.180119.jpg" data-rank="61" style="width: 449.3pt;"/>
</picture>
</figure>
<figure class="type-Figure">
<figcaption><p class="header"><span class="name">Fig. 12.14-2</span></p>


</figcaption>
<picture class="role-medium">
<img class="role-medium web-winner" src="media/12.13.10.12.jpg" data-rank="61" style="width: 449.3pt;"/>
</picture>
</figure>
<figure class="type-Figure">
<figcaption><p class="header"><span class="name">Fig. 12.14-3</span></p>


</figcaption>
<picture class="role-medium">
<img class="role-medium web-winner" src="media/12.13.10.13.180119.jpg" data-rank="61" style="width: 449.3pt;"/>
</picture>
</figure>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.180(D)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.180(D)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.180(D)(2)"><p><span class="header"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Corner Lots.</span> </span>A corner lot is defined as a lot at the junction of and having frontage on two or more intersecting streets. On a corner lot, each lot line abutting a street right-of-way is considered a front lot line.</p>




<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.180(D)(2)(a)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.180(D)(2)(a)" data-short-cite="12.14.180(D)(2)(a)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span></span>A building on a corner lot shall be placed at the intersection of the front lot lines so that there is no parking between the building and either front lot line.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.180(D)(2)(b)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.180(D)(2)(b)" data-short-cite="12.14.180(D)(2)(b)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span></span>When a corner lot has frontage on more than one street intersection, the regulation in subsection <a class="cite link-broken" href="12.14.180(A)(2)(a)">(A)(2)(a)</a> of this section shall apply only to one of the intersections. An example of how this regulation applies is provided in Figure 12.14-4.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.180(D)(2)(c)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.180(D)(2)(c)" data-short-cite="12.14.180(D)(2)(c)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">c.&#x2002;</span></span>Buildings on a corner lot shall be located so as not to interfere with sight distance at intersections.</p>



<figure class="type-Figure">
<figcaption><p class="header"><span class="name">Fig. 12.14-4</span></p>


</figcaption>
<picture class="role-medium">
<img class="role-medium web-winner" src="media/12144.png" data-rank="62" style="width: 480pt;"/>
</picture>
</figure>
</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.180(D)(3)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.180(D)(3)" data-short-cite="12.14.180(D)(3)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">3.&#x2002;</span></span>A through lot is defined as a lot which has frontage on two parallel or approximately parallel streets. In such a case, the requirements in subsection <a class="cite" href="12.14.180(A)" title="12.14.180(A)&#x2002;Residential Development Access Design">A</a> of this section shall apply to only one of the frontages. An example of how this regulation applies is provided in Figure 12.14-5.</p>



<figure class="type-Figure">
<figcaption><p class="header"><span class="name">Fig. 12.14-5</span></p>


</figcaption>
<picture class="role-medium">
<img class="role-medium web-winner" src="media/12145.png" data-rank="62" style="width: 480pt;"/>
</picture>
</figure>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.180(D)(4)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.180(D)(4)" data-short-cite="12.14.180(D)(4)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">4.&#x2002;</span></span>Where a development consists of two or more buildings on one property, the requirement in this subsection D shall apply to only one of the buildings in the development. Buildings containing less than 1,000 square feet of gross floor area shall not be counted as satisfying this requirement.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.180(D)(5)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.180(D)(5)" data-short-cite="12.14.180(D)(5)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">5.&#x2002;</span></span>The area between the building and the front lot line may be utilized for landscaping, outdoor pedestrian activities, critical areas and their buffers, or storm water detention facilities.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.180(D)(6)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.180(D)(6)" data-short-cite="12.14.180(D)(6)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">6.&#x2002;</span></span>Where it can be demonstrated that one or more site characteristics make compliance with these regulations unfeasible, the community development director may allow an alternative building location. Such site characteristics include but are not limited to:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.180(D)(6)(a)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.180(D)(6)(a)" data-short-cite="12.14.180(D)(6)(a)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span></span>Parcel shape;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.180(D)(6)(b)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.180(D)(6)(b)" data-short-cite="12.14.180(D)(6)(b)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span></span>Topography;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.180(D)(6)(c)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.180(D)(6)(c)" data-short-cite="12.14.180(D)(6)(c)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">c.&#x2002;</span></span>Existing shared access with adjacent properties;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.180(D)(6)(d)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.180(D)(6)(d)" data-short-cite="12.14.180(D)(6)(d)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">d.&#x2002;</span></span>Existing significant trees which might be saved by locating a building elsewhere.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.180(D)(7)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.180(D)(7)" data-short-cite="12.14.180(D)(7)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">7.&#x2002;</span></span>Where an overall site plan has been adopted, through a subarea plan and implementing regulations, a planned unit development, a binding site plan, a conditional use permit, or other mechanism requiring approval by the hearing body, planning commission and/or the city council, the adopted site plan shall control the location of buildings and parking.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.180(D)(8)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.180(D)(8)" data-short-cite="12.14.180(D)(8)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">8.&#x2002;</span></span>These regulations shall not apply to location of single-family dwellings.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.180(E)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.180(E)" data-short-cite="12.14.180(E)"><p class="heading"><span class="header"><span class="num">E.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Community Gathering Place.</span> </span>To encourage public interaction and promote a sense of community, proposed developments of five acres or more shall provide a community gathering place or places at a ratio of 20 square feet of improved space per acre, with a minimum of 100 square feet per gathering place. For the purposes of these regulations, &#x201C;community gathering place&#x201D; shall mean an informal, small-scale, hard-surfaced area intended for use by the general public. The community gathering place may be located adjacent to public right-of-way, for example, as a plaza connected to the sidewalk, or internal to the development. Adjacent developments may combine to form one or more community gathering places. Each community gathering place shall include one or more features to encourage public interaction. Such features include but are not limited to the following:</p>




<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.180(E)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.180(E)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.180(E)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span></span>Benches;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.180(E)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.180(E)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.180(E)(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span></span>Fountains;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.180(E)(3)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.180(E)(3)" data-short-cite="12.14.180(E)(3)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">3.&#x2002;</span></span>Sculpture or other art forms;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.180(E)(4)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.180(E)(4)" data-short-cite="12.14.180(E)(4)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">4.&#x2002;</span></span>Kiosks for posting neighborhood items;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.180(E)(5)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.180(E)(5)" data-short-cite="12.14.180(E)(5)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">5.&#x2002;</span></span>Links to transit and pedestrian and bike trails;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.180(E)(6)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.180(E)(6)" data-short-cite="12.14.180(E)(6)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">6.&#x2002;</span></span>Bandstands or gazebos;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.180(E)(7)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.180(E)(7)" data-short-cite="12.14.180(E)(7)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">7.&#x2002;</span></span>Pedestrian-scale lighting.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.180(F)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.180(F)" data-short-cite="12.14.180(F)"><p class="heading"><span class="header"><span class="num">F.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Exterior Lighting.</span> </span>Exterior lighting shall be an integral part of the site and building design and shall serve the purposes of enhancing safety and security, defining on-site pedestrian and bicycle paths and connections with off-site paths, and encouraging safe nighttime pedestrian activity and public interaction. Such lighting shall accomplish one or more of the following:</p>




<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.180(F)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.180(F)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.180(F)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span></span>Illuminate landscaping elements such as tree foliage and shrubs;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.180(F)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.180(F)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.180(F)(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span></span>Illuminate building elements such as entryways, canopies, cornices or other architectural features;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.180(F)(3)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.180(F)(3)" data-short-cite="12.14.180(F)(3)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">3.&#x2002;</span></span>Illuminate parking lots;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.180(F)(4)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.180(F)(4)" data-short-cite="12.14.180(F)(4)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">4.&#x2002;</span></span>Illuminate pedestrian and bicycle pathways with fixtures such as lighting bollards or low pole lights.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.180(G)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.180(G)" data-short-cite="12.14.180(G)"><p><span class="header"><span class="num">G.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Design of Parking Lots, Parking Structures, Transit Stops and Shelters and Pedestrian Sidewalks and Pathways.</span> </span>The design of these facilities shall be in accordance with BMC <span class="range"><a class="cite" href="12.16.080" title="12.16.080&#x2002;Off-street parking location and design">12.16.080</a> through <a class="cite" href="12.16.120" title="12.16.120&#x2002;Pedestrian circulation and access">12.16.120</a></span>. <span class="note history history--linked" data-action="click-&gt;levelnodes#openHistory">(Ord. 2269 &#xA7;&#xA0;8, 2018; Ord. 2171 &#xA7;&#xA0;3 (Exh. C), 2015; Ord. 2140 &#xA7;&#xA0;2 (Exh. B), 2014; Ord. 1946 &#xA7;&#xA0;2, 2005; Ord. 1815 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 2000; Ord. 1798 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 2000. Formerly 12.14.190).</span></p>




</section>









</article>
<article class="level6 chunking-small type-Section has-history" id="12.14.190" data-cite="BMC 12.14.190" data-short-cite="12.14.190" data-history="/BMC/12.14.190/history"><header class=" full-header" data-type="Section" data-search="/BMC/12.14.190/search" data-share="/BMC/12.14.190/share" data-levelnodes-target="header"><h6 class=" inner-header"><span class="num">12.14.190</span> <span class="name">Exterior building design.</span></h6></header><div class="track-indicator selected d-none">
<span class="glyphicons glyphicons-check" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="text">This section is included in your selections.</span>
</div>




<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.190(A)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.190(A)" data-short-cite="12.14.190(A)"><p class="heading"><span class="header"><span class="num">A.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Overall Design.</span> </span>The exterior of each non-single-family building shall be designed so as to have a visually distinct base, main section and roofline, to provide visual references to the scale of the building, and to help the building fit in with its context, in accordance with the following:</p>




<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.190(A)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.190(A)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.190(A)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span></span>The base may comprise the foundation itself; the nonstructural use of materials such as brick, stone or textured concrete which convey an impression of permanence and support; or a contrast in materials or colors between the base and the main section.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.190(A)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.190(A)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.190(A)(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span></span>The main section shall incorporate architectural features and detailing to create visual interest. In addition to the requirements of BMC <a class="cite" href="12.14.200" title="12.14.200&#x2002;Exterior building design&#xA0;&#x2013; Multiple-family residential">12.14.200</a> and <a class="cite" href="12.14.210" title="12.14.210&#x2002;Exterior building design&#xA0;&#x2013; Commercial and industrial">12.14.210</a>, such features and detailing may include but are not limited to windows, columns, pilasters, belt courses, brackets, arches, decorative molding, quoins and similar architectural devices. Any architectural decoration shall be part of a unified design.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.190(A)(3)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.190(A)(3)" data-short-cite="12.14.190(A)(3)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">3.&#x2002;</span></span>The design of the roof shall employ at least one of the following:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.190(A)(3)(a)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.190(A)(3)(a)" data-short-cite="12.14.190(A)(3)(a)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span></span>A gable, gambrel or hipped roof;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.190(A)(3)(b)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.190(A)(3)(b)" data-short-cite="12.14.190(A)(3)(b)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span></span>A broken or articulated roofline;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.190(A)(3)(c)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.190(A)(3)(c)" data-short-cite="12.14.190(A)(3)(c)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">c.&#x2002;</span></span>A prominent cornice or fascia that defines and emphasizes the top of the building;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.190(A)(3)(d)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.190(A)(3)(d)" data-short-cite="12.14.190(A)(3)(d)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">d.&#x2002;</span></span>Any other roof element that emphasizes a building&#x2019;s unifying architectural concept and helps it to fit in with its context.</p>



</section>
</section>
<p>Examples of how these regulations apply are illustrated in Figures 12.14-9, 12.14-10 and 12.14-11.</p>
<figure class="type-Figure">
<figcaption><p class="header"><span class="name">Fig. 12.14-9</span></p>


</figcaption>
<picture class="role-medium">
<img class="role-medium web-winner" src="media/12149.jpg" data-rank="61" style="width: 436.5pt;"/>
</picture>
</figure>
<figure class="type-Figure">
<figcaption><p class="header"><span class="name">Fig. 12.14-10</span></p>


</figcaption>
<picture class="role-medium">
<img class="role-medium web-winner" src="media/121410.jpg" data-rank="61" style="width: 429.8pt;"/>
</picture>
</figure>
<figure class="type-Figure">
<figcaption><p class="header"><span class="name">Fig. 12.14-11</span></p>


</figcaption>
<picture class="role-medium">
<img class="role-medium web-winner" src="media/12.13.10.18.180119.jpg" data-rank="61" style="width: 452.3pt;"/>
</picture>
</figure>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.190(B)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.190(B)" data-short-cite="12.14.190(B)"><p class="heading"><span class="header"><span class="num">B.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Pedestrian Entries.</span> </span>The building design shall clearly define and emphasize the pedestrian entries. Any primary pedestrian entry to a building shall be designed so as to be visually predominant over parking lot driveways or garage entrances. Pedestrian entries shall be emphasized by one or more of the following methods:</p>




<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.190(B)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.190(B)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.190(B)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span></span>Landscaping the entry and approach to the entry in a manner which is consistent with and extends the building design theme;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.190(B)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.190(B)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.190(B)(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span></span>Incorporating a formal entry;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.190(B)(3)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.190(B)(3)" data-short-cite="12.14.190(B)(3)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">3.&#x2002;</span></span>Utilizing modulation to emphasize by indentation or protrusion the portion of the building containing the entry;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.190(B)(4)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.190(B)(4)" data-short-cite="12.14.190(B)(4)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">4.&#x2002;</span></span>Incorporating a porte-cochere or other cover providing weather protection for the entry.</p>



</section>
<p>Examples of how these regulations apply are illustrated in Figure 12.14-12.</p>
<figure class="type-Figure">
<figcaption><p class="header"><span class="name">Fig. 12.14-12</span></p>


</figcaption>
<picture class="role-medium">
<img class="role-medium web-winner" src="media/12.13.10.19.180119.jpg" data-rank="61" style="width: 480pt;"/>
</picture>
</figure>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.190(C)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.190(C)" data-short-cite="12.14.190(C)"><p class="heading"><span class="header"><span class="num">C.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Exterior Building Materials.</span> </span>Building exteriors shall be constructed of durable and easily maintainable materials, to enhance the overall appearance, community character and long-term economic vitality of the area in which the building is located. The use of various types of materials in exterior building design shall be in accordance with the following:</p>




<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.190(C)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.190(C)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.190(C)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span></span>Metal siding shall have visible corner moldings and trim, and a matte finish;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.190(C)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.190(C)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.190(C)(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span></span>Mirrored glass is allowed only in the LI zone and above the street level story in the OP, NB, CB and GC zones;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.190(C)(3)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.190(C)(3)" data-short-cite="12.14.190(C)(3)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">3.&#x2002;</span></span>Concrete walls shall be enhanced by texturing, coloring with paint or an admixture, or by incorporating embossed or sculpted surfaces, mosaics or artwork.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.190(D)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.190(D)" data-short-cite="12.14.190(D)"><p><span class="header"><span class="num">D.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Screening of Roof-Mounted Mechanical Devices.</span> </span>Mechanical devices, such as exhaust fans, vents, air conditioning units, heating equipment, electrical devices, mechanical, electrical and other equipment located on the roof of any structure shall be screened to avoid unsightly roof appearance as viewed from surrounding property, including hillsides. The design of the screening shall be incorporated as an integral part of the overall building design. Screening materials shall be consistent with subsection <a class="cite" href="12.14.190(C)" title="12.14.190(C)&#x2002;Exterior Building Materials">C</a> of this section. Roof-mounted solar or wind equipment is exempt from screening requirements, provided the installation is consistent with BMC <a class="cite" href="12.14.250(D)" title="12.14.250(D)&#x2002;Solar and Wind Infrastructure">12.14.250(D)</a>. <span class="note history history--linked" data-action="click-&gt;levelnodes#openHistory">(Ord. 2107 &#xA7;&#xA0;2 (Exh. B), 2012; Ord. 1815 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 2000; Ord. 1798 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 2000. Formerly 12.14.200).</span></p>




</section>






</article>
<article class="level6 chunking-small type-Section has-history" id="12.14.200" data-cite="BMC 12.14.200" data-short-cite="12.14.200" data-history="/BMC/12.14.200/history"><header class=" full-header" data-type="Section" data-search="/BMC/12.14.200/search" data-share="/BMC/12.14.200/share" data-levelnodes-target="header"><h6 class=" inner-header"><span class="num">12.14.200</span> <span class="name">Exterior building design&#xA0;&#x2013; Multiple-family residential.</span></h6></header><div class="track-indicator selected d-none">
<span class="glyphicons glyphicons-check" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="text">This section is included in your selections.</span>
</div>




<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.200(A)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.200(A)" data-short-cite="12.14.200(A)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">A.&#x2002;</span></span>Multiple-family residential building design shall promote public interaction and a sense of community within each development by incorporating one or more of the following features:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.200(A)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.200(A)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.200(A)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span></span>Balconies;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.200(A)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.200(A)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.200(A)(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span></span>Porches;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.200(A)(3)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.200(A)(3)" data-short-cite="12.14.200(A)(3)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">3.&#x2002;</span></span>Rooftop terraces;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.200(A)(4)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.200(A)(4)" data-short-cite="12.14.200(A)(4)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">4.&#x2002;</span></span>At-grade patios;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.200(A)(5)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.200(A)(5)" data-short-cite="12.14.200(A)(5)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">5.&#x2002;</span></span>Courtyards;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.200(A)(6)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.200(A)(6)" data-short-cite="12.14.200(A)(6)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">6.&#x2002;</span></span>Formal or informal gardens.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.200(B)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.200(B)" data-short-cite="12.14.200(B)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">B.&#x2002;</span></span>Where a building containing residential dwelling units would be located in close proximity to a public right-of-way, the design shall create a transition between the public realm of the street and the private realm of the residences by incorporating one or more of the following:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.200(B)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.200(B)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.200(B)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span></span>Screening and buffering with trees, shrubs, fences and/or walls to create a physical separation between pedestrians and the windows of residential units;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.200(B)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.200(B)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.200(B)(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span></span>Construction of the first story of units so that they are above the level of the sidewalk a sufficient height to prevent a direct view into the dwelling units;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.200(B)(3)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.200(B)(3)" data-short-cite="12.14.200(B)(3)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">3.&#x2002;</span></span>Partially enclosed porches and other outdoor living areas which front onto the street;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.200(B)(4)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.200(B)(4)" data-short-cite="12.14.200(B)(4)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">4.&#x2002;</span></span>Courtyards fronting on the street and defined by landscaping and/or fences.</p>



</section>
<p>Examples of how these regulations apply are illustrated in Figure 12.14-13.</p>
<figure class="type-Figure">
<figcaption><p class="header"><span class="name">Fig. 12.14-13</span></p>


</figcaption>
<picture class="role-medium">
<img class="role-medium web-winner" src="media/12.13.10.20.180119.jpg" data-rank="61" style="width: 474pt;"/>
</picture>
</figure>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.200(C)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.200(C)" data-short-cite="12.14.200(C)"><p class="heading"><span class="header"><span class="num">C.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Building Facade Modulation.</span> </span>Modulation is a measured and proportioned inflexion or setback in a building&#x2019;s face. Multifamily residential buildings shall provide modulation on facades as follows:</p>




<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.200(C)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.200(C)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.200(C)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span></span>The maximum wall length without modulation shall be 40 feet;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.200(C)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.200(C)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.200(C)(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span></span>The sum of the modulation depth and modulation width shall be no less than eight feet. The modulation depth shall be not less than two feet and the modulation width shall be not less than six feet.</p>



</section>
<p>An example of how these regulations apply is illustrated in Figure 12.14-14.</p>
<figure class="type-Figure">
<figcaption><p class="header"><span class="name">Fig. 12.14-14</span></p>


</figcaption>
<picture class="role-medium">
<img class="role-medium web-winner" src="media/12.13.10.21.180119.jpg" data-rank="61" style="width: 280.5pt;"/>
</picture>
</figure>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.200(D)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.200(D)" data-short-cite="12.14.200(D)"><p class="heading"><span class="header"><span class="num">D.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Roofline Variation.</span> </span>Multifamily residential buildings shall provide roofline variation as follows:</p>




<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.200(D)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.200(D)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.200(D)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span></span>The maximum roof length without variation shall be 40 feet;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.200(D)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.200(D)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.200(D)(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span></span>The minimum horizontal or vertical offset shall be three feet;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.200(D)(3)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.200(D)(3)" data-short-cite="12.14.200(D)(3)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">3.&#x2002;</span></span>The minimum variation length shall be six feet;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.200(D)(4)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.200(D)(4)" data-short-cite="12.14.200(D)(4)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">4.&#x2002;</span></span>Roofline variation shall be achieved using one or more of the following methods:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.200(D)(4)(a)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.200(D)(4)(a)" data-short-cite="12.14.200(D)(4)(a)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span></span>Vertical offset in ridge line;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.200(D)(4)(b)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.200(D)(4)(b)" data-short-cite="12.14.200(D)(4)(b)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span></span>Horizontal offset in ridge line;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.200(D)(4)(c)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.200(D)(4)(c)" data-short-cite="12.14.200(D)(4)(c)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">c.&#x2002;</span></span>Variations of roof pitch;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.200(D)(4)(d)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.200(D)(4)(d)" data-short-cite="12.14.200(D)(4)(d)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">d.&#x2002;</span></span>Gables;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.200(D)(4)(e)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.200(D)(4)(e)" data-short-cite="12.14.200(D)(4)(e)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">e.&#x2002;</span></span>Any other technique approved by the community development director which avoids the appearance of a continuous unrelieved monolithic roofline.</p>



</section>
</section>
<p>An example of how these regulations apply is illustrated in Figure 12.14-15.</p>
<figure class="type-Figure">
<figcaption><p class="header"><span class="name">Fig. 12.14-15</span></p>


</figcaption>
<picture class="role-medium">
<img class="role-medium web-winner" src="media/12.13.10.22.180119.jpg" data-rank="61" style="width: 462.8pt;"/>
</picture>
</figure>
</section>

<div class="note history history--linked" data-action="click-&gt;levelnodes#openHistory"><p>(Ord. 1815 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 2000; Ord. 1798 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 2000; Ord. 1629 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 1996. Formerly 12.14.210).</p></div>





</article>
<article class="level6 chunking-small type-Section has-history" id="12.14.210" data-cite="BMC 12.14.210" data-short-cite="12.14.210" data-history="/BMC/12.14.210/history"><header class=" full-header" data-type="Section" data-search="/BMC/12.14.210/search" data-share="/BMC/12.14.210/share" data-levelnodes-target="header"><h6 class=" inner-header"><span class="num">12.14.210</span> <span class="name">Exterior building design&#xA0;&#x2013; Commercial and industrial.</span></h6></header><div class="track-indicator selected d-none">
<span class="glyphicons glyphicons-check" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="text">This section is included in your selections.</span>
</div>




<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.210(A)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.210(A)" data-short-cite="12.14.210(A)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">A.&#x2002;</span></span>Where pedestrian activity is present, windows are preferable to blank walls because windows create visual interest both for the passerby and for the building occupant. Windowless walls shall not be oriented to the street unless necessary for the use conducted in the building, mandated by the fire code, or otherwise required by city codes. Where a windowless wall is oriented to the street, the appearance of the wall shall be softened by application of BMC <a class="cite" href="12.18.080(C)" title="12.18.080(C)">12.18.080(C)</a> and one or more of the following:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.210(A)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.210(A)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.210(A)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span></span>Installation of trellises and vines;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.210(A)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.210(A)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.210(A)(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span></span>Use of brick, stone, split-face or fluted concrete block, textured poured-in-place concrete, or other materials with texture to reduce the scale of the wall;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.210(A)(3)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.210(A)(3)" data-short-cite="12.14.210(A)(3)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">3.&#x2002;</span></span>Incorporation of artwork, such as a mural, sculpture, or bas-relief, on the wall surface;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.210(A)(4)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.210(A)(4)" data-short-cite="12.14.210(A)(4)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">4.&#x2002;</span></span>False windows.</p>



</section>
<p>An example of how these regulations apply is illustrated in Figure 12.14-16.</p>
<figure class="type-Figure">
<figcaption><p class="header"><span class="name">Fig. 12.14-16</span></p>


</figcaption>
<picture class="role-medium">
<img class="role-medium web-winner" src="media/12.13.10.23.180119.jpg" data-rank="61" style="width: 459pt;"/>
</picture>
</figure>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.210(B)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.210(B)" data-short-cite="12.14.210(B)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">B.&#x2002;</span></span>Buildings at the corners of street intersections shall incorporate design features to create or reinforce the appearance and feeling of the intersection as an outdoor community &#x201C;room.&#x201D; Such design features include but are not limited to:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.210(B)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.210(B)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.210(B)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span></span>A change of wall plane;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.210(B)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.210(B)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.210(B)(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span></span>A change of roofline;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.210(B)(3)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.210(B)(3)" data-short-cite="12.14.210(B)(3)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">3.&#x2002;</span></span>A major pedestrian entry;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.210(B)(4)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.210(B)(4)" data-short-cite="12.14.210(B)(4)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">4.&#x2002;</span></span>Balconies;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.210(B)(5)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.210(B)(5)" data-short-cite="12.14.210(B)(5)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">5.&#x2002;</span></span>Turrets;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.210(B)(6)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.210(B)(6)" data-short-cite="12.14.210(B)(6)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">6.&#x2002;</span></span>Sculpture, mosaic, or other artwork.</p>



</section>
<p>An example of how these regulations apply is illustrated in Figure 12.14-17.</p>
<figure class="type-Figure">
<figcaption><p class="header"><span class="name">Fig. 12.14-17</span></p>


</figcaption>
<picture class="role-medium">
<img class="role-medium web-winner" src="media/12.13.10.24.180119.jpg" data-rank="61" style="width: 444pt;"/>
</picture>
</figure>
</section>

<div class="note history history--linked" data-action="click-&gt;levelnodes#openHistory"><p>(Ord. 1815 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 2000; Ord. 1798 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 2000. Formerly 12.14.220).</p></div>




</article>
<article class="level6 chunking-small type-Section has-history" id="12.14.220" data-cite="BMC 12.14.220" data-short-cite="12.14.220" data-history="/BMC/12.14.220/history"><header class=" full-header" data-type="Section" data-search="/BMC/12.14.220/search" data-share="/BMC/12.14.220/share" data-levelnodes-target="header"><h6 class=" inner-header"><span class="num">12.14.220</span> <span class="name">Exterior building design&#xA0;&#x2013; Multifamily residential, commercial or industrial development abutting single-family residential zoning.</span></h6></header><div class="track-indicator selected d-none">
<span class="glyphicons glyphicons-check" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="text">This section is included in your selections.</span>
</div>




<p class="heading">Where multifamily residential, commercial or industrial development abut single-family residential zones, design compatibility shall be achieved through a combination of measures including but not limited to the following, in addition to other requirements of this title:</p>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.220(A)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.220(A)" data-short-cite="12.14.220(A)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">A.&#x2002;</span></span>The minimum setback from adjacent single-family zoned property shall be 25 feet;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.220(B)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.220(B)" data-short-cite="12.14.220(B)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">B.&#x2002;</span></span>At a minimum, a Type II landscaping buffer shall be required along the single-family property line(s); this and other landscaping within the development shall reinforce the existing landscape character of the area to the maximum extent possible;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.220(C)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.220(C)" data-short-cite="12.14.220(C)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">C.&#x2002;</span></span>Multiple-family and mixed use building design shall incorporate a repetitive articulation and modulation which reflects the character of development in the adjacent single-family zones, as illustrated in Figure 12.14-18, unless a majority of the houses within one street of the proposed development do not share a common style or a different character has been identified and adopted under subarea regulations;</p>



<figure class="type-Figure">
<figcaption><p class="header"><span class="name">Fig. 12.14-18</span></p>


</figcaption>
<picture class="role-medium">
<img class="role-medium web-winner" src="media/12.13.10.25.180119.jpg" data-rank="61" style="width: 476.3pt;"/>
</picture>
</figure>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.220(D)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.220(D)" data-short-cite="12.14.220(D)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">D.&#x2002;</span></span>The following design features of adjacent single-family residential development shall be incorporated in multiple-family and mixed use developments unless a majority of the houses within one street of the proposed development do not share a common style or a different character and scale has been identified and adopted under subarea regulations:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.220(D)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.220(D)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.220(D)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span></span>Siding materials and styles;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.220(D)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.220(D)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.220(D)(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span></span>Window arrangement, spacing and sizing;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.220(D)(3)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.220(D)(3)" data-short-cite="12.14.220(D)(3)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">3.&#x2002;</span></span>Roof materials and styles; and</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.220(D)(4)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.220(D)(4)" data-short-cite="12.14.220(D)(4)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">4.&#x2002;</span></span>Proportions of architectural elements;</p>



</section>
<p>Examples of how the above regulations apply are illustrated in Figure 12.14-19.</p>
<figure class="type-Figure">
<figcaption><p class="header"><span class="name">Fig. 12.14-19</span></p>


</figcaption>
<picture class="role-medium">
<img class="role-medium web-winner" src="media/12.13.10.26.180119.jpg" data-rank="61" style="width: 472.5pt;"/>
</picture>
</figure>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.220(E)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.220(E)" data-short-cite="12.14.220(E)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">E.&#x2002;</span></span>Building design shall respect and preserve the privacy of adjacent single-family development by minimizing the quantity and size of windows oriented to the single-family residential zone. Any decks and balconies overlooking neighboring single-family yards are prohibited;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.220(F)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.220(F)" data-short-cite="12.14.220(F)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">F.&#x2002;</span></span>Parking lots shall be designed so as to least impact the character of the single-family neighborhood;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.220(G)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.220(G)" data-short-cite="12.14.220(G)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">G.&#x2002;</span></span>Heating, cooling, kitchen and other mechanical equipment, conduits, service panels, meters and other electrical equipment, and refuse collection facilities shall be located and screened so as not to be visible or audible above ambient noise levels from adjacent single-family development or the street. <span class="note history history--linked" data-action="click-&gt;levelnodes#openHistory">(Ord. 1815 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 2000; Ord. 1798 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 2000; Ord. 1629 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 1996. Formerly 12.14.230).</span></p>



</section>






</article>
<article class="level6 chunking-small type-Section has-history" id="12.14.230" data-cite="BMC 12.14.230" data-short-cite="12.14.230" data-history="/BMC/12.14.230/history"><header class=" full-header" data-type="Section" data-search="/BMC/12.14.230/search" data-share="/BMC/12.14.230/share" data-levelnodes-target="header"><h6 class=" inner-header"><span class="num">12.14.230</span> <span class="name">Design of developments on or adjacent to historic register or historic inventory properties.</span></h6></header><div class="track-indicator selected d-none">
<span class="glyphicons glyphicons-check" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="text">This section is included in your selections.</span>
</div>




<p class="heading">Where a proposed development would be on or adjacent to a property on the local, state or national historic register or local historic inventory or within an historic district on the register, the following regulations shall apply:</p>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.230(A)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.230(A)" data-short-cite="12.14.230(A)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">A.&#x2002;</span></span>If the proposed development would entail any changes to a structure on an historic register or demolition of a structure on the local historic inventory, such proposed changes or demolition shall be subject to the procedures and criteria set forth in Chapter <a class="cite" href="22.28" title="Ch. 22.28&#x2002;Changes to Property on Register or Inventory">22.28</a> BMC, Changes to Property on Register or Inventory.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.230(B)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.230(B)" data-short-cite="12.14.230(B)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">B.&#x2002;</span></span>The proposed development shall reflect the architectural character of adjacent and nearby historic properties by one or more of the following measures:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.230(B)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.230(B)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.230(B)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span></span>A similar unifying design concept which organizes a building&#x2019;s architectural elements into a harmonious composition;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.230(B)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.230(B)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.230(B)(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span></span>Similar building proportions, scale and roof line;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.230(B)(3)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.230(B)(3)" data-short-cite="12.14.230(B)(3)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">3.&#x2002;</span></span>Similar architectural style and exterior finish materials;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.230(B)(4)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.230(B)(4)" data-short-cite="12.14.230(B)(4)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">4.&#x2002;</span></span>Similar patterns and proportions of windows;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.230(B)(5)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.230(B)(5)" data-short-cite="12.14.230(B)(5)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">5.&#x2002;</span></span>Similar entry configuration and relationship to the street;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.230(B)(6)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.230(B)(6)" data-short-cite="12.14.230(B)(6)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">6.&#x2002;</span></span>Similar architectural details or features (such as cornices or trellises). <span class="note history history--linked" data-action="click-&gt;levelnodes#openHistory">(Ord. 1815 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 2000; Ord. 1798 &#xA7;&#xA0;1, 2000. Formerly 12.14.240).</span></p>



</section>
</section>





</article>
<article class="level6 chunking-small type-Section has-history" id="12.14.240" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240" data-short-cite="12.14.240" data-history="/BMC/12.14.240/history"><header class=" full-header" data-type="Section" data-search="/BMC/12.14.240/search" data-share="/BMC/12.14.240/share" data-levelnodes-target="header"><h6 class=" inner-header"><span class="num">12.14.240</span> <span class="name">Exterior lighting.</span></h6></header><div class="track-indicator selected d-none">
<span class="glyphicons glyphicons-check" aria-hidden="true"></span><span class="text">This section is included in your selections.</span>
</div>




<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.240(A)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(A)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(A)"><p class="heading"><span class="header"><span class="num">A.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Purpose.</span> </span>The purpose of this section is to regulate exterior lighting by promoting the principles of good lighting design, including:</p>




<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(A)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(A)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(A)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span></span>Permit reasonable amounts of exterior lighting for night-time safety, utility, security, productivity, enjoyment and commerce;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(A)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(A)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(A)(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span></span>Minimize adverse off-site impacts including light trespass, glare, and night glow;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(A)(3)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(A)(3)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(A)(3)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">3.&#x2002;</span></span>Conserve energy and resources to the greatest extent possible; and</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(A)(4)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(A)(4)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(A)(4)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">4.&#x2002;</span></span>Curtail light pollution and preserve the night-time environment.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.240(B)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(B)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(B)"><p><span class="header"><span class="num">B.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Definitions.</span> </span>The following definitions are listed in alphabetical order for the purpose of exterior lighting regulations, and shall apply to the administration of this section. In addition, except as otherwise provided in this section, those definitions as contained in Chapter <a class="cite" href="11.02" title="Ch. 11.02&#x2002;Definitions">11.02</a> BMC are adopted and incorporated into this section by reference.</p>




<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__b26c45d220d5bda0e231ea76ab0c9ebf">
<p>&#x201C;Adjacent grade&#x201D; means the grade directly below a luminaire (light fixture) on a plumb line or nearest grade thereto based upon city-approved finish grade.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__3fa0cf53ac656340f28f2498b35eefbc">
<p>&#x201C;Astronomic time switch&#x201D; means an automatic lighting control device that operates as an on/off switch for outdoor lighting relative to the time of solar day with time of year correction.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__d402768c56199dbe3b579ab554128675">
<p>&#x201C;Canopy&#x201D; means a covered, unconditional structure with at least one side open for pedestrian and/or vehicular access.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__9210b30e5281447ff4d025061191299a">
<p>&#x201C;Common outdoor areas&#x201D; means one or more of the following: a common parking for three or more domiciles or buildings; a common parking garage or covering entrance intended to be used by three or more domiciles or buildings; a common entrance for three or more domiciles or buildings.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__930324b326fa4e4d404a6dffcfc510d2">
<p>&#x201C;Curfew&#x201D; means a time defined by the authority when outdoor lighting is reduced to a specified maximum level or extinguished.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__3a9bff69b3c90b46178fec93b8f309fc">
<p>&#x201C;Emergency conditions&#x201D; means a loss of electrical power, fire, security alarm, or other situation requiring uninterrupted illumination for the path of egress.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__6d9c1c0d90447faf12bc59be7037545b">
<p>&#x201C;Fully shielded luminaire&#x201D; means a luminaire typically with opaque top and sides, capable of only emitting light in the lower photometric hemisphere as installed.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__fed3a51674af17c571f820d4664a5744">
<p>&#x201C;Glare&#x201D; means a light entering the eye directly from luminaires that causes visual discomfort or reduced visibility.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__45168fbd7b6a5911797d0c4c42b9dd72">
<p>&#x201C;Hardscape area&#x201D; means area in square feet of all hardscape including any medians, walkways, landscape areas 10 feet or less in width within the hardscape area.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__9e842afef39e65022946b3af52338c49">
<p>&#x201C;Hardscape perimeter&#x201D; means a perimeter in linear feet of all hardscape outside perimeter plus perimeter around buildings and structures greater than 10 feet in width.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__740379fdc42b5ce6c1f4034539c49893">
<p>&#x201C;IDA&#x201D; means the International Dark-Sky Association.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__a9845584158759efc6d1bbce9cda0101">
<p>&#x201C;IESNA&#x201D; means the Illuminating Engineering Society of North America.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__1bb02fbbb643bd93f111c0a71acbd183">
<p>&#x201C;Illuminated area&#x201D; means an exterior area for which lighting of reasonable uniformity and illumination is provided; not incidentally lighted or partially lighted.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__48085898476e5d88ad74b106cdb18e0a">
<p>&#x201C;Improved area&#x201D; means the area of a specific use, measured in plain view.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__731c3cae7595b0e26d739b47bbccc4cc">
<p>&#x201C;Initial lamp lumens&#x201D; means the lumen rating of a lamp when the lamp is new and has not depreciated in light output (rated lamp lumens). Lamp lumen depreciation equals 1.0.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__ae5770ba852b3857ca0e3f6b0a1d5a4f">
<p>&#x201C;Intended manner&#x201D; means the manner of use of the product generally as listed, advertised and/or per manufacturer&#x2019;s standard installation instructions.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__b1ea096128c59cb62f3c26fd2b48cacc">
<p>&#x201C;Lamp&#x201D; means a generic term for a source created to produce optical radiation (i.e., &#x201C;light&#x201D;), often called a bulb or tube.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__709265f802674d8025e804f134983fd9">
<p>&#x201C;Lamp watts&#x201D; means the rated watts of the lamp, not including the watts of external auxiliaries.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__af4efc76569bbc300362280f9c1eaceb">
<p>&#x201C;Landscape lighting&#x201D; means lighting not mounted to poles or buildings, for the purpose of illumination of trees, shrubbery and other natural external elements.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__b6255712654f0894e7b10813ea6f79d5">
<p>&#x201C;Light pollution&#x201D; means light trespass, and excessive light levels, beyond what is necessary to perform the defined task.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__fa05371df14164cfc7c24ac3b2ee0a15">
<p>&#x201C;Light trespass&#x201D; means unwanted light that falls on neighboring properties.</p>
</section>
