<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__3bd40b91d10d116775b8670e94e27978">
<p>&#x201C;Lighting equipment&#x201D; means equipment specifically intended to provide electric illumination, including, but not limited to, luminaire(s), poles, posts, and related structures, electrical wiring, and other necessary or auxiliary components.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__30496bbad36c25108cd86958a084be1e">
<p>&#x201C;Lighting system&#x201D; means on a site, all exterior artificial lighting sources, associated infrastructure and controls.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__fea8add2c4f7f1e8fd0441ea58e8760a">
<p>&#x201C;Lighting zone (LZ)&#x201D; means a designation assigned by the city for specified parcels, areas or districts defining allowable ambient lighting levels, operational characteristics and other control criteria.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__223cbf87c27d05701fd7b069f6eda5fd">
<p>&#x201C;Low voltage landscape lighting&#x201D; means electric lighting powered at less than 24 volts and limited to lamps of 50 watts or less, not mounted to poles or buildings, for the purpose of illuminating trees, shrubbery and other natural external elements.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__364eff18473ac9eb08ab33eeef3b6abf">
<p>&#x201C;Lumens (lm)&#x201D; means an international unit of luminous flux; light power corrected for V&#x3BB;, the human photopic sensitivity function.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__9a2a54e37393c7a895ee861d7b8d8bdb">
<p>&#x201C;Luminaire&#x201D; means the complete lighting unit assembly (entire fixture), consisting of a lamp, or lamps and ballast(s), drivers or transformers (when applicable), together with the parts designated to distribute the light (reflector, lens, diffuser), to position and protect the lamps, and to connect the lamps to the power supply.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__75907c6d5a3e577c4014e0fea66fbeb9">
<p>&#x201C;Mounting height&#x201D; means the height of a luminaire above finished grade level. The horizontal spacing of poles is often measured in units of &#x201C;mounting height.&#x201D; Example: &#x201C;The luminaires can be spaced up to four mounting heights apart.&#x201D;</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__395df3a96db589540746be1b99ab2c62">
<p>&#x201C;New lighting&#x201D; means lighting for areas not previously illuminated; newly installed lighting of any type except for replacement lighting or lighting repairs.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__0d2d674261522402ce9068a0b3172d2c">
<p>&#x201C;Nontradable surface&#x201D; means those surfaces where lighting power allowance amounts may be used only for the specific application identified within subsection <a class="cite" href="12.14.240(F)" title="12.14.240(F)">F</a> of this section and Table 12.14-2 and cannot be transferred to other surfaces or be used as a group.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__8fced806578c478a447aa7613d45ddb8">
<p>&#x201C;Opaque&#x201D; means a solid material not allowing light to pass through.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__6069f5f76efc28028ab69898dfb45fd7">
<p>&#x201C;Ornamental lighting&#x201D; means lighting that is not a sign and does not impact the function and safety of an area but is purely decorative, or used to illuminate architecture and/or landscaping, and installed for aesthetic effect.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__12a5638e65af5b4f277ef29e924d20f1">
<p>&#x201C;Partly shielded luminaire&#x201D; means a luminaire in which the lamp is shielded by a translucent shade as to prevent light from being directly emitted by the lamp or reflector into the upper photometric hemisphere.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__1f464f8746cbcf0e0ba4ab919f801b3d">
<p>&#x201C;Photoelectric switch&#x201D; means a control device employing a photocell or photodiode to detect daylight and automatically switch lights off by day.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__2aac2288e554bd742d1fa69eed58a3f9">
<p>&#x201C;Project&#x201D; means installation of a lighting system under a single electrical permit or for a specific construction project, multiple permits when required for phased construction.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__de0b9992f6ca73b07e03296eca992e87">
<p>&#x201C;Property line&#x201D; means the boundary line, or lot line between platted or unplatted lots or both, defining the edges of a legally defined piece of property.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__692fbe95c6c8ccfe16cef8f351b59050">
<p>&#x201C;Replacement lighting&#x201D; means lighting installed specifically to replace existing lighting equipment that is sufficiently inoperable to be beyond repair(s).</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__d8110ed26aae68033c81ea42a9313afa">
<p>&#x201C;Repair(s)&#x201D; means the reconstruction or renewal of any part of an existing luminaire for the purpose of its ongoing operation, including but not limited to relamping or replacement of components such as capacitor, ballast, or photoelectric control.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__cc7b3a747ba4c73794e1ae90445b3837">
<p>&#x201C;Residential luminaire&#x201D; means luminaires used solely for compliance with Section V.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__3c1103a095db93a1cb4b343faf8e6f4a">
<p>&#x201C;Right-of-way&#x201D; means the land, property, or interest therein acquired for or devoted to transportation purposes.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__9e97e6aa1dd21b317ff82c161ce44cc2">
<p>&#x201C;Sales area&#x201D; means exterior areas used for sales of retail goods and materials, including but not limited to automobiles, recreational vehicles, trailers, boats, building supplies, and gardening and nursery products.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__69dcb7eee65a5fa509e5e7db0c79eb90">
<p>&#x201C;Seasonal lighting&#x201D; means temporary lighting installed and operated in connection with holidays, community celebrations or traditions.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__d5e50b73cbaabc66e6b7bd40cc373fa0">
<p>&#x201C;Service yard&#x201D; means uncovered exterior area specifically used for vehicular, marine or aviation service or for outdoor storage and/or loading of goods and materials.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__5f86b34af43102333d350cc078c29174">
<p>&#x201C;Shielded directional luminaire&#x201D; means a fully shielded luminaire with an adjustable mounting device allowing aiming in a direction other than straight downward.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__0d0273facd1755b2bf6752ce15e2369a">
<p>&#x201C;Sign&#x201D; is a communication device, structure, or fixture which incorporates graphics, symbols, or written copy for the purpose of conveying a particular message to public observers.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__e52e43bb16407775231c156ca7db1e11">
<p>&#x201C;Site&#x201D; means a geographical area delineated by specific dimensions and coordinates or a complete land parcel defined by designated property boundaries as recorded by the authority.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__2e11f88390ad7b7a8f06353e1d1a296e">
<p>&#x201C;Sky glow&#x201D; means the illumination of clouds, moisture and airborne matter by lighting.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__a93bf12ccf995b3aed8e54834413c087">
<p>&#x201C;Temporary lighting&#x201D; means lighting installed and operated for periods not to exceed 60 days, completely removed and not operated again for at least 30 days.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__6e5f34955736179a7dc1e53d5dd71720">
<p>&#x201C;Third party&#x201D; means a party contracted to provide lighting, such as a utility company.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__a8c710dd5501da56f1bed61804d6e07c">
<p>&#x201C;Time switch&#x201D; means an automatic lighting control device that operates as an on/off switch for outdoor lighting according to time of day.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__dc14ed585c9cd91dd44ebb84083ef646">
<p>&#x201C;Tradable surface&#x201D; means those surfaces where lighting power allowances may be traded or transferred from one surface to another surface resulting in an increase of the power allowance on one surface and a decrease of power allowance on other surfaces. Tradable surface power allowances may be added together used as a group. See subsection <a class="cite" href="12.14.240(F)" title="12.14.240(F)">F</a> of this section and Table 12.14-1.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__3a786a3ca58008584a51aa6ebff1f4d6">
<p>&#x201C;Translucent&#x201D; means a material allowing light to pass through while obscuring or diffusing the lamp.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__5e1defacf3b509dbe0f592a66a52496d">
<p>&#x201C;Uplight&#x201D; means, for an exterior luminaire, light emitted in the hemisphere at or above the horizontal plane.</p>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(B)__0d3ede38f3f56a5386440a36127c441f">
<p>&#x201C;WS NREC&#x201D; means the Washington State Non-Residential Energy Code (current version).</p>
</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.240(C)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(C)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(C)"><p><span class="header"><span class="num">C.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Applicability.</span> </span>These regulations shall be applicable to all exterior lighting whether attached to structures, poles, the earth, or any other location, installed in conjunction with development applications, including building permits, grading permits, utility permits, shorelines substantial development permits, conditional use permits, tenant improvement permits, and right-of-way invasion permits except as provided in subsection <a class="cite" href="12.14.240(D)" title="12.14.240(D)&#x2002;Exemptions">D</a> of this section. Provided, however, that different land uses and development scale may be subject to specific exterior lighting provisions as provided below. Where the exterior lighting regulations within this section are in conflict with exterior lighting provisions of a subarea zoning regulation, or the conditions of approval for a conditional use permit or planned unit development, the subarea zoning regulations or conditions of approval shall control.</p>




</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.240(D)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(D)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(D)"><p class="heading"><span class="header"><span class="num">D.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Exemptions.</span> </span>The following exterior lighting is exempt from the provisions of this section:</p>




<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(D)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(D)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(D)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span></span>Lighting equipment placed within public rights-of-way intended solely for roadway and sidewalk illumination;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(D)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(D)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(D)(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span></span>Lighting equipment used exclusively for signs;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(D)(3)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(D)(3)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(D)(3)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">3.&#x2002;</span></span>Lighting equipment necessary for emergency situations and law enforcement actions;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(D)(4)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(D)(4)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(D)(4)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">4.&#x2002;</span></span>Temporary lighting installed for holidays and celebrations; provided, that individual lamps are 10 watts or less;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(D)(5)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(D)(5)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(D)(5)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">5.&#x2002;</span></span>Traffic control signals and devices;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(D)(6)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(D)(6)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(D)(6)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">6.&#x2002;</span></span>Low voltage landscape lighting equipment controlled by a photoelectric switch or astronomic time switch;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(D)(7)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(D)(7)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(D)(7)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">7.&#x2002;</span></span>Lighting equipment in swimming pools, spas, fountains, and other water features;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(D)(8)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(D)(8)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(D)(8)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">8.&#x2002;</span></span>Temporary lighting equipment for theatrical, television, performance areas and construction sites;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(D)(9)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(D)(9)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(D)(9)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">9.&#x2002;</span></span>Repairs to existing lighting equipment; provided, all light fixtures repaired or replaced shall comply with basic light glare and light pollution standards as detailed within subsection <a class="cite" href="12.14.240(H)" title="12.14.240(H)&#x2002;Illumination Standards">H</a> of this section;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(D)(10)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(D)(10)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(D)(10)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">10.&#x2002;</span></span>Lighting equipment for public monuments, flags, and statuary; and</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(D)(11)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(D)(11)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(D)(11)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">11.&#x2002;</span></span>Existing lighting equipment installed on or before January 22, 2011, for the following minor building or site alterations:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.240(D)(11)(a)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(D)(11)(a)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(D)(11)(a)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span></span>Tenant improvements that do not alter the building exterior;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.240(D)(11)(b)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(D)(11)(b)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(D)(11)(b)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span></span>Alterations or remodels that do not alter the building exterior;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.240(D)(11)(c)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(D)(11)(c)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(D)(11)(c)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">c.&#x2002;</span></span>Improvements to parking lots that do not increase the number of parking stalls;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.240(D)(11)(d)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(D)(11)(d)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(D)(11)(d)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">d.&#x2002;</span></span>Changes in use when the change is for uses listed within the same use table within Chapter <a class="cite" href="12.06" title="Ch. 12.06&#x2002;Permitted Uses">12.06</a> BMC. For example, BMC <a class="cite" href="12.06.050" title="12.06.050&#x2002;Business and personal services">12.06.050</a>, Business and personal services, lists a number of similar uses, including architectural/engineering and banking and financial services. When the change in use is from one business and personal use to another business and personal use the change in use is exempt from these lighting regulations.</p>



</section>
</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.240(E)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(E)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(E)"><p class="heading"><span class="header"><span class="num">E.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Lighting Zones.</span> </span>The city of Bothell has established lighting zones (LZ) which limits the amount of electrical energy used for exterior lighting as specified in subsection <a class="cite" href="12.14.240(F)" title="12.14.240(F)">F</a> of this section and Tables 12.14-1 and 12.14-2 (see Figure 12.14-20 for a map of zone locations). The descriptive criteria for each lighting zone shall be as follows:</p>




<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(E)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(E)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(E)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span></span>Lighting Zone 1 (LZ1). Low ambient lighting including developed areas of national parks, state parks, forest land, and rural areas. No Lighting Zone 1 areas have been identified within the city of Bothell;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(E)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(E)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(E)(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span></span>Lighting Zone 2 (LZ2). Moderate ambient lighting areas including lands predominately consisting of residential zoning, neighborhood business districts, light industrial with limited night-time use and residential mixed use areas. These areas are depicted on Figure 12.14-20 and include the following zoning classifications: R-C; R-L1; R-L2; R-M1; R-M2; R-M3; R-M4; OP; OP; R-M4, OP; MHP; SSHO; R-C (LID); R-L1 (LID), R-M3 (LID); C; SVV; PPOS; DT; and OP, LI;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(E)(3)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(E)(3)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(E)(3)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">3.&#x2002;</span></span>Lighting Zone 3 (LZ3). Moderately high ambient lighting areas such as commercial districts. These areas are depicted on Figure 12.14-20 and include the following zoning classifications: CB; R-M4, OP, CB; R-M4, OP, NB; R-AC, OP, CB, LI; R-AC, OP, CB, MVSO; OP, CB, MVSO; OP, CB, LI, MVSO; SR-522; and GDC; and</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(E)(4)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(E)(4)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(E)(4)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">4.&#x2002;</span></span>Lighting Zone 4 (LZ4). High ambient lighting including high activity commercial districts in major metropolitan areas. No Lighting Zone 4 areas have been identified within the city of Bothell. However, pursuant to subsection <a class="cite" href="12.14.240(I)(2)(b)" title="12.14.240(I)(2)(b)&#x2002;Lighting Standards for Larger Developments">(I)(2)(b)</a> of this section, an applicant of a large development may request Lighting Zone 4 high ambient lighting energy levels;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(E)(5)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(E)(5)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(E)(5)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">5.&#x2002;</span></span>Lighting zone power allowances shall be based upon subsection <a class="cite" href="12.14.240(F)" title="12.14.240(F)">F</a> of this section and Tables 12.14-1 and 12.14-2.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.240(F)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(F)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(F)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">F.&#x2002;</span></span>Exterior Lighting Power Allowances (ANSI/ASHRAE/IESNA 90.1). The city of Bothell is utilizing the exterior lighting power allowances as promulgated by the American National Standards Institute/American Society of Hearing Refrigeration and Air Conditioning Engineers/Illuminating Engineering Society of North America (ANSI/ASHRAE/IESNA) Standard 90.1 to promote energy efficiency and reduce power consumption associated with exterior lighting. Certain modifications to the standard have been incorporated to accommodate lighting zones as described in subsection <a class="cite" href="12.14.240(E)" title="12.14.240(E)&#x2002;Lighting Zones">E</a> of this section.</p>



<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(F)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(F)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(F)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span></span>The total exterior lighting power allowance for exterior areas shall be the sum of the individual lighting power densities specified in Tables 12.14-1 and 12.14-2 plus the base power allotment specified for the lighting zone of the subject property.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(F)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(F)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(F)(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span></span>Transference or trade-offs of lighting power densities may only occur between exterior surfaces listed as tradable surfaces in Table 12.14-1.</p>



<figure class="type-Table">
<table class="role-medium table-ruled-outside-horizontal table-ruled-inside-horizontal"><caption><p class="header"><span class="name">Table 12.14-1 Lighting Power Allowances Tradable Surfaces</span></p>


</caption><colgroup><col style="width: 123.7pt;"/><col style="width: 99pt;"/><col style="width: 76pt;"/><col style="width: 76pt;"/><col style="width: 76pt;"/>
</colgroup><thead>
<tr>
<th colspan="2"></th>
<th>
<p><b>Zone 2</b></p>
</th>
<th>
<p><b>Zone 3</b></p>
</th>
<th>
<p><b>Zone 4</b></p>
</th>
</tr>
</thead><tbody>
<tr>
<td class="center" rowspan="8">
<p><b>Base Site Allowance</b> (All properties are allowed the base site allowance and may use the base site allowance for either tradable or nontradable surfaces but cannot be combined)</p>
</td>
<td></td>
<td class="center">
<p>600 W</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>750 W</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>1300 W</p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td class="center">
<p><b>Building Grounds</b></p>
</td>
<td colspan="3"></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td class="center">
<p>Walkways less than 10 feet wide</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>0.7 W/linear foot</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>0.8 W/linear foot</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>1.0 W/linear foot</p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td class="center">
<p>Walkways 10 feet wide or greater</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>0.14 W/ft<sup>2</sup></p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>0.16 W/ft<sup>2</sup></p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>0.2 W/ft<sup>2</sup></p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td class="center">
<p>Plaza areas</p>
</td>
<td colspan="3" rowspan="3"></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td class="center">
<p>Special feature areas</p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td class="center">
<p><b>Uncovered Parking Areas</b></p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td class="center">
<p>Parking areas and drives</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>0.06 W/ft<sup>2</sup></p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>0.10 W/ft<sup>2</sup></p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>0.15 W/ft<sup>2</sup></p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td class="center" rowspan="4">
<p><b>Tradable Surfaces</b> (Lighting power densities for uncovered parking areas, building grounds, building entrances and exits, canopies and overhangs and outdoor sales areas may be traded)</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p><b>Building Entrances and Exits</b></p>
</td>
<td colspan="3"></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td class="center">
<p>Main entries</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>20 W/linear foot of door width</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>30 W/linear foot of door width</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>30 W/linear foot of door width</p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td class="center">
<p>Other doors</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>20 W/linear foot of door width</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>20 W/linear foot of door width</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>20 W/linear foot of door width</p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td class="center">
<p>Entry canopies</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>0.25 W/ft<sup>2</sup></p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>0.4 W/ft<sup>2</sup></p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>0.4 W/ft<sup>2</sup></p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td rowspan="5"></td>
<td class="center">
<p><b>Sales Canopies</b></p>
</td>
<td colspan="3"></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td class="center">
<p>Freestanding and attached</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>0.8 W/ft<sup>2</sup></p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>0.8 W/ft<sup>2</sup></p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>1.0 W/ft<sup>2</sup></p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td class="center">
<p><b>Outdoor Sales</b></p>
</td>
<td colspan="3"></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td class="center">
<p>Open areas (including vehicle sales lots)</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>0.5 W/ft<sup>2</sup></p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>0.5 W/ft<sup>2</sup></p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>0.5 W/ft<sup>2</sup></p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td class="center">
<p>Street frontage for vehicle sales lots in addition to open area allowance</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>10 W/linear foot</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>10 W/linear foot</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>10 W/linear foot</p>
</td>
</tr>
</tbody></table>
</figure>
<figure class="type-Table">
<table class="role-medium table-ruled-outside-horizontal table-ruled-inside-horizontal"><caption><p class="header"><span class="name">Table 12.14-2 Lighting Power Allowances Nontradable Surfaces</span>&#xA0;</p>


</caption><colgroup><col style="width: 123.7pt;"/><col style="width: 99pt;"/><col style="width: 76pt;"/><col style="width: 76pt;"/><col style="width: 76pt;"/>
</colgroup><thead>
<tr>
<th colspan="2"></th>
<th>
<p><b>Zone 2</b></p>
</th>
<th>
<p><b>Zone 3</b></p>
</th>
<th>
<p><b>Zone 4</b></p>
</th>
</tr>
</thead><tbody>
<tr>
<td class="center">
<p><b>Base Site Allowance </b>(All properties are allowed the base site allowance and may use the base site allowance for either tradable or nontradable surfaces but cannot be combined)</p>
</td>
<td></td>
<td class="center">
<p>600 W</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>750 W</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>1300 W</p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td class="center" rowspan="6">
<p><b>Nontradable Surfaces</b> (Lighting power density calculations for the following applications can be used only for the specific application and cannot be traded between surfaces or with other exterior lighting. The following allowances are in addition to any allowance otherwise permitted in the &#x201C;Tradable Surfaces&#x201D; section of this table)</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p><b>Building Facades</b></p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>0.1 W/ft<sup>2</sup> for each illuminated wall or surface or 2.5 W/linear foot for each illuminated wall or surface length</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>0.15 W/ft<sup>2</sup> for each illuminated wall or surface or 3.75 W/linear foot for each illuminated wall or surface length</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>0.2 W/ft<sup>2</sup> for each illuminated wall or surface or 3.75 W/linear foot for each illuminated wall or surface length</p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td class="center">
<p><b>Automated teller machines and night depositories</b></p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>270 W per location plus 90 W per additional ATM per location</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>270 W per location plus 90 W per additional ATM per location</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>270 W per location plus 90 W per additional ATM per location</p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td class="center">
<p><b>Entrances and gate-house inspections at guarded facilities</b></p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>1.25 W/ft<sup>2</sup> of covered and uncovered area</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>1.25 W/ft<sup>2</sup> of covered and uncovered area</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>1.25 W/ft<sup>2</sup> of covered and uncovered area</p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td class="center">
<p><b>Loading areas for law enforcement, fire, ambulance and other emergency service vehicles</b></p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>0.5 W/ft<sup>2</sup> of covered and uncovered area</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>0.5 W/ft<sup>2</sup> of covered and uncovered area</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>0.5 W/ft<sup>2</sup> of covered and uncovered area</p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td class="center">
<p><b>Drive-up windows/doors</b></p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>400 W per drive-through</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>400 W per drive-through</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>400 W per drive-through</p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td class="center">
<p><b>Parking near 24-hour retail entrances</b></p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>800 W per main entry</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>800 W per main entry</p>
</td>
<td class="center">
<p>800 W per main entry</p>
</td>
</tr>
</tbody></table>
</figure>
<figure class="type-Figure">
<figcaption><p class="header"><span class="name">Figure 12.14-20 Lighting Zones Map</span></p>


</figcaption>
<picture class="role-medium">
<img class="role-medium web-winner" src="media/12.14-20.png" data-rank="62" style="width: 430pt;"/>
</picture>
</figure>
</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.240(G)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(G)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(G)"><p class="heading"><span class="header"><span class="num">G.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Exterior Lighting Controls and Curfews.</span> </span>Exterior lighting shall be equipped with automatic controls and, for the specific uses identified below, curfews and automatic lighting reduction controls as consistent with the following:</p>




<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(G)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(G)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(G)(1)"><p><span class="header"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Automatic Control Requirements.</span> </span>All exterior lighting shall have controls that automatically extinguish all exterior lighting during daylight hours using a switching device such as a photoelectric switch, astronomic time switch or a control system such as a programmable lighting controller, building automation system, lighting energy management system or equivalent, per the requirement of WS NREC.</p>




</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(G)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(G)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(G)(2)"><p class="heading"><span class="header"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Curfew and Automatic Lighting Reduction Requirements.</span> </span>The city of Bothell has established curfew time(s) specific to business and/or use types, for all lighting systems installed after January 22, 2011, whereupon the total outdoor lighting lumens shall be uniformly reduced by a specified amount. Individual light fixtures shall not be extinguished to meet this curfew lighting reduction requirement. The following uses and project types shall have curfews as follows:</p>




<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.240(G)(2)(a)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(G)(2)(a)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(G)(2)(a)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span></span>Office, warehouse, manufacturing, light industrial and similar use exterior parking lots&#xA0;&#x2013; 10:00 p.m. to 6:00 a.m. or 30 minutes before opening. The lighting system must be uniformly reduced to a maximum of 25 percent of normal output during curfew times.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.240(G)(2)(b)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(G)(2)(b)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(G)(2)(b)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span></span>Retail and mixed use without residential uses exterior parking lots&#xA0;&#x2013; two hours after close of business hours. Lighting system must be uniformly reduced to a maximum of 25 percent of normal output during curfew times.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.240(G)(2)(c)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(G)(2)(c)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(G)(2)(c)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">c.&#x2002;</span></span>Mixed use containing residential uses, exterior parking lots&#xA0;&#x2013; 10:00 p.m. to 6:00 a.m. or 30 minutes before opening. The lighting system must be uniformly reduced to a maximum of 50 percent of normal output during curfew times.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.240(G)(2)(d)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(G)(2)(d)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(G)(2)(d)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">d.&#x2002;</span></span>Parking structures or garages and other types of covered parking areas&#xA0;&#x2013; no curfew; provided, that all light fixtures are fully shielded or obscured by building components such that the fixture is not visible from surrounding properties.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.240(G)(2)(e)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(G)(2)(e)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(G)(2)(e)"><p class="header"><span class="num">e.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Sports Fields.</span> </p>



<section class="subsec subsec4 chunking-none type-Subsec4" id="12.14.240(G)(2)(e)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(G)(2)(e)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(G)(2)(e)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(1)&#x2002;</span></span>Located on lands with detached residential zoning classifications of R-C, R-L1, and R-L2&#xA0;&#x2013; 10:00 p.m. to 6:00 a.m. unless a game is in progress. Games may not start after 8:00 p.m. Spectator, parking lot, and other non-sports field areas may remain fully illuminated for up to two hours after completion of the event. </p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec4 chunking-none type-Subsec4" id="12.14.240(G)(2)(e)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(G)(2)(e)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(G)(2)(e)(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(2)&#x2002;</span></span>Located on lands with all other zoning classifications&#xA0;&#x2013; 11:00 p.m. to 6:00 a.m. Spectator, parking lot, and other non-sports field areas may remain fully illuminated for up to two hours after completion of the event.</p>



</section>
</section>
</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.240(H)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(H)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(H)"><p><span class="header"><span class="num">H.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Illumination Standards.</span> </span>The city of Bothell requires that all exterior lighting satisfy the illumination level recommendations as established within the latest edition of the Illuminating Engineering Society of North America (IESNA) Lighting Handbook and associated recommended practice documents.</p>




</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.240(I)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(I)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(I)"><p class="header"><span class="num">I.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Requirements for Exterior Lighting.</span> </p>



<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(I)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(I)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(I)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span></span>The following exterior lighting requirements are applicable to all proposed development applications and existing developments, except outdoor performance, sport and recreation facilities and playfield lighting, which must comply with subsection <a class="cite" href="12.14.240(H)" title="12.14.240(H)&#x2002;Illumination Standards">H</a> of this section, and individual single-family residential structures, which must comply with subsection <a class="cite" href="12.14.240(J)" title="12.14.240(J)&#x2002;Lighting of Exterior Performance, Sport and Recreation Facilities and Playfields">J</a> of this section.</p>



<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.240(I)(1)(a)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(I)(1)(a)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(I)(1)(a)"><p class="header"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Submittal Requirements.</span> </p>



<section class="subsec subsec4 chunking-none type-Subsec4" id="12.14.240(I)(1)(a)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(I)(1)(a)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(I)(1)(a)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(1)&#x2002;</span></span>A site plan showing the proposed location, mounting height, aiming point, type, and size of all exterior lighting including building mounted, landscape, roadways, and parking lot lighting drawings should include property line or project limit line if part of a larger campus.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec4 chunking-none type-Subsec4" id="12.14.240(I)(1)(a)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(I)(1)(a)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(I)(1)(a)(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(2)&#x2002;</span></span>Manufacturer fixture specification sheets or cut-sheets for all fixture types. Fixture cut sheets must be labeled with fixture type and all fixture features and accessories must be defined and an image of the fixture included.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec4 chunking-none type-Subsec4" id="12.14.240(I)(1)(a)(3)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(I)(1)(a)(3)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(I)(1)(a)(3)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(3)&#x2002;</span></span>A fixture schedule including quantity and type of lamps.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec4 chunking-none type-Subsec4" id="12.14.240(I)(1)(a)(4)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(I)(1)(a)(4)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(I)(1)(a)(4)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(4)&#x2002;</span></span>If building elevations are to be illuminated, submit building elevation drawings showing the location and type of all fixtures, the area of the building elevation to be illuminated, the illumination levels, and the aiming point for all light fixtures.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec4 chunking-none type-Subsec4" id="12.14.240(I)(1)(a)(5)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(I)(1)(a)(5)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(I)(1)(a)(5)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(5)&#x2002;</span></span>If building mounted fixtures are used, submit building elevations showing the location of each fixture.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec4 chunking-none type-Subsec4" id="12.14.240(I)(1)(a)(6)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(I)(1)(a)(6)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(I)(1)(a)(6)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(6)&#x2002;</span></span>All drawings must have fixture type indicators.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec4 chunking-none type-Subsec4" id="12.14.240(I)(1)(a)(7)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(I)(1)(a)(7)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(I)(1)(a)(7)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(7)&#x2002;</span></span>The community development director may require submittal of additional information demonstrating the objectives of the lighting design including:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec5 chunking-none type-Subsec5" id="12.14.240(I)(1)(a)(7)(A)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(I)(1)(a)(7)(A)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(I)(1)(a)(7)(A)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(A)&#x2002;</span></span>A brief written narrative; and/or</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec5 chunking-none type-Subsec5" id="12.14.240(I)(1)(a)(7)(B)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(I)(1)(a)(7)(B)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(I)(1)(a)(7)(B)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(B)&#x2002;</span></span>Supplemental drawings.</p>



</section>
</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.240(I)(1)(b)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(I)(1)(b)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(I)(1)(b)"><p class="header"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Fixture Requirements.</span> </p>



<section class="subsec subsec4 chunking-none type-Subsec4" id="12.14.240(I)(1)(b)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(I)(1)(b)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(I)(1)(b)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(1)&#x2002;</span></span>All fixtures must be similar to those identified as acceptable within Figures 12.14-21, 12.14-22, and 12.14-23;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec4 chunking-none type-Subsec4" id="12.14.240(I)(1)(b)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(I)(1)(b)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(I)(1)(b)(2)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(2)&#x2002;</span></span>The maximum mounting pole height of exterior light fixtures shall be consistent with the following schedule:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec5 chunking-none type-Subsec5" id="12.14.240(I)(1)(b)(2)(A)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(I)(1)(b)(2)(A)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(I)(1)(b)(2)(A)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(A)&#x2002;</span></span>Within Lighting Zone 2: 15 feet above city-approved finish grade.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec5 chunking-none type-Subsec5" id="12.14.240(I)(1)(b)(2)(B)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(I)(1)(b)(2)(B)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(I)(1)(b)(2)(B)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(B)&#x2002;</span></span>Within developments classified as large under subsection <a class="cite" href="12.14.240(I)(2)" title="12.14.240(I)(2)">(I)(2)</a> of this section located within Lighting Zone 2: 20 feet above city-approved finish grade.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec5 chunking-none type-Subsec5" id="12.14.240(I)(1)(b)(2)(C)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(I)(1)(b)(2)(C)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(I)(1)(b)(2)(C)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(C)&#x2002;</span></span>Within Lighting Zone 3: 20 feet above city-approved finish grade.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec5 chunking-none type-Subsec5" id="12.14.240(I)(1)(b)(2)(D)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(I)(1)(b)(2)(D)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(I)(1)(b)(2)(D)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(D)&#x2002;</span></span>Within developments classified as large under subsection <a class="cite" href="12.14.240(I)(2)" title="12.14.240(I)(2)">(I)(2)</a> of this section located within Lighting Zone 3: 25 feet above city-approved finish grade.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec5 chunking-none type-Subsec5" id="12.14.240(I)(1)(b)(2)(E)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(I)(1)(b)(2)(E)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(I)(1)(b)(2)(E)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(E)&#x2002;</span></span>Within properties which have been assigned a Lighting Zone 4 classification by the community development director per subsection <a class="cite" href="12.14.240(I)(2)(b)" title="12.14.240(I)(2)(b)&#x2002;Lighting Standards for Larger Developments">(I)(2)(b)</a> of this section: 25 feet above city-approved finish grade.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec4 chunking-none type-Subsec4" id="12.14.240(I)(1)(b)(3)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(I)(1)(b)(3)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(I)(1)(b)(3)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(3)&#x2002;</span></span>All light fixtures within two mounting pole heights of any adjacent property (excluding public rights-of-way) that are not building mounted must be facing in toward the project and have house-side shields consistent with Figure 12.14-25 installed on the side of the fixture nearest the adjacent property. (See Figure 12.14-25).</p>



<figure class="type-Table">
<table class="role-medium table-ruled-outside-both table-ruled-inside-both"><caption><p class="header"><span class="name">Figure 12.14-21 Acceptable and unacceptable light fixtures</span>&#xA0;</p>


</caption><colgroup><col style="width: 112.7pt;"/><col style="width: 112.7pt;"/><col style="width: 112.7pt;"/><col style="width: 112.7pt;"/>
</colgroup><thead>
<tr>
<th>
<p>Acceptable fixtures</p>
</th>
<th>
<p>Comments</p>
</th>
<th>
<p>Not acceptable fixtures</p>
</th>
<th>
<p>Comments</p>
</th>
</tr>
</thead><tbody>
<tr>
<td>
<picture class="role-small">
<img class="role-small web-winner" src="media/12_14-21a1.png" data-rank="42" style="width: 84.8pt;"/>
</picture>
</td>
<td>
<p>Example of ceiling mounted fixture</p>
</td>
<td>
<picture class="role-small">
<img class="role-small web-winner" src="media/12_14-21a2.png" data-rank="42" style="width: 68.3pt;"/>
</picture>
</td>
<td>
<p>Surface mounted square fixture with exposed lamp and lens</p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>
<picture class="role-small">
<img class="role-small web-winner" src="media/12_14-21b1.png" data-rank="42" style="width: 73.5pt;"/>
</picture>
</td>
<td>
<p>Lamp concealed in opaque upper portion. Clear flat glass lens</p>
</td>
<td>
<picture class="role-small">
<img class="role-small web-winner" src="media/12_14-21b2.png" data-rank="42" style="width: 38.3pt;"/>
</picture>
</td>
<td>
<p>Translucent white lens with decorative metal grill work is not acceptable</p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>
<picture class="role-small">
<img class="role-small web-winner" src="media/12_14-21c1.png" data-rank="42" style="width: 84.8pt;"/>
</picture>
</td>
<td>
<p>Solid, opaque sides and top. Flat clear glass lens</p>
</td>
<td>
<picture class="role-small">
<img class="role-small web-winner" src="media/12_14-21c2.png" data-rank="42" style="width: 85.5pt;"/>
</picture>
</td>
<td>
<p>Vertical lamp with dropped lens is not acceptable</p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>
<picture class="role-small">
<img class="role-small web-winner" src="media/12_14-21d1.png" data-rank="42" style="width: 67.5pt;"/>
</picture>
</td>
<td>
<p>Flat lens and horizontal top is acceptable</p>
</td>
<td>
<picture class="role-small">
<img class="role-small web-winner" src="media/12_14-21d2.png" data-rank="42" style="width: 70.5pt;"/>
</picture>
</td>
<td>
<p>Clear, translucent, white or clear textured with or without an internal refractor is unacceptable</p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>
<picture class="role-small">
<img class="role-small web-winner" src="media/12_14-21e1.png" data-rank="42" style="width: 84.8pt;"/>
</picture>
</td>
<td>
<p>Clear flat lens. Lamp concealed within opaque portion of fixture at less than 5 degrees above the horizontal</p>
</td>
<td>
<picture class="role-small">
<img class="role-small web-winner" src="media/12_14-21e2.png" data-rank="42" style="width: 96.8pt;"/>
</picture>
</td>
<td>
<p>Fixture aimed above the horizontal is not acceptable</p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>
<picture class="role-small">
<img class="role-small web-winner" src="media/12_14-21f1.png" data-rank="42" style="width: 75.8pt;"/>
</picture>
</td>
<td>
<p>Bollard lamp concealed by opaque louvers aimed in a downward direction</p>
</td>
<td>
<picture class="role-small">
<img class="role-small web-winner" src="media/12_14-21f2.png" data-rank="42" style="width: 30.8pt;"/>
</picture>
</td>
<td>
<p>Bollards with translucent lens with decorative grill-work directed horizontally are not acceptable</p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>
<picture class="role-small">
<img class="role-small web-winner" src="media/12_14-21g1.png" data-rank="42" style="width: 93.8pt;"/>
</picture>
</td>
<td>
<p>Fixture has opaque shields with flat lens</p>
</td>
<td>
<picture class="role-small">
<img class="role-small web-winner" src="media/12_14-21g2.png" data-rank="42" style="width: 80.3pt;"/>
</picture>
</td>
<td>
<p>Standard yard or barn light</p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>
<picture class="role-small">
<img class="role-small web-winner" src="media/12_14-21h1.png" data-rank="42" style="width: 85.5pt;"/>
</picture>
</td>
<td>
<p>Flat lens fixtures with lamp concealed in top opaque portion</p>
</td>
<td>
<picture class="role-small">
<img class="role-small web-winner" src="media/12_14-21h2.png" data-rank="42" style="width: 68.3pt;"/>
</picture>
</td>
<td>
<p>Lamp below opaque portion of fixture is not acceptable</p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>
<picture class="role-small">
<img class="role-small web-winner" src="media/12_14-21i1.png" data-rank="42" style="width: 75.8pt;"/>
</picture>
</td>
<td>
<p>Full cut-off wall pack fixture</p>
</td>
<td>
<picture class="role-small">
<img class="role-small web-winner" src="media/12_14-21i2.png" data-rank="42" style="width: 50.3pt;"/>
</picture>
</td>
<td>
<p>Perimeter wall pack with exposed lens or lamp</p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>
<picture class="role-small">
<img class="role-small web-winner" src="media/12_14-21j1.png" data-rank="42" style="width: 98.3pt;"/>
</picture>
</td>
<td>
<p>Flat glass lens. Lamp completely concealed within opaque fixture</p>
</td>
<td>
<picture class="role-small">
<img class="role-small web-winner" src="media/12_14-21j2.png" data-rank="42" style="width: 72pt;"/>
</picture>
</td>
<td>
<p>Compact refractor wall pack with exposed lens or lamp</p>
</td>
</tr>
</tbody></table>
</figure>
<figure class="type-Table">
<table class="role-medium table-ruled-outside-both table-ruled-inside-both"><caption><p class="header"><span class="name">Figure 12.14-22 Acceptable fixtures subject to special requirements</span>&#xA0;</p>


</caption><colgroup><col style="width: 193.3pt;"/><col style="width: 260.2pt;"/>
</colgroup><thead>
<tr>
<th>
<p>Fixtures acceptable subject to specific requirements</p>
</th>
<th>
<p>Requirements</p>
</th>
</tr>
</thead><tbody>
<tr>
<td>
<picture class="role-small">
<img class="role-small web-winner" src="media/12_14-22a.png" data-rank="42" style="width: 133.5pt;"/>
</picture>
</td>
<td>
<p>Pole-mounted fixtures limited to 6,600 lumens. Fixture must not have more than two percent of lumens above 90 degrees</p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>
<picture class="role-small">
<img class="role-small web-winner" src="media/12_14-22b.png" data-rank="42" style="width: 159pt;"/>
</picture>
</td>
<td>
<p>Floodlights aimed above the horizontal to illuminate flags and building elements are acceptable only when the fixture is shielded or aimed such that people on the ground cannot see into the fixture or the lamp from normal viewing angles. See Figure 12.14-26 for information regarding other options for illuminating building elements</p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>
<picture class="role-small">
<img class="role-small web-winner" src="media/12_14-22c.png" data-rank="42" style="width: 161.3pt;"/>
</picture>
</td>
<td>
<p>Wall sconces with opaque fronts that are limited to 6,200 lumens. Uplighting is acceptable only when fixture is located beneath building overhangs, cantilevers, or canopies. Arrows indicate direction of light</p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>
<picture class="role-small">
<img class="role-small web-winner" src="media/12_14-22d.png" data-rank="42" style="width: 121.5pt;"/>
</picture>
</td>
<td>
<p>Horizontal lamp completely contained inside opaque portion of housing. Lamp limited to 6,200 lumens. Clear or translucent lens acceptable</p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>
<picture class="role-small">
<img class="role-small web-winner" src="media/12_14-22e.png" data-rank="42" style="width: 146.3pt;"/>
</picture>
</td>
<td>
<p>Wall mounted fixtures with translucent, white lens up to 12 inches in diameter and limited to 1,250 lumens</p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>
<picture class="role-small">
<img class="role-small web-winner" src="media/12_14-22f.png" data-rank="42" style="width: 146.3pt;"/>
</picture>
</td>
<td>
<p>Decorative wall sconce with translucent white lens. If using metal halide lamp, lamp must be concealed in upper or lower opaque portion of fixture. Limited to 3,300 lumens. If using fluorescent lamp, limited to one T8 or T5 standard output lamp mounted vertically within the fixture</p>
</td>
</tr>
</tbody></table>
</figure>
<figure class="type-Table">
<table class="role-medium table-ruled-outside-both table-ruled-inside-both"><caption><p class="header"><span class="name">Figure 12.14-23 Acceptable fixtures for individual single-family residential</span>&#xA0;</p>


</caption><colgroup><col style="width: 225.2pt;"/><col style="width: 225.2pt;"/>
</colgroup><thead>
<tr>
<th>
<p>Acceptable fixtures (may be similar)</p>
</th>
<th>
<p>Requirements</p>
</th>
</tr>
</thead><tbody>
<tr>
<td>
<picture class="role-small">
<img class="role-small web-winner" src="media/12_14-23a.png" data-rank="42" style="width: 156.8pt;"/>
</picture>
</td>
<td>
<p>Clear glass with a maximum of 345 lumens per lamp. Two lamps per fixture maximum</p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>
<picture class="role-small">
<img class="role-small web-winner" src="media/12_14-23b.png" data-rank="42" style="width: 200.3pt;"/>
</picture>
</td>
<td>
<p>Translucent lens up to 1,220 lumens per lamp maximum. One lamp per fixture maximum</p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>
<picture class="role-small">
<img class="role-small web-winner" src="media/12_14-23c.png" data-rank="42" style="width: 98.3pt;"/>
</picture>
</td>
<td>
<p>Post top with clear glass with maximum lamp lumens of 345 with a three lamp maximum</p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>
<picture class="role-small">
<img class="role-small web-winner" src="media/12_14-23d.png" data-rank="42" style="width: 189.8pt;"/>
</picture>
</td>
<td>
<p>Bare bulbs with motion sensor</p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>
<picture class="role-small">
<img class="role-small web-winner" src="media/12_14-23e.png" data-rank="42" style="width: 199.5pt;"/>
</picture>
</td>
<td>
<p>Light completely concealed within opaque housing. All light aimed in a downward direction</p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>
<picture class="role-small">
<img class="role-small web-winner" src="media/12_14-23f.png" data-rank="42" style="width: 184.5pt;"/>
</picture>
</td>
<td>
<p>Shielded lamps with motion sensors</p>
</td>
</tr>
</tbody></table>
</figure>
<figure class="type-Table">
<table class="role-medium table-ruled-outside-both table-ruled-inside-both"><caption><p class="header"><span class="name">Figure 12.14-24 Unacceptable fixtures</span>&#xA0;</p>


</caption><colgroup><col style="width: 225.2pt;"/><col style="width: 225.2pt;"/>
</colgroup><thead>
<tr>
<th>
<p>Unacceptable light fixtures</p>
</th>
<th>
<p>Comment</p>
</th>
</tr>
</thead><tbody>
<tr>
<td>
<picture class="role-small">
<img class="role-small web-winner" src="media/12_14-24a.png" data-rank="42" style="width: 100.5pt;"/>
</picture>
</td>
<td>
<p>Commonly called a yard or area light, these fixtures are not acceptable under any circumstances</p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>
<picture class="role-small">
<img class="role-small web-winner" src="media/12_14-24b.png" data-rank="42" style="width: 111.8pt;"/>
</picture>
</td>
<td>
<p>Bare bulbs with no shielding and no motion sensor</p>
</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>
<picture class="role-small">
<img class="role-small web-winner" src="media/12_14-24c.png" data-rank="42" style="width: 96pt;"/>
</picture>
</td>
<td>
<p>Flood light fixtures with no shielding and no motion sensors</p>
</td>
</tr>
</tbody></table>
</figure>
<figure class="type-Figure">
<figcaption><p class="header"><span class="name">Figure 12.14-25 Example of house-sided shielding</span></p>


</figcaption>
<picture class="role-medium">
<img class="role-medium web-winner" src="media/12_14-25.png" data-rank="62" style="width: 270pt;"/>
</picture>
</figure>
<figure class="type-Table">
<table class="role-medium table-ruled-outside-both table-ruled-inside-both"><caption><p class="header"><span class="name">Figure 12.14-26 Illustration demonstrating building uplight requirement</span></p>


</caption><colgroup><col style="width: 225.2pt;"/><col style="width: 225.2pt;"/>
</colgroup><thead>
<tr>
<th>
<p>Acceptable flood light use for architectural uplighting</p>
</th>
<th>
<p>Unacceptable flood light use for architectural uplighting</p>
</th>
</tr>
</thead><tbody>
<tr>
<td>
<picture class="role-small">
<img class="role-small web-winner" src="media/12_14-26a1.png" data-rank="42" style="width: 141pt;"/>
</picture>
</td>
<td>
<picture class="role-small">
<img class="role-small web-winner" src="media/12_14-26a2.png" data-rank="42" style="width: 171pt;"/>
</picture>
</td>
</tr>
</tbody></table>
</figure>
<figure class="type-Figure">
<figcaption><p class="header"><span class="name">Figure 12.14-27 Illustration demonstrating shielding requirement for pole-mounted fixtures located within two pole heights of adjacent properties</span></p>


</figcaption>
<picture class="role-medium">
<img class="role-medium web-winner" src="media/12_14-27.png" data-rank="62" style="width: 389.3pt;"/>
</picture>
</figure>
<figure class="type-Figure">
<figcaption><p class="header"><span class="name">Figure 12.14-28 Illustration demonstrating when pole-mounted fixtures are located two or more pole heights away from adjacent properties house-side shielding not required</span></p>


</figcaption>
<picture class="role-medium">
<img class="role-medium web-winner" src="media/12_14-28.png" data-rank="62" style="width: 449.3pt;"/>
</picture>
</figure>
</section>
</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(I)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(I)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(I)(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span></span>The following additional requirements are applicable to development applications for projects that include: buildings covering 10,000 square feet or more of gross floor area; parking lots containing 40 or more parking stalls; and residential developments of 20 or more dwelling units shall comply with subsection <a class="cite" href="12.14.240(G)(1)" title="12.14.240(G)(1)&#x2002;Automatic Control Requirements">(G)(1)</a> of this section except outdoor performance, sport and recreation facilities and playfield lighting, which must comply with subsection <a class="cite" href="12.14.240(H)" title="12.14.240(H)&#x2002;Illumination Standards">H</a> of this section, and individual single-family residential structures, which must comply with subsection <a class="cite" href="12.14.240(J)" title="12.14.240(J)&#x2002;Lighting of Exterior Performance, Sport and Recreation Facilities and Playfields">J</a> of this section.</p>



<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.240(I)(2)(a)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(I)(2)(a)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(I)(2)(a)"><p class="header"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Submittal Requirements.</span> </p>



<section class="subsec subsec4 chunking-none type-Subsec4" id="12.14.240(I)(2)(a)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(I)(2)(a)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(I)(2)(a)(1)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(1)&#x2002;</span></span>All of the submittal items required for general requirements plus provide site lighting calculations showing:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec5 chunking-none type-Subsec5" id="12.14.240(I)(2)(a)(1)(A)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(I)(2)(a)(1)(A)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(I)(2)(a)(1)(A)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(A)&#x2002;</span></span>Illumination points at maximum 10 foot intervals for all parking and on-site roadways measured at finish grade;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec5 chunking-none type-Subsec5" id="12.14.240(I)(2)(a)(1)(B)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(I)(2)(a)(1)(B)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(I)(2)(a)(1)(B)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(B)&#x2002;</span></span>Pathways, stairs, entrances, under canopies, and other pedestrian areas must be at five-foot maximum intervals. Include any areas intended for human use; and</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec5 chunking-none type-Subsec5" id="12.14.240(I)(2)(a)(1)(C)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(I)(2)(a)(1)(C)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(I)(2)(a)(1)(C)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(C)&#x2002;</span></span>The grid shall extend to the points surrounding the property line to the point where the light levels fall below 0.2 footcandles. The calculations shall include any existing or proposed ROW lighting that will contribute light onto the property.</p>



</section>
</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.240(I)(2)(b)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(I)(2)(b)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(I)(2)(b)"><p class="header"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Lighting Standards for Larger Developments.</span> </p>



<section class="subsec subsec4 chunking-none type-Subsec4" id="12.14.240(I)(2)(b)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(I)(2)(b)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(I)(2)(b)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(1)&#x2002;</span></span>Maximum fixture pole height shall be 20 feet above city-approved finish grade, including height of pole base;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec4 chunking-none type-Subsec4" id="12.14.240(I)(2)(b)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(I)(2)(b)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(I)(2)(b)(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(2)&#x2002;</span></span>Lighting power density shall not exceed the limits established within Table 12.14-1 for the applicable lighting zone;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec4 chunking-none type-Subsec4" id="12.14.240(I)(2)(b)(3)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(I)(2)(b)(3)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(I)(2)(b)(3)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(3)&#x2002;</span></span>The community development may allow increases from the established lighting zone to the next highest zone, if any, if safety and security is an issue. This may include instances where:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec5 chunking-none type-Subsec5" id="12.14.240(I)(2)(b)(3)(A)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(I)(2)(b)(3)(A)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(I)(2)(b)(3)(A)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(A)&#x2002;</span></span>The parking facility is used during all hours of the day and night; where the police chief has identified special security needs exist;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec5 chunking-none type-Subsec5" id="12.14.240(I)(2)(b)(3)(B)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(I)(2)(b)(3)(B)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(I)(2)(b)(3)(B)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(B)&#x2002;</span></span>High vandalism or crime rates have been documented by the Bothell police department; or</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec5 chunking-none type-Subsec5" id="12.14.240(I)(2)(b)(3)(C)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(I)(2)(b)(3)(C)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(I)(2)(b)(3)(C)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(C)&#x2002;</span></span>The community development director may consider specific site characteristics, level of vehicle and pedestrian conflict, special security needs, and history or likelihood of crimes in making a determination.</p>



</section>
</section>
</section>
</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.240(J)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(J)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(J)"><p class="heading"><span class="header"><span class="num">J.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Lighting of Exterior Performance, Sport and Recreation Facilities and Playfields.</span> </span>The following requirements shall be applied to all illuminated exterior performance, sport and recreation facilities and playfields:</p>




<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(J)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(J)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(J)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span></span>Lighting levels for outdoor performance areas, sport and recreation facilities, and playfields shall be Class III and Class IV as determined by the IESNA RP-6 (Recommended Practice for Sports and Recreational Area Lighting). The community development director may allow increases in designed lighting levels for venues hosting higher classes of sports play (collegiate/professional) and/or for stadiums with seating capacities in excess of 2,500 spectators.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(J)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(J)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(J)(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span></span>Where exterior playing fields or other exterior activity areas are to be illuminated, lighting fixtures shall be mounted, aimed and shielded such that light is directed only to the primary playing area and immediate surroundings associated with the playing area, such as spectator seating, concessions, ingress/egress areas, pedestrian aisles, maintenance facilities, and general pedestrian use areas. Direct illumination of adjacent nonplaying and immediate surroundings associated with the playing areas is prohibited.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(J)(3)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(J)(3)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(J)(3)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">3.&#x2002;</span></span>Floodlight shielding shall consist of one of the following:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.240(J)(3)(a)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(J)(3)(a)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(J)(3)(a)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span></span>Full cutoff shoebox style floodlights aimed a maximum of five degrees above the horizontal mounting height of the luminaire. (See Figure 12.14-25). This is the only light fixture acceptable for tennis or similar hardcourts.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.240(J)(3)(b)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(J)(3)(b)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(J)(3)(b)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span></span>Spun parabolic floodlights with external shielding consisting of a visor attached to the front of the floodlight. The visor shall extend beyond the upper hemisphere of the fixture a minimum of 12 inches and wrap a minimum of 170 degrees around the upper hemisphere of the floodlight. All surfaces of the fixture shall be nonreflecting and the external visor shall have a dull powder coat factory finish.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(J)(4)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(J)(4)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(J)(4)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">4.&#x2002;</span></span>Areas used for ingress/egress, pedestrian aisles, and general pedestrian use not associated with the playing or activity area shall be illuminated with separate lighting fixtures and shall provide an average horizontal illumination level at grade of no more than 1.0 footcandle average.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(J)(5)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(J)(5)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(J)(5)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">5.&#x2002;</span></span>In facilities with a seating capacity of 2,500 or more spectators, circulation areas around the concessions and restroom areas shall be no more than 10.0 footcandles average at three feet above grade, and shall be no more than 5.0 footcandles average at three feet above grade for facilities with a seating capacity of less than 2,500 spectators. The uniformity ratio (average horizontal illumination to minimum illumination) shall not exceed 4:1 within pedestrian aisles and walkways.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(J)(6)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(J)(6)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(J)(6)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">6.&#x2002;</span></span>Light trespass levels shall be a maximum of one and one-half footcandles when measured on either a horizontal surface (three feet above grade) or a vertical surface (five feet above grade) at all residential property lines.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(J)(7)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(J)(7)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(J)(7)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">7.&#x2002;</span></span>Applicants shall submit lighting plans for all outdoor sports courts and playing fields for review and approval. The following plans and information shall be included:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.240(J)(7)(a)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(J)(7)(a)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(J)(7)(a)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span></span>Pole locations;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.240(J)(7)(b)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(J)(7)(b)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(J)(7)(b)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span></span>Pole heights (poles used for exterior performance, sport and recreation facilities and playfields may exceed the height maximum established within the lighting zone requirements);</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.240(J)(7)(c)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(J)(7)(c)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(J)(7)(c)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">c.&#x2002;</span></span>Pole types;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.240(J)(7)(d)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(J)(7)(d)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(J)(7)(d)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">d.&#x2002;</span></span>Luminaire quantities;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.240(J)(7)(e)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(J)(7)(e)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(J)(7)(e)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">e.&#x2002;</span></span>Luminaire types;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.240(J)(7)(f)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(J)(7)(f)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(J)(7)(f)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">f.&#x2002;</span></span>Luminaire mounting heights;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.240(J)(7)(g)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(J)(7)(g)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(J)(7)(g)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">g.&#x2002;</span></span>Aiming points;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.240(J)(7)(h)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(J)(7)(h)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(J)(7)(h)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">h.&#x2002;</span></span>Fieldcourt boundaries; and</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.240(J)(7)(i)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(J)(7)(i)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(J)(7)(i)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">i.&#x2002;</span></span>Site property lines.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.240(J)(7)(j)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(J)(7)(j)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(J)(7)(j)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">j.&#x2002;</span></span>Computer generated vertical and horizontal illumination grids for the field or court shall be provided with the plans showing:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec4 chunking-none type-Subsec4" id="12.14.240(J)(7)(j)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(J)(7)(j)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(J)(7)(j)(1)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(1)&#x2002;</span></span>Horizontal footcandle levels calculated on a 15-foot grid three feet above the playing surface. The horizontal grid shall extend to the points surrounding the lighted field/court to the point where the light levels fall below 0.2 footcandles including lands beyond the subject property when necessary.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec4 chunking-none type-Subsec4" id="12.14.240(J)(7)(j)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(J)(7)(j)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(J)(7)(j)(2)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">(2)&#x2002;</span></span>Vertical footcandle illumination levels at 15-foot increments, five feet high facing into the field at all property lines.</p>



</section>
</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(J)(8)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(J)(8)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(J)(8)"><p><span class="header"><span class="num">8.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Measurement and Verification.</span> </span>Prior to issuance of occupancy permit, the applicant shall provide the city with confirmation of compliance with regulations. Applicant shall measure lighting along residential property lines at a minimum of 30 feet on center. Documentation shall be submitted in writing and signed by the engineer of record.</p>




<figure class="type-Figure">
<figcaption><p class="header"><span class="name">Figure 12.14-29 Illustration of spun parabolic floodlight with shields</span></p>


</figcaption>
<picture class="role-medium">
<img class="role-medium web-winner" src="media/12_14-29.png" data-rank="62" style="width: 234pt;"/>
</picture>
</figure>
<figure class="type-Figure">
<figcaption><p class="header"><span class="name">Figure 12.14-30 Example of full-cut-off shoe box floodlight</span></p>


</figcaption>
<picture class="role-small">
<img class="role-small web-winner" src="media/12_14-30.png" data-rank="42" style="width: 162pt;"/>
</picture>
</figure>
<figure class="type-Figure">
<figcaption><p class="header"><span class="name">Figure 12.14-31 Illustration of five degree maximum tilt</span></p>


</figcaption>
<picture class="role-medium">
<img class="role-medium web-winner" src="media/12_14-31.png" data-rank="62" style="width: 392.3pt;"/>
</picture>
</figure>
</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec1 chunking-none type-Subsec1" id="12.14.240(K)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(K)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(K)"><p class="heading"><span class="header"><span class="num">K.&#x2002;</span><span class="name">Requirements for Single-Family Residential Lighting.</span> </span>Individual single-family residential structures shall be subject to the following requirements:</p>




<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(K)(1)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(K)(1)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(K)(1)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">1.&#x2002;</span></span>Decorative building mounted lighting (porch lights and sconces) are acceptable with the following limitations:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.240(K)(1)(a)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(K)(1)(a)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(K)(1)(a)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span></span>Fixtures with clear glass lenses are limited to up to two 345 lumen lamps (equivalent to 25 watt incandescent).</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.240(K)(1)(b)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(K)(1)(b)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(K)(1)(b)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span></span>Fixtures with translucent lenses are limited to a total of 1,220 lumens (equivalent to 75 watt incandescent).</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.240(K)(1)(c)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(K)(1)(c)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(K)(1)(c)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">c.&#x2002;</span></span>Fully shielded fixtures (see example in Figure 12.14-21) can be up to 2,700 lumens (equivalent to 150 watt incandescent).</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.240(K)(1)(d)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(K)(1)(d)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(K)(1)(d)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">d.&#x2002;</span></span>Low voltage fixtures up to 50 watts and/or 1,500 lumens.</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.240(K)(1)(e)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(K)(1)(e)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(K)(1)(e)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">e.&#x2002;</span></span>Fixtures shall comply with Figures 12.14-23 and 12.14-24 or be consistent with the above standards.</p>



</section>
</section>
<section class="subsec subsec2 chunking-none type-Subsec2" id="12.14.240(K)(2)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(K)(2)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(K)(2)"><p class="heading"><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">2.&#x2002;</span></span>Pole-mounted light fixtures are acceptable with the following limitations:</p>



<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.240(K)(2)(a)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(K)(2)(a)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(K)(2)(a)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">a.&#x2002;</span></span>Fixtures&#x2019; lumen output shall be consistent with subsection <a class="cite" href="12.14.240(K)(1)" title="12.14.240(K)(1)">(K)(1)</a> of this section;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.240(K)(2)(b)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(K)(2)(b)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(K)(2)(b)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">b.&#x2002;</span></span>Fixtures shall be mounted a maximum of 12 feet above finish grade;</p>



</section>
<section class="subsec subsec3 chunking-none type-Subsec3" id="12.14.240(K)(2)(c)" data-cite="BMC 12.14.240(K)(2)(c)" data-short-cite="12.14.240(K)(2)(c)"><p><span class="header num-only"><span class="num">c.&#x2002;</span></span>Fixtures used for hardscape sports courts, such as tennis, basketball, pickleball and other courts shall be consistent with subsection <a class="cite" href="12.14.240(J)" title="12.14.240(J)&#x2002;Lighting of Exterior Performance, Sport and Recreation Facilities and Playfields">J</a> of this section. <span class="note history history--linked" data-action="click-&gt;levelnodes#openHistory">(Ord. 2445 &#xA7;&#xA0;6 (Exh. F), 2024; Ord. 2373 &#xA7;&#xA0;9, 2022; Ord. 2055 &#xA7;&#xA0;2 (Exh. B), 2011).</span></p>



</section>
</section>
</section>





</article>
